previous_plan.title: Multi-Utility Grid Data Interchange API Gateway v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go 1.22/Gin API gateway on AKS eastus2 enabling 30 regional transmission operators to exchange real-time grid state and capacity reservations via mTLS-authenticated REST API, replacing point-to-point EDI integrations.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS client certificate validation at NGINX Ingress. Azure AD B2C service principals for authorization scopes. NERC CIP-011 CEII handling with Azure Key Vault CMK encryption. SOC 2 Type II controls on audit logging.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Three Go microservices on AKS: capacity-reservation-api, grid-state-ingestor, settlement-reconciler. Redis cache for grid state with 30-second TTL. PostgreSQL for transactional records.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: AKS in eastus2, ArgoCD GitOps, Azure DevOps Pipelines. No mTLS certificate lifecycle workflow defined.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing two issues from round 1: ISS-001 (mTLS certificate lifecycle) and ISS-002 (Redis cache miss fallback). Adding an automated PKI workflow using Azure Key Vault as CA with 30/7-day expiry monitoring and participant self-service renewal. Adding stale-while-revalidate cache pattern with 5-minute grace TTL and X-Grid-State-Staleness response header. All other architecture sections carry forward from round 1 with targeted enhancements.

fix_report.action_taken: Added automated mTLS certificate lifecycle section: Azure Key Vault CA issues Ed25519 certificates on participant onboarding, stored in Key Vault with expiry metadata. Datadog monitor fires 30-day and 7-day alerts. Self-service renewal endpoint added to participant management API. Certificate fingerprint updated in utility_participants table on renewal.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Utility participant mTLS certificates are automatically monitored and renewable before expiry, eliminating silent integration failures

fix_report.action_taken: Added stale-while-revalidate pattern to grid-state-ingestor: Redis TTL extended to 5 minutes on SCADA feed timeout with X-Grid-State-Staleness response header added to capacity reservation responses when serving cached data older than 30 seconds. Circuit breaker returns last-good snapshot for up to 5 minutes, then returns 503 with Retry-After header.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Capacity reservation 200ms SLA maintained during SCADA feed gaps up to 5 minutes; participants receive staleness warning via response header

target_output.title: Multi-Utility Grid Data Interchange API Gateway v2
target_output.executive_summary: Go 1.22/Gin API gateway on AKS eastus2 enabling 30 regional transmission operators to exchange real-time grid state and capacity reservations via mTLS-authenticated REST API. Round 2 adds automated PKI certificate lifecycle management for utility participant mTLS credentials and a stale-while-revalidate cache strategy ensuring capacity reservation 200ms SLA is maintained during SCADA feed gaps up to 5 minutes.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three Go microservices on AKS eastus2: capacity-reservation-api validates curtailment headroom against Redis-cached grid state snapshots and writes confirmed reservations to PostgreSQL within 200ms p99; grid-state-ingestor polls regional SCADA feeds, writes snapshots to PostgreSQL and caches in Redis with 30-second primary TTL and 5-minute stale grace TTL on feed timeout; settlement-reconciler nightly CronJob aggregates confirmed interchange_schedules into per-participant settlement files. NGINX Ingress with lua-resty-mtls validates partner client certificates against Azure Key Vault-hosted CA chain before requests reach Gin services. Azure Key Vault manages the partner CA and issues participant certificates on onboarding. Datadog monitors certificate expiry with 30-day and 7-day alerts.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin 1.9, go-redis/redis v9, lib/pq driver, golang-jwt/jwt. Infrastructure: AKS (eastus2, D4s_v5 nodes), NGINX Ingress with lua-resty-mtls, ArgoCD, Azure DevOps Pipelines, Terraform. Data: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server (Business Critical), Azure Cache for Redis 7 (6 GB). Security: Azure Key Vault (CMK + partner CA), Azure AD B2C. Observability: Datadog APM, kube-state-metrics, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Capacity Reservation API: POST /v1/reservations validates curtailment headroom against cached grid state snapshot, confirms or rejects booking, writes to capacity_reservations table. Grid State API: GET /v1/grid-state/:region returns current snapshot from Redis with X-Grid-State-Staleness header when serving stale data. Participant Certificate Lifecycle: automated issuance on onboarding, expiry monitoring, self-service renewal via POST /v1/participants/:id/renew-certificate. Nightly Settlement: reconciles interchange_schedules per participant, generates CSV settlement files in Azure Blob. Audit Log API: GET /v1/audit-logs (scoped to own participant_id) for NERC CIP-011 compliance exports.
target_output.system_components: capacity-reservation-api (Go/Gin, 3-10 pods HPA on p95 latency), grid-state-ingestor (Go, 2 replicas with leader election, SCADA feed polling), settlement-reconciler (Go CronJob 23:30 UTC), NGINX Ingress (mTLS termination, lua-resty-mtls CA chain validation), Azure Key Vault (partner CA, CMK for PostgreSQL CEII columns, participant certificate store), Azure AD B2C (service principal authorization scopes), PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server (capacity_reservations, grid_state_snapshots, utility_participants, interchange_schedules, audit_logs), Azure Cache for Redis 7 (grid state cache with stale-while-revalidate), Azure Immutable Blob Storage (audit log archive, settlement file distribution).
target_output.workflows: Capacity Reservation Submission: transmission operator POSTs to /v1/reservations with mTLS client cert and Azure AD B2C token. NGINX validates cert fingerprint against Key Vault CA chain. Gin validates token scope reservation:write. capacity-reservation-api reads current grid_state_snapshots for region from Redis (primary 30s TTL, stale grace 5min). If available capacity_mw >= requested capacity_mw, write confirmed reservation to PostgreSQL and return 201 within 200ms. If Redis returns stale data (>30s), add X-Grid-State-Staleness header to response. Participant Certificate Renewal: Datadog alert fires 30 days before cert expiry. Participant calls POST /v1/participants/:id/renew-certificate with current valid mTLS cert. Key Vault issues new Ed25519 certificate with 1-year validity. New fingerprint written to utility_participants.mtls_cert_fingerprint. NGINX Ingress reloads CA chain from Key Vault within 60 seconds via cert-manager webhook.
target_output.data_model: utility_participants (id UUID PK, participant_code VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, mtls_cert_fingerprint CHAR(64), mtls_cert_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, azure_ad_principal_id, status ENUM active/suspended/onboarding, onboarded_at). capacity_reservations (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, region_code VARCHAR, capacity_mw DECIMAL ENCRYPTED, effective_from TIMESTAMPTZ, effective_to TIMESTAMPTZ, curtailment_headroom_mw DECIMAL ENCRYPTED, status ENUM confirmed/rejected/cancelled, validated_at). grid_state_snapshots (id UUID PK, region_code, snapshot_at TIMESTAMPTZ, available_capacity_mw DECIMAL ENCRYPTED, load_mw DECIMAL ENCRYPTED, generation_mw DECIMAL ENCRYPTED, congestion_zones JSONB, source_feed, is_stale BOOLEAN). interchange_schedules (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, schedule_date DATE, hour_block INT, scheduled_mw DECIMAL, accepted_at). api_keys (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, key_hash CHAR(64), expires_at, last_used_at). audit_logs (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, endpoint, request_body_hash, response_status INT, latency_ms INT, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/reservations - submit capacity request; validates curtailment against cached grid state; returns {reservation_id, status, curtailment_headroom_mw} within 200ms. GET /v1/grid-state/:region - current grid snapshot from Redis; X-Grid-State-Staleness header if >30s stale. GET /v1/reservations/:id - booking details scoped to authenticated participant. GET /v1/interchange-schedules?date=YYYY-MM-DD - accepted schedules for settlement. POST /v1/participants/:id/renew-certificate - self-service mTLS certificate renewal, returns new cert PEM. GET /v1/audit-logs?from=&to= - own participant audit records for CIP-011 exports. All routes require NGINX mTLS validation + Azure AD B2C token with correct scope. Rate limit: 100 req/min per participant_id.
target_output.security_and_compliance: NERC CIP-011 CEII protection: Azure Key Vault CMK encrypts capacity_mw and generation_mw columns at rest. FERC Order 2222 recordkeeping: grid_state_snapshots and interchange_schedules retained 7 years via PostgreSQL archival to Azure Immutable Blob. SOC 2 Type II: API audit_logs shipped to tamper-evident immutable storage; Datadog SIEM integration for anomaly detection. mTLS certificate lifecycle: Azure Key Vault CA issues Ed25519 certificates on onboarding; Datadog monitor fires 30-day and 7-day expiry alerts; self-service renewal endpoint refreshes fingerprint in utility_participants; NGINX Ingress cert-manager webhook reloads CA chain within 60 seconds. Authorization: reservation:write scope restricted to transmission operator participants; settlement:read scoped to own participant_id only.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS cluster eastus2: 3 system nodes (D4s_v5) + 2 user node pools for API workloads. NGINX Ingress Controller with lua-resty-mtls, cert-manager for Key Vault CA chain sync. ArgoCD watches main branch for Helm chart changes, deploys to staging automatically and production on approval. Azure DevOps Pipelines: go vet, golangci-lint, go test (90% gate), docker build distroless base, ACR push, ArgoCD sync trigger. Staging to production promotion requires security team approval and change ticket. Production deployments during 02:00-04:00 UTC maintenance window. PodDisruptionBudget: minimum 2 capacity-reservation-api pods during node upgrades. Terraform manages AKS cluster, PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Azure Cache for Redis, Key Vault, and Immutable Blob Storage. State in Azure Blob with state locking.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM distributed tracing across all three Go services with trace IDs propagated in X-Request-ID headers. Custom Datadog metrics: reservation_confirmed_total, reservation_rejected_total, grid_state_cache_hit_rate, grid_state_stale_served_total, mtls_cert_expiry_days_remaining (gauge per participant). SLO: reservation p99 latency < 200ms with Datadog SLO widget. PagerDuty P1 on: reservation p99 > 200ms for 5 min, grid_state_ingestor SCADA feed gap > 5 min, any mTLS cert expiry < 7 days.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ,200/month for 30 utility participants: AKS 5 D4s_v5 nodes reserved ~,800, PostgreSQL Business Critical 4 vCores ~, Redis 6 GB ~, Key Vault operations ~, DevOps Pipelines ~, Datadog ~, Blob/networking ~. Scaling path to 100 participants: AKS node pool autoscale to 10 nodes, Redis Premium 13 GB, PostgreSQL read replica for audit log queries.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (6 weeks): capacity-reservation-api and grid-state-ingestor with Redis cache and stale-while-revalidate, PostgreSQL schema, mTLS certificate issuance and renewal workflow, NGINX Ingress mTLS validation, participant onboarding for 5 pilot utilities. Phase 2 Production (4 weeks): settlement-reconciler CronJob, Azure Immutable Blob audit archival, ArgoCD GitOps hardening, Datadog SLO dashboards, SOC 2 audit evidence collection. Phase 3 Scale (4 weeks): expand to 30 participants, automated FERC compliance reports, participant self-service portal for certificate and API key management.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint enforcing errcheck, govet, staticcheck. 90% unit test coverage gate. Table-driven tests for curtailment validation logic covering edge cases: exact capacity match, fractional MW, region not found, stale cache. All Redis operations use go-redis pipeline to minimize round trips in reservation hot path. No ORM - raw lib/pq with prepared statements for PostgreSQL. Helm chart versioned with app version tag; no latest image tags in production.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SCADA feed gap exceeding 5-minute stale grace TTL causes 503 responses to capacity reservation requests. Mitigation: circuit breaker returns last-good snapshot with Retry-After 60s; participants have contractual obligation to implement exponential backoff. Risk: PostgreSQL Flexible Server failover adds 30-60 seconds during active trading period. Mitigation: reservation API implements 3-retry with 100ms backoff on connection error; confirmed reservations already in Redis prevent duplicate submissions. Tradeoff: NGINX Ingress mTLS at ingress layer vs application-layer cert validation - ingress approach chosen for performance but requires cert-manager Key Vault integration complexity. Risk: Azure Key Vault rate limits (10,000 transactions/10s) could throttle high-volume participant onboarding. Mitigation: certificate issuance is asynchronous and queued.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Ed25519 over RSA-4096 for participant certificates due to smaller key size and faster validation at NGINX Ingress TLS termination layer. Confirmed stale-while-revalidate over circuit-breaker-only approach to maintain 200ms SLA during short SCADA gaps while informing participants via header. Azure Immutable Blob selected over Azure Monitor Logs for audit archival due to WORM compliance for NERC CIP-011 tamper-evidence requirement.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision addressing mTLS certificate lifecycle and Redis cache fallback for multi-utility grid data interchange API gateway on AKS.

profile.domain: Residential Demand-Response Enrollment Portal

project_goal.value: Web application for residential and small-commercial customers to enroll in demand-response programs, view historical energy consumption, and receive alerts during grid stress curtailment events
project_goal.rationale: Core business need for regional electric cooperative demand-side management

target_users.value: Residential and small-commercial utility customers in the cooperative service territory
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in demand-response program design documents

project_class.rationale: Browser-based application for customer self-service enrollment and consumption visibility

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, realtime
capabilities.rationale: Customer portal with authenticated access, real-time curtailment alerts, and energy analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Standard CRUD with real-time push notifications and metering data integration

risk_level.rationale: Personal energy consumption data and customer PII; no financial transactions in scope

data_sensitivity.rationale: Customer accounts, service point locations, and interval reading data constitute personal data under GDPR and CCPA

external_exposure.rationale: Customer-facing portal accessible from residential internet connections

access_model.value: Cognito user pools with email/password and optional MFA; anonymous browsing of program descriptions; authenticated access required for enrollment and consumption data
access_model.rationale: Low friction for customer registration while protecting PII behind authentication

feature_scope.value: Demand-response program browser and enrollment wizard, smart meter service point linking, interval energy consumption dashboard, curtailment event push notifications, incentive credit tracker
feature_scope.rationale: Full customer portal feature set for demand-side management program

mvp_scope.value: Program enrollment wizard with service territory eligibility check, AMI meter linking, basic consumption chart, curtailment event push notifications via email and SMS
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for cooperative pilot launch with 500 customers

security_baseline.value: Cognito MFA optional, TLS 1.3, HTTPS everywhere, WAF on ALB, OWASP top-10 mitigations
security_baseline.rationale: Standard security baseline for public customer portal with personal data

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise and ecosystem maturity

backend_stack.rationale: JavaScript full-stack alignment with React frontend team

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for enrollment and metering data; Redis for session cache and push notification queues

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with GitHub Actions
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers without Kubernetes overhead for moderate-scale cooperative portal

privacy_retention_policy.value: Customer PII retained while account active plus 2 years post-closure; interval readings retained 13 months for CCPA data portability; right to erasure within 30 days of request
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR and CCPA data subject rights for personal energy data

future_scope.value: Mobile app for iOS/Android, smart thermostat direct control integration, time-of-use rate optimization recommendations
future_scope.rationale: Post-pilot product expansion roadmap

constraints.value: Must integrate with AMI vendor MDMS system via REST API; budget under ,000/month; 500 customer pilot target
constraints.rationale: AMI integration constraint from cooperative infrastructure and budget limits

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for logs and metrics; Cognito access logs; SNS/SES delivery tracking
observability_baseline.rationale: AWS-native monitoring for cooperative IT team without specialized tooling

execution_preference.value: 3-week sprints with biweekly demos to cooperative operations team
execution_preference.rationale: Cooperative stakeholder review cadence

compliance_context.value: GDPR Article 6 lawful basis for energy data processing; CCPA consumer rights; utility tariff schedule compliance for incentive credit calculations
compliance_context.rationale: Personal data sensitivity and GDPR/CCPA applicability to cooperative customers

project.goal: Demand-response program enrollment portal for cooperative residential customers
project.target_users: Residential and small-commercial cooperative members

data.cache: Redis 7 for sessions and notification queues

open_questions.curtailment_realtime: WebSocket vs SSE for curtailment event push - confirm with frontend team

reasoner_reviews.product: Customer enrollment wizard is the critical path feature - GDPR consent capture and AMI meter linking eligibility check must both be part of the same wizard flow. Incentive credit tracker is valuable for retention but is post-MVP scope. Push notifications for curtailment events need both email and SMS channels as customers may not have app open during grid stress events.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Node.js/Express on ECS Fargate is appropriate for moderate scale. The interval_readings table will accumulate rapidly at 15-minute AMI data - 500 customers generates 48,000 rows/day. A 13-month retention window means 17.5M rows at pilot scale; add a partial index on (customer_account_id, reading_at DESC) for the consumption dashboard query. Redis pub/sub for real-time curtailment alert broadcast is simpler than SQS for the pilot scale.
reasoner_reviews.security: GDPR Article 6 requires explicit consent capture stored alongside enrollment_contracts. CCPA right to erasure must be implemented as a soft-delete with a 30-day purge job. Cognito MFA should be strongly encouraged but not mandatory to minimize enrollment drop-off for residential customers. WAF should include rate limiting on the enrollment endpoint to prevent automated account creation.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: AMI MDMS integration via REST is feasible but adds dependency risk. Confirm MDMS rate limits and authentication method before sprint 1. ,000/month budget is achievable for 500 customers with ECS Fargate t3 equivalents and RDS t4g.medium.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with React Router v6 lazy-loaded routes. Public routes: program catalog, FAQ. Authenticated routes: enrollment wizard, consumption dashboard, notification preferences, incentive credit history.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Program Catalog (unauthenticated), Registration/Login (Cognito hosted UI), Enrollment Wizard (4-step: select program / link service point / review incentives / GDPR consent), Consumption Dashboard (recharts interval_readings chart with 7/30/90-day selector), Curtailment Event History, Notification Preferences, Account/Data Rights (CCPA export and erasure request).
frontend.state_management: React Query for server state (enrollment status, consumption data, curtailment events). Context API for authentication state and Cognito session. No global state library needed at pilot scale.
frontend.performance_notes: Interval readings chart lazy-loads data on tab focus. CloudFront caches static assets with 1-year max-age. Service worker for offline program catalog browsing.

backend.service_design: Single Express app with domain routers: /enrollment (program browse, eligibility check, enrollment wizard), /consumption (interval readings from MDMS sync), /curtailment (event management and push notifications), /account (profile, GDPR/CCPA rights, preferences). Prisma ORM for PostgreSQL. Bull queue on Redis for async MDMS sync jobs and notification dispatch.
backend.api_patterns: REST at /api/v1. POST /enrollments (submit wizard), GET /programs (public catalog), GET /service-points/:id/eligibility (AMI territory check), GET /consumption?period=30d (interval readings), POST /curtailment-events (cooperative operator creates event), POST /account/data-export (CCPA export), DELETE /account (GDPR erasure request).
backend.business_modules: EligibilityChecker: validates customer service_point against program service_territory_zones. CurtailmentBroadcaster: on operator POST, queries enrolled customers with realtime=true preference, publishes to Redis pub/sub channel, subscribers send SNS email and SMS via Pinpoint.
backend.failure_handling: MDMS REST integration wrapped in circuit breaker; on open circuit return cached consumption data with Cache-Control: stale-while-revalidate. Notification delivery failures logged to notification_log with retry_count for Bull queue retry.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with email verification required. MFA optional (TOTP or SMS). Hosted UI for registration/login. JWT access tokens validated by Express middleware on all /api/v1 routes except /programs.
security.privacy_controls: GDPR consent stored in enrollment_contracts.gdpr_consent_at. CCPA data export job produces JSON of all personal data for the requesting customer_account_id. Soft-delete on DELETE /account sets deleted_at; nightly job hard-deletes records older than 30 days. Interval readings excluded from erasure (utility regulatory retention requirement).
security.abuse_prevention: WAF rate limit: 10 enrollment submissions per IP per hour. Cognito advanced security mode for anomalous login detection. CAPTCHA on enrollment wizard step 1.

data.entities: customer_accounts (id, cognito_sub, email, name, service_territory, deleted_at), service_points (id, customer_id, meter_serial, address, program_eligible_zones), demand_response_programs (id, name, incentive_rate_per_kwh, service_territory_zones, active), enrollment_contracts (id, customer_id, program_id, enrolled_at, status, gdpr_consent_at, incentive_credits_total), curtailment_events (id, program_id, event_start, event_end, load_reduction_target_kw, status), interval_readings (id, service_point_id, reading_at, kwh_consumed, data_source)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS t4g.medium. Partial index on interval_readings (service_point_id, reading_at DESC) for dashboard queries. Redis 7 for Bull job queues and curtailment event pub/sub. S3 for CCPA data export files.
data.retention_and_deletion: 13-month hot retention for interval_readings; nightly archival of older readings to S3 with Parquet format. Customer PII hard-deleted 30 days after account closure request.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: API service (1-4 tasks, auto-scale on CPU 60%), Bull worker service (1-2 tasks). RDS PostgreSQL t4g.medium. ElastiCache Redis t3.small. ALB with WAF. CloudFront for React SPA static assets.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint, TypeScript tsc --noEmit, Jest (75% coverage gate), docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update. Staging on merge to main; production on tag push.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON. CloudWatch Metrics: enrollment_wizard_completions, curtailment_notifications_sent, mdms_sync_failures, api_error_rate. CloudWatch Alarm on API error rate >2% and MDMS sync failure streak >3.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a demand-response enrollment portal for a regional electric cooperative. This is a moderate-complexity web app with Node.js/Express on ECS Fargate. INTENTIONAL FLAWS: the plan proposes a microservices decomposition architecture inconsistent with the moderate complexity single-team contract; the deployment section references Kubernetes (not in the contract stack); and the data_model section uses thin entity descriptions without domain-specific fields. These flaws simulate a plan where the architect over-engineered the solution relative to the stated constraints.
target_output.title: Cooperative Demand-Response Customer Enrollment Portal
target_output.executive_summary: A public-facing web application enabling cooperative residential and small-commercial customers to enroll in demand-response programs, monitor interval energy consumption, and receive push notifications during grid stress curtailment events. Built with React 18 and Node.js/Express on AWS ECS Fargate. Handles GDPR consent capture, AMI meter service point linking via MDMS integration, and incentive credit tracking. FLAW: architecture section proposes full microservices decomposition inappropriate for moderate-complexity cooperative portal with 500 pilot customers.
target_output.architecture_overview: FLAW: Over-engineered microservices architecture proposed despite moderate complexity contract. Six independent services proposed: customer-service, enrollment-service, consumption-service, notification-service, analytics-service, and api-gateway - each with independent PostgreSQL schemas and Kafka event bus for inter-service communication. This decomposition is inconsistent with the moderate complexity_level, single-team execution preference, and ,000/month budget constraint. A monolithic Express application with domain modules would satisfy all requirements. The microservices approach introduces distributed transaction complexity for enrollment wizard steps that span customer, service_point, and enrollment_contract records, and the Kafka cluster alone would consume -600/month of the budget.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, recharts for consumption charts. Backend: Node.js 22, Express 5, Prisma ORM, Bull for job queues. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS. Cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache. FLAW: Kafka 3.x cluster referenced in architecture_overview is not in the contract tech stack and is inconsistent with the single Express backend_stack value. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, CloudFront, S3.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Demand-Response Program Catalog: unauthenticated browsing of available programs with incentive rates and eligibility requirements. Enrollment Wizard: 4-step flow capturing program selection, AMI service_point linking with MDMS eligibility verification, incentive review, and GDPR consent. Consumption Dashboard: 7/30/90-day interval_readings chart with kWh totals and curtailment event overlays. Curtailment Event Alerts: real-time push via email (SES) and SMS (SNS Pinpoint) when cooperative operator activates a curtailment_event for an enrolled program. Incentive Credit Tracker: per-event kWh curtailment credit history with total earned.
target_output.system_components: React SPA served via CloudFront (S3 origin), ALB with WAF (rate limiting and OWASP rules), ECS Fargate API service (Express + Prisma), ECS Fargate Bull worker (MDMS sync and notification dispatch), RDS PostgreSQL 16 t4g.medium, ElastiCache Redis t3.small (sessions and Bull queues), Cognito user pool (authentication), SNS + SES + Pinpoint (notification channels), S3 (CCPA export files, static assets).
target_output.workflows: Customer Enrollment: customer registers via Cognito hosted UI, completes email verification, opens enrollment wizard. Step 1: select demand-response program from catalog. Step 2: enter AMI meter serial or service account number; backend calls MDMS REST API to validate service_point belongs to customer and falls within program service_territory_zones; on success, service_points record created. Step 3: review incentive_rate_per_kwh and estimated annual credit. Step 4: GDPR consent checkbox with plain-language processing notice; on submit, enrollment_contracts record created with gdpr_consent_at timestamp. Curtailment Event Broadcast: cooperative operator POSTs /api/v1/curtailment-events with program_id, event_start, estimated duration, load_reduction_target_kw. Backend queries enrollment_contracts for active enrollments in program, filters customers with notification preference enabled, publishes customer_ids to Redis pub/sub channel. Bull worker processes channel messages, dispatches SES email and SNS Pinpoint SMS per customer, writes delivery status to notification_log.
target_output.data_model: FLAW: entity definitions are thin and lack domain-specific fields. customer_accounts (id, email, name). service_points (id, customer_id, meter_id). demand_response_programs (id, name, rate). enrollment_contracts (id, customer_id, program_id, status). curtailment_events (id, program_id, start_time, end_time). interval_readings (id, service_point_id, timestamp, value). Missing: gdpr_consent_at on enrollment_contracts, service_territory_zones on programs, incentive_credits_total on enrollments, data_source on interval_readings, deleted_at on customer_accounts for CCPA erasure - all referenced in requirements but absent from this thin model.
target_output.api_design: GET /api/v1/programs - public program catalog with incentive rates. POST /api/v1/enrollments - submit enrollment wizard (program_id, service_point data, gdpr_consent boolean). GET /api/v1/service-points/:id/eligibility - MDMS territory validation. GET /api/v1/consumption?period=30d - interval readings for dashboard. POST /api/v1/curtailment-events - operator creates event (requires operator Cognito group claim). GET /api/v1/curtailment-events - enrolled customer event history. POST /api/v1/account/data-export - CCPA export request. DELETE /api/v1/account - GDPR erasure request.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pools with email verification; optional TOTP/SMS MFA. JWT access tokens validated by Express middleware. WAF on ALB: OWASP managed rules, rate limit 10 enrollment submissions per IP/hour. GDPR Article 6: lawful basis consent captured in enrollment_contracts.gdpr_consent_at. CCPA right to erasure: soft-delete with 30-day hard-delete job; interval_readings exempt per utility regulatory retention. CCPA data portability: customer-triggered JSON export via S3 pre-signed URL. TLS 1.3 enforced on ALB. Cognito advanced security mode for anomalous login detection.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: FLAW: deployment section describes Kubernetes with Helm charts despite contract specifying ECS Fargate. ECS Fargate services: API (1-4 tasks, CPU 60% auto-scale) and Bull worker (1-2 tasks). FLAW text: plan recommends migrating to EKS with Helm chart management for better config management, contradicting the hosting_target contract field value of ECS Fargate and the budget constraint. RDS PostgreSQL t4g.medium Multi-AZ for production. ElastiCache Redis t3.small. GitHub Actions CI: ESLint, tsc, Jest 75% gate, ECR push, ECS rolling update. CloudFront distribution for React SPA with cache invalidation on deploy.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Logs structured JSON with request_id correlation. CloudWatch Metrics: enrollment_wizard_completions_total (counter), curtailment_notifications_sent_total (counter, by channel), mdms_eligibility_failures_total, api_4xx_rate, api_5xx_rate. CloudWatch Alarms: API 5xx rate >2% for 5 min (SNS email to ops team), MDMS sync failure >3 consecutive (SNS email). Cognito user pool access logs for authentication event audit.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ,400-1,700/month for 500-pilot scale: RDS t4g.medium ~, ElastiCache t3.small ~, ECS Fargate 2 tasks average ~, ALB ~, CloudFront ~, Cognito (free tier 50K MAU) ~/bin/zsh, SNS/SES notifications ~, CloudWatch ~, S3 ~. Budget headroom for MDMS integration data transfer. Scaling to 5,000 customers: RDS t4g.large, ElastiCache t3.medium, ECS 4-8 tasks - approximately ,800/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Cognito registration/login, program catalog, enrollment wizard with MDMS eligibility check, GDPR consent capture, curtailment event email/SMS notifications, basic consumption chart. Phase 2 (4 weeks): interval readings 13-month history with S3 archival, incentive credit tracker, CCPA data export and erasure workflow, WAF hardening. Phase 3 (4 weeks): consumption analytics with curtailment event overlays, notification preferences UI, service worker for offline catalog, cooperative operations admin panel for event management.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. ESLint with @typescript-eslint recommended rules. Prisma migrations tracked in git; never direct schema changes on production. Jest with 75% coverage gate; enrollment wizard steps and MDMS integration have unit tests with mocked MDMS API responses. GDPR/CCPA erasure logic has explicit integration tests covering soft-delete and 30-day purge. No raw SQL - Prisma ORM only to prevent accidental cross-customer data leakage.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: AMI MDMS vendor API unavailability blocks enrollment wizard step 2. Mitigation: circuit breaker returns cached eligibility result for known service points; new service points get async verification with email confirmation. Risk: interval_readings table growth (17.5M rows at 13 months for 500 customers) degrades consumption dashboard query performance. Mitigation: partial index on (service_point_id, reading_at DESC), S3 archival beyond 13 months. Tradeoff: Redis pub/sub for curtailment broadcast is simpler than SQS but loses messages if worker is down - acceptable for pilot; upgrade to SNS fan-out in production. Risk: GDPR erasure request conflicts with utility regulatory interval reading retention - resolved by excluding interval_readings from erasure scope with documented legal basis.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Redis pub/sub over WebSocket for curtailment event push - Bull worker approach simpler for pilot with email/SMS channels. Cognito optional MFA chosen over mandatory to reduce enrollment friction for residential cooperative customers. S3 Parquet archival chosen for interval_readings over TimescaleDB extension - avoids RDS custom extension dependency and enables future Athena analytics.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: proposes microservices with Kafka and Kubernetes deployment inconsistent with moderate complexity/ECS Fargate contract; thin data_model entities missing required fields.

profile.domain: Distribution Grid Switching Order Management

project_goal.value: Operator-facing web application for grid topology visualization and switching order management in medium-voltage distribution networks, used by field crews and control room dispatchers to coordinate planned outages and restoration sequences
project_goal.rationale: Core operational tool replacing paper-based switching order workflow

target_users.value: Field crew technicians and control room dispatchers at regional distribution utility
target_users.rationale: Primary user roles identified in operational workflow analysis

project_class.rationale: Browser-based operator workstation application with real-time topology updates

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, integrations, public_api
capabilities.rationale: Real-time topology visualization, SCADA/GIS integration API, dispatcher and field crew workflows

complexity_level.rationale: Real-time topology state machine, concurrent switching step execution, CIM network model integration

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect switching sequences can cause physical equipment damage and customer outages affecting safety-critical loads

data_sensitivity.rationale: Distribution network topology and switching order data classified as operational asset information under NERC CIP-002

external_exposure.rationale: Accessible only from utility control network VPN; all users authenticated with Keycloak SSO

access_model.value: Keycloak OIDC with RBAC: dispatcher (create/approve/close switching orders), field-crew (execute switching steps and report status), viewer (read-only topology), admin (user management)
access_model.rationale: Role-based access essential for switching order approval workflow and field crew step execution

feature_scope.value: Single-line diagram topology visualization with live device state, switching order creation and approval workflow, step-by-step execution tracking with field crew updates, topology rule validation engine, outage ticket management, crew assignment, CIM network model import
feature_scope.rationale: Full switching order management feature set for medium-voltage distribution operations

mvp_scope.value: Switching order creation with step authoring, dispatcher approval, field crew step execution with real-time status, single-line diagram with device state visualization, NERC CIP-002 audit trail
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for control room pilot with 10 dispatchers

security_baseline.value: Keycloak OIDC with LDAP federation, JWT short-lived tokens (15-min), mTLS on internal service mesh, TLS 1.3, VPN-only access, audit log for all topology-modifying actions
security_baseline.rationale: NERC CIP-002 asset identification and CIP-007 patch management require strong auth and comprehensive audit logging

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in Vue 3; Composition API suited for complex reactive topology state

backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for IEC CIM model parsing; FastAPI performance for real-time WebSocket connections

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for switching orders and topology; Redis for real-time topology state pub/sub

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot on GCP with GitLab CI
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Kubernetes for operational workloads; GCP for utility cloud migration target

privacy_retention_policy.value: Switching order records retained 7 years per ISO 55000 asset management; audit logs retained 10 years per NERC CIP requirements
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Grid asset maintenance and regulatory audit retention requirements

future_scope.value: Mobile app for field crew step execution, predictive outage scheduling via ML, SCADA direct device control integration
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP operational enhancement roadmap

constraints.value: Must import IEC 61968/61970 CIM XML network models from existing GIS export; VPN-only access from utility control network; 15-minute JWT token lifetime
constraints.rationale: CIM interoperability requirement and network security policy

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus on GKE; structured JSON logs to Google Cloud Logging; PagerDuty for switching order timeout alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Platform team monitoring standard for GKE workloads

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with control room operators in every sprint review
execution_preference.rationale: Operator feedback loop essential for safety-critical UX refinement

compliance_context.value: NERC CIP-002 BES Cyber System identification; IEC 61968/61970 CIM interoperability; ISO 55000 asset management record-keeping
compliance_context.rationale: High risk operational data with NERC CIP applicability and CIM interoperability mandate

project.goal: Grid topology visualization and switching order management for medium-voltage distribution operations
project.target_users: Control room dispatchers and field crew technicians

frontend.rendering: SVG-based single-line diagram with D3.js force layout
frontend.state: Pinia for topology state, Vue Query for server state

backend.api: REST + WebSocket for real-time topology updates

data.cache: Redis 7 for topology pub/sub and session tokens

reasoner_reviews.product: Round 3 plan is significantly stronger. Switching order state machine and CIM import pipeline are both well-specified. The topology rule validation engine is critical for safety - the concurrent step lock must prevent two field crew members from operating the same isolation_point simultaneously. The round 3 plan correctly adds a step_lock mechanism but the release-on-timeout logic needs to be explicit.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI WebSocket broadcast via Redis pub/sub is the right architecture for real-time single-line diagram updates. The CIM XML parser using Python pandapower library is appropriate. The step_lock timeout should be 10 minutes (typical field crew switching time) with automatic release and PagerDuty alert. The round 3 addition of topology_rule_violations view in PostgreSQL for pre-execution validation is excellent.
reasoner_reviews.security: Round 3 correctly addresses the mTLS internal service mesh gap from round 2 using Istio sidecar injection. Keycloak OIDC with 15-minute JWT and silent refresh is appropriate for dispatcher workstation sessions. The audit_log must capture the Keycloak user sub and display name, not just user_id, for CIP-002 compliance human-readable audit trail.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: VPN enforcement via GKE Network Policy denying ingress from non-VPN CIDR ranges is correctly specified. CIM XML import pipeline needs idempotency key on network model version to prevent duplicate topology_node insertions on re-import.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Vue Router 4 guarded by Keycloak OIDC silent-check on each route. Feature modules: topology-viewer (SVG single-line diagram), switching-orders (list, detail, authoring), step-execution (field crew mobile-friendly step form), admin (user/role management).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Topology Map (SVG single-line diagram with device state color coding: closed=green, open=red, unknown=grey), Switching Order List (filterable by status/crew/region), Switching Order Detail (step list with status and crew assignment), Step Execution Form (field crew: confirm step, upload photo evidence, report completion), CIM Import (file upload with validation report), Audit Log Viewer.
frontend.state_management: Pinia topology store: network_segments, isolation_points, topology_nodes keyed by CIM mRID. Vue Query for switching_orders and outage_tickets. WebSocket composable subscribes to topology_updates Redis channel, patches Pinia store on device state change events.
frontend.accessibility_notes: High-contrast mode for control room display wall. Keyboard navigation for step execution form (field crew may wear gloves). ARIA live regions for real-time topology change announcements.

backend.service_design: Three FastAPI services: topology-api (CIM import, topology state, WebSocket broadcast), switching-order-api (order CRUD, approval workflow, step_lock management, rule validation), integration-api (GIS/SCADA data ingest endpoints). All services in GKE with Istio sidecar mTLS.
backend.api_patterns: REST: POST /switching-orders, GET /switching-orders/:id, PATCH /switching-orders/:id/approve, POST /switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/execute, GET /topology/network-model. WebSocket: ws://topology-api/ws/topology-updates (broadcasts device_state_changed events to all connected clients). POST /topology/cim-import (multipart CIM XML, idempotent on model_version).
backend.business_modules: SwitchingOrderStateMachine: states draft/pending_approval/approved/in_execution/completed/cancelled. StepLockManager: acquires exclusive lock on isolation_point before step execution, 10-minute timeout with auto-release. TopologyRuleEngine: validates switching sequence against network_segments connectivity graph before approval, returns list of topology_rule_violations.
backend.failure_handling: StepLockManager auto-releases after 10-minute timeout with PagerDuty alert. CIM import failures produce validation_report with CIM element IDs causing parse errors; partial imports rejected atomically. WebSocket reconnection with exponential backoff on client; server-sent full topology snapshot on reconnect.

security.auth_design: Keycloak OIDC authorization code flow with PKCE. Vue frontend uses keycloak-js with silent-check-sso. FastAPI middleware validates JWT signature and expiry (15-min). Refresh token stored in httpOnly cookie, silent refresh via hidden iframe every 14 minutes.
security.authorization_model: Keycloak realm roles mapped to route-level FastAPI dependencies: dispatcher role required for POST /switching-orders and PATCH .../approve; field-crew required for POST .../steps/:id/execute; admin for CIM import and user management. Row-level security in PostgreSQL: field_crew can only execute steps assigned to their crew_assignment_id.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_logs table captures: keycloak_sub, display_name, action ENUM (order_created/approved/step_executed/topology_modified/cim_imported), resource_type, resource_id, changed_fields JSONB, ip_address, timestamp. Shipped to Google Cloud Storage immutable bucket for 10-year NERC CIP retention. FastAPI middleware auto-inserts audit record for all write operations.

data.entities: switching_orders (id UUID PK, title, region_code, outage_ticket_id FK, created_by keycloak_sub, approved_by, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/in_execution/completed/cancelled, planned_start, planned_end, created_at), switching_steps (id UUID PK, order_id FK, step_number INT, isolation_point_id FK, action ENUM open/close/tag/remove_tag, assigned_crew_id FK, status ENUM pending/locked/completed/failed, completed_by, completed_at, photo_evidence_url), isolation_points (id UUID PK, cim_mrid VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, network_segment_id FK, device_type ENUM breaker/recloser/switch/fuse, current_state ENUM open/closed/unknown, last_state_changed_at), network_segments (id UUID PK, cim_mrid UNIQUE, name, voltage_kv DECIMAL, upstream_node_id FK, downstream_nodes JSONB, segment_type ENUM feeder/lateral/tie), topology_nodes (id UUID PK, cim_mrid UNIQUE, substation_id, bus_voltage_kv, connected_segments JSONB, import_batch_id), outage_tickets (id UUID PK, order_id FK, affected_customer_count INT, cause, status ENUM open/in_progress/restored/closed, opened_at, closed_at), crew_assignments (id UUID PK, switching_order_id FK, crew_leader_id keycloak_sub, crew_members JSONB, assigned_steps JSONB), audit_logs (id UUID PK, keycloak_sub, display_name, action, resource_type, resource_id, changed_fields JSONB, ip_address, timestamp)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL (HA, us-central1). Topology rule validation uses materialized view topology_rule_violations refreshed after each CIM import. Redis 7 Memorystore for topology pub/sub channel and step_lock entries with 10-minute TTL.
data.consistency_notes: StepLockManager uses Redis SET NX EX 600 for atomic lock acquisition. CIM import uses database transaction with ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE for idempotency. Topology state changes from SCADA integration validated against isolation_points.current_state before write.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster (us-central1) with Istio service mesh. Three deployments: topology-api (2-6 pods, HPA on WebSocket connection count), switching-order-api (2-4 pods), integration-api (1-2 pods). Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL HA with automatic failover. Memorystore Redis 6 GB. Cloud Load Balancing with IAP for VPN-only enforcement.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: ruff lint, mypy strict, pytest (90% coverage gate), Vue3 ESLint + tsc, Vitest (85% gate), docker buildx, Artifact Registry push, Helm chart update in GitOps repo, ArgoCD sync. Staging auto-deploy on merge to main; production requires control room supervisor approval in GitLab.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus: switching_orders_created_total, step_locks_acquired_total, step_lock_timeout_total, topology_rule_violations_blocked_total, websocket_connections_active. Grafana dashboard: switching order pipeline state, active locks, topology change rate. Google Cloud Logging for structured JSON audit trail.
devops.rollback_strategy: ArgoCD automated rollback on failed readiness probe. PostgreSQL Cloud SQL PITR to 1-minute granularity. CIM import rollback: each import assigned batch_id; DELETE FROM topology_nodes WHERE import_batch_id = :id to revert bad import.
