previous_plan.title: FERC Interconnection Queue Management Portal - Round 3
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 and Fastify fullstack portal for independent power producers to submit interconnection study requests, track FERC Order 2023 milestone deadlines, and receive webhook notifications to utility planning systems. Auth0 org-based multi-tenancy with PostgreSQL RLS isolation. Study reports stored in S3. ECS Fargate deployment.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Auth0 organization JWT isolation, PostgreSQL RLS policies per organization_id on all tables including study_reports with operator_org_id constraint (fixed round 3). S3 pre-signed URLs for report access. FERC Order 2023 compliance milestone tracking.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate: api service 2-8 tasks, webhook-worker 2 tasks, milestone-notifier as separate ECS scheduled task. RDS Multi-AZ. GitHub Actions CI with Playwright E2E.
previous_plan.workflows: Interconnection request submission with queue_position assignment via SELECT FOR UPDATE (fixed round 2). Study report upload via Fastify body parser (ISS-004 not yet fixed). Webhook delivery via Bull queue with no DLQ (ISS-003 not yet fixed).

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 resolving final two issues: ISS-003 (Bull webhook queue has no dead-letter queue or admin retry) and ISS-004 (study report upload buffers 500 MB through Fastify causing ECS OOM). Both are targeted fixes. ISS-003: add a separate 'webhook-dlq' Bull queue; failed jobs after maxAttempts move to DLQ; admin panel /admin/webhooks shows DLQ contents with per-job retry and discard actions; DLQ depth Datadog alert. ISS-004: replace Fastify body buffer with S3 multipart pre-signed URL flow - Fastify generates presigned URLs for each 5 MB part, browser uploads parts directly, browser sends complete-multipart request to Fastify which calls S3 CompleteMultipartUpload then creates study_reports record. These changes address reliability and memory correctness without redesigning the core data model, which has been stable since round 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Added 'webhook-dlq' Bull queue. webhook-worker listens to both 'webhook-delivery' (active) and 'webhook-dlq' queues. Failed jobs on webhook-delivery are moved to webhook-dlq after 5 attempts with exponential backoff (30s, 2m, 8m, 32m, 128m). Admin panel /admin/webhooks now queries webhook_deliveries table (updated with dlq_status column) and exposes per-endpoint failed delivery list with manual Retry and Discard actions. Retry action re-enqueues job in webhook-delivery queue and resets attempt count. Datadog custom metric webhook_dlq_depth alerts if > 0 for > 10 minutes.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No webhook deliveries are silently lost; all failures are visible to admins and retryable; utility planning systems receive at-least-once delivery guarantee for FERC Order 2023 queue events.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced Fastify multipart body parser with S3 multipart pre-signed URL flow. New endpoint POST /api/v1/reports/{request_id}/multipart/initiate returns an upload_id and pre-signed URL for first part; POST /api/v1/reports/{request_id}/multipart/part?part_number={n} returns pre-signed URL for each subsequent 5 MB part; POST /api/v1/reports/{request_id}/multipart/complete sends part ETags to Fastify which calls S3 CompleteMultipartUpload and creates study_reports record. ECS Fastify tasks never buffer file bytes. Frontend useMultipartUpload hook manages part chunking, parallel uploads (up to 3 concurrent parts), progress tracking, and retry on part failure.
fix_report.expected_outcome: 500 MB study report uploads complete successfully without ECS memory exhaustion; upload progress visible to transmission operators; S3 handles all byte transfer.

target_output.title: FERC Interconnection Queue Management Portal
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 and Fastify fullstack portal enabling independent power producers to submit, track, and manage interconnection study requests with transmission operators under FERC Order 2023 queue reform requirements. Auth0 organization-based multi-tenancy with PostgreSQL row-level security isolates project developer data. Study reports upload directly to S3 via multipart pre-signed URLs (Fastify never buffers file bytes). Webhook deliveries to utility planning systems use a Bull queue with dead-letter queue and admin retry UI to guarantee at-least-once delivery for FERC-relevant status events. ECS Fargate deploys the API, webhook worker, and milestone notifier as independent services.
target_output.architecture_overview: Four-tier architecture on AWS ECS Fargate: Next.js 14 frontend (SSR on App Router for portal pages), Fastify REST API backend (Auth0 JWT validation, PostgreSQL RLS context injection, S3 pre-signed URL generation, Bull queue producer), webhook-worker service (Bull queue consumer with DLQ for failed deliveries), and milestone-notifier scheduled task (independent ECS task running every 5 minutes, reading milestone_deadlines for due notifications). PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ stores interconnection requests, study queues, and milestone state with row-level security enforcing organization isolation. S3 receives study report PDFs via multipart upload directly from browser. ElastiCache Redis hosts Bull queues. Auth0 organization tokens carry organization_id and role claims consumed by both frontend and backend. Datadog APM traces all Fastify handlers; PagerDuty fires on milestone overdue events.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, React Query, Zustand, next-auth v5 for Auth0. Backend: Node.js 22, Fastify 4, Zod, Bull 4, AWS SDK v3 for S3 and Secrets Manager, pg driver with node-postgres. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7 (Bull queues). Storage: S3 with SSE-S3 and multipart upload. Auth: Auth0 organizations with JWT RS256. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, ECR, Secrets Manager. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Playwright E2E. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Developer Request Submission: multi-step POI wizard validating coordinates against interconnection zones, capacity MW and study type selection, queue position auto-assigned via SELECT FOR UPDATE, FERC milestone deadlines computed from milestone_templates. Developer Queue Dashboard: list of submitted requests with queue position, milestone deadline countdown, and study report download via S3 pre-signed URL (15-min expiry). Transmission Operator Queue Management: pending request list filterable by study type, request detail with assignment controls, study report upload via S3 multipart pre-signed URL flow with browser-side progress indicator. Webhook Integration: developer or operator org registers webhook endpoint URL and HMAC secret; Bull queue delivers event payloads with retry; DLQ captures persistent failures; admin retry UI. Milestone Notifier: separate ECS task reads milestone_deadlines with due_date <= NOW() + 48h and unsent notifications, sends alerts via SES and emits Datadog metric for overdue count. Admin Panel: queue position management, organization and user management, webhook DLQ retry interface.
target_output.system_components: Next.js 14 ECS Fargate service (SSR pods, 2-4 tasks): portal pages with Auth0 session. Fastify API ECS Fargate service (2-8 tasks, auto-scale CPU 60%): Auth0 JWT validation middleware, RLS context plugin (SET LOCAL app.current_org_id), Zod route schemas, S3 pre-signed URL generation, Bull job enqueue. webhook-worker ECS Fargate service (2 tasks): Bull consumer on webhook-delivery queue with 5-attempt exponential backoff; on failure after maxAttempts moves job to webhook-dlq queue; Bull consumer on webhook-dlq with admin-triggered retry. milestone-notifier ECS scheduled task (every 5 minutes, separate task definition): reads milestone_deadlines, sends SES notifications, emits Datadog metric. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ: primary + standby, all tables with RLS policies. ElastiCache Redis 7: webhook-delivery and webhook-dlq Bull queues, session cache. S3 bucket interconnection-study-reports: SSE-S3, versioning, lifecycle for Glacier at 2 years and deletion at 7 years, abort-incomplete-multipart lifecycle rule 24 hours.
target_output.workflows: Interconnection Request Submission: developer submits POST /api/v1/requests with project_name, poi_latitude, poi_longitude, capacity_mw, study_type; Fastify middleware sets app.current_org_id from JWT; service layer opens PostgreSQL transaction, SELECT FOR UPDATE on study_queues.last_position for the target operator_org_id, increments and assigns queue_position, inserts interconnection_requests row, computes milestone_deadlines from milestone_templates for the study_type, inserts milestone rows; commits transaction; enqueues webhook event 'request.submitted' on Bull delivery queue. Study Report Multipart Upload: operator calls POST /reports/{id}/multipart/initiate - Fastify calls S3 CreateMultipartUpload, stores upload_id in Redis, returns upload_id and first part pre-signed URL. Operator browser uses useMultipartUpload hook: reads file in 5 MB chunks, obtains pre-signed URL per part from POST /reports/{id}/multipart/part?part_number={n}, uploads part directly to S3 (up to 3 concurrent parts), collects ETags. On last part, calls POST /reports/{id}/multipart/complete with part ETags array; Fastify calls S3 CompleteMultipartUpload, inserts study_reports record with s3_key and file_size_bytes, enqueues 'report.available' webhook event. Webhook DLQ Retry: admin navigates to /admin/webhooks, views webhook_deliveries with dlq_status='dlq'; clicks Retry on a delivery row; admin panel calls PATCH /api/v1/webhooks/deliveries/{id}/retry; Fastify re-enqueues job in webhook-delivery Bull queue with attempt_count reset to 0.
target_output.data_model: project_developers (id UUID PK, organization_id TEXT NOT NULL, auth0_org_id TEXT UNIQUE, name TEXT, contact_email TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). transmission_operators (id UUID PK, organization_id TEXT NOT NULL, auth0_org_id TEXT UNIQUE, name TEXT, planning_region TEXT). interconnection_requests (id UUID PK, developer_org_id TEXT NOT NULL, operator_org_id TEXT NOT NULL, project_name TEXT, poi_latitude NUMERIC(9,6), poi_longitude NUMERIC(9,6), capacity_mw NUMERIC(8,2), study_type ENUM('feasibility','system_impact','facilities'), queue_position INT, status ENUM('submitted','study_in_progress','study_complete','withdrawn'), submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, withdrawn_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ferc_filing_ref TEXT). study_queues (id UUID PK, operator_org_id TEXT, queue_name TEXT, last_position INT DEFAULT 0). study_reports (id UUID PK, request_id UUID FK, report_type TEXT, s3_key TEXT NOT NULL, file_size_bytes BIGINT, uploaded_by_id UUID, available_at TIMESTAMPTZ). milestone_deadlines (id UUID PK, request_id UUID FK, milestone_type TEXT, due_date DATE NOT NULL, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, notification_sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ). milestone_templates (id UUID PK, study_type TEXT, milestone_type TEXT, days_from_submission INT). webhook_endpoints (id UUID PK, organization_id TEXT, url TEXT, hmac_secret_encrypted TEXT, events TEXT[], active BOOLEAN). webhook_deliveries (id UUID PK, endpoint_id UUID FK, event_type TEXT, payload_json JSONB, status ENUM('pending','delivered','failed','dlq'), attempts INT DEFAULT 0, next_retry_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_error TEXT, delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1/ with OpenAPI 3.0 spec. POST /api/v1/requests: submit interconnection request; returns request with queue_position and milestone_deadlines. GET /api/v1/requests: developer sees own requests; operator sees assigned queue; cursor pagination. GET /api/v1/requests/{id}: request detail with milestones and report availability. PATCH /api/v1/requests/{id}/status: operator updates status (study_in_progress, study_complete). POST /api/v1/reports/{id}/multipart/initiate: start S3 multipart upload; returns {upload_id, first_part_url}. POST /api/v1/reports/{id}/multipart/part?part_number={n}: returns pre-signed URL for part n (max 100 parts, 5 MB each). POST /api/v1/reports/{id}/multipart/complete: finalize upload with part ETags; creates study_reports record. GET /api/v1/reports/{id}/download: returns 302 redirect to S3 pre-signed URL (15-min expiry). POST /api/v1/webhooks/endpoints: register endpoint URL and HMAC secret. GET /api/v1/webhooks/endpoints: list org webhook endpoints with delivery success rate. PATCH /api/v1/webhooks/deliveries/{id}/retry (admin): re-enqueue from DLQ. GET /api/v1/admin/webhooks/dlq (admin): list DLQ entries with last_error and attempt count.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 RS256 JWT with organization_id and role claims validated on every Fastify request via JWKS. PostgreSQL RLS: interconnection_requests SELECT/UPDATE WHERE developer_org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id') OR operator_org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id'); study_reports SELECT WHERE EXISTS (request WHERE developer_org_id OR operator_org_id matches). Webhook HMAC-SHA256 signature header (X-Interconnect-Signature) on all deliveries; receiving system validates before processing. S3 pre-signed URLs with 15-minute expiry; multipart part URLs include upload_id binding to prevent URL reuse across uploads. S3 bucket: no public access, SSE-S3. ECS task IAM role: S3 PutObject/CompleteMultipartUpload on reports bucket, GetObject, Secrets Manager GetSecretValue. FERC compliance: milestone_deadlines records with notification_sent_at ensure audit trail for FERC Order 2023 timeline evidence.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate us-east-1: four services - frontend (Next.js, 2-4 tasks), api (Fastify, 2-8 tasks, auto-scale CPU 60%), webhook-worker (2 tasks fixed), milestone-notifier (EventBridge rule every 5 minutes, ECS run-task). ALB with HTTPS listener (ACM cert), WAF OWASP managed rule group. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ in private subnets. ElastiCache Redis in private subnets. S3 VPC gateway endpoint. GitHub Actions CI: on PR ESLint, tsc, Jest 80% coverage, docker build. On merge to main: push ECR, ECS rolling deploy for frontend and api services, webhook-worker rolling deploy, update milestone-notifier task definition. Database migrations via node-pg-migrate ECS one-off task before deploy. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for Auth0 secret, S3 signing key, PostgreSQL password; ECS task role reads at startup. Rollback: ECS service update to previous task definition revision.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces: Fastify handler spans including PostgreSQL query time (RLS context SET LOCAL overhead), Bull enqueue latency, S3 pre-signed URL generation time. Custom Datadog metrics: interconnection_requests_submitted_total (by study_type), webhook_delivery_success_rate (per endpoint), webhook_dlq_depth (total failed jobs in webhook-dlq queue), milestone_overdue_count (milestones past due_date with no completed_at). Dashboards: queue depth by operator region, milestone compliance rate by study_type, webhook delivery success rate trending, DLQ depth over time. PagerDuty alerts: milestone_overdue_count > 0 fires critical (FERC compliance risk), webhook_dlq_depth > 0 for > 10 minutes fires high, API p99 latency > 2s fires warning.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate estimated monthly (us-east-1): frontend 2 tasks ~$40, api 2-8 tasks ~$80-300, webhook-worker 2 tasks ~$40, milestone-notifier (run-task 5-min intervals) ~$10. RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.medium ~$110. ElastiCache Redis cache.t3.medium ~$40. S3 storage at 1 TB (5 years of study reports) ~$25. ALB ~$25. Total ~$330-550/month scaling with queue volume. Scaling: ECS auto-scale on CPU for api tier; separate webhook-worker scaling on Bull queue depth metric via Datadog to ECS auto-scale policy.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): developer request submission with queue position, Auth0 org multi-tenancy, PostgreSQL RLS, milestone deadline computation from templates, study report multipart S3 upload, study report download via pre-signed URL, webhook endpoint registration and Bull delivery queue with DLQ, admin webhook retry UI, milestone notifier ECS scheduled task, GitHub Actions CI with Playwright E2E, Datadog APM. Phase 2 (8 weeks): operator portal enhancements (batch assignment, milestone calendar view), SES email notifications for milestone due dates, webhook delivery HMAC-SHA256 signature validation reference implementation for utility planners, FERC filing reference number tracking and search. Phase 3 (6 weeks): FERC eTariff API integration for automatic queue position publication, cluster study coordination tools for FERC Order 2023 cluster interconnection batching, analytics dashboard for queue throughput and milestone compliance rates.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode end-to-end; no any types in Fastify handlers or Bull job payloads. Zod schemas shared between frontend (React Hook Form validation) and backend (Fastify schema validation) via packages/shared. PostgreSQL RLS policies covered by integration tests asserting cross-org reads return empty results. Bull job handlers idempotent: duplicate webhook deliveries safe to re-enqueue. S3 multipart upload: useMultipartUpload hook has unit tests for part size boundary conditions and retry on 5xx S3 error. Playwright E2E covers submission-to-report-download flow and admin DLQ retry flow. ADR documents for Bull DLQ design, S3 multipart URL flow, and PostgreSQL RLS policy patterns.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Bull Redis connection loss halts webhook delivery without alerting; mitigate with Datadog custom metric webhook_dlq_depth and Bull health check endpoint scraped by Datadog Agent. Risk: S3 multipart upload parts not aborted on client abandonment; mitigate with S3 lifecycle rule aborting incomplete multipart uploads after 24 hours to prevent orphaned storage. Risk: Auth0 JWT JWKS cache staleness after Auth0 key rotation causes 401 errors; implement refresh on 401 before propagating to client. Tradeoff: separate ECS milestone-notifier task adds operational complexity but ensures FERC deadline notifications fire independently of API rolling deployments. Tradeoff: Bull DLQ as a separate named queue over Bull built-in failed queue for cleaner admin UI query patterns and explicit retry semantics.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose S3 multipart pre-signed URLs over Fastify streaming proxy to S3 because ECS Fargate memory per task is bounded at 2 GB and concurrent 500 MB uploads would exceed it; direct S3 upload removes Fastify from the data path entirely. Selected Bull DLQ as separate queue over failed job retry built-in to enable admin-triggered selective retry without re-queuing all failed jobs at once. Confirmed SELECT FOR UPDATE on study_queues.last_position is sufficient queue position serialization given expected submission rate (< 50 concurrent submissions per operator_org); advisory lock not needed at this scale.

metadata.notes: FERC interconnection queue portal round 4; resolves webhook DLQ missing (ISS-003) and Fastify file upload OOM (ISS-004) with S3 multipart pre-signed URL flow.

profile.domain: Grid Operations Tooling — EMS Model Management

project_goal.value: Provide EMS grid operations engineers with a high-performance CLI tool to query, diff, and validate CIM XML network model snapshots, enabling rapid identification of topology changes between model versions during outage planning and EMS model updates.
project_goal.rationale: Model validation and diff operations currently require manual XML comparison; the tool automates this for models up to 500 MB.

target_users.value: EMS network model engineers, grid operations model managers, and transmission planning staff at utilities and ISOs who maintain CIM XML model snapshots.
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified through interviews with model management teams at three utility clients.

project_class.rationale: Local command-line execution on engineer workstations; no browser UI, no hosted service.

capabilities.rationale: CLI processing logic and structured data parsing; no frontend or hosted backend required.

complexity_level.rationale: CIM XML parsing at 500 MB scale requires memory-efficient streaming; diff and validation logic is non-trivial but well-bounded.

risk_level.rationale: Local-only CLI with no network exposure; reads model files from local filesystem, writes reports to stdout or local file.

data_sensitivity.rationale: CIM XML model files contain utility network topology data — internal use only but not classified or regulated.

external_exposure.rationale: CLI runs on local engineer workstations with no network endpoints exposed.

access_model.value: Local file system access only; no authentication layer. Model files protected by OS-level file permissions on engineer workstations.
access_model.rationale: Local CLI tool; OS-level access controls are sufficient.

feature_scope.value: cim-diff command comparing two CIM XML snapshots with streaming output of added, modified, and removed network elements; cim-validate command running configurable topology rule sets against a single model file; cim-query command for filtered extraction of specific CIM object types; JSON and human-readable text output formats; configurable rule files in TOML.
feature_scope.rationale: Core commands covering the three principal model management workflows.

mvp_scope.value: cim-diff and cim-validate commands with built-in IEC 61970 topology rule set, JSON output mode, and human-readable text summary.
mvp_scope.rationale: The two highest-priority commands covering outage planning diff and pre-promotion validation workflows.

security_baseline.value: Binary distributed via signed releases on GitHub Releases; no network calls at runtime; no telemetry; input file path validation to prevent directory traversal.
security_baseline.rationale: Local-only CLI security baseline sufficient given no network exposure.

frontend_stack.value: None — CLI tool with stdout/stderr output only
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; all output goes to terminal or redirected file.

backend_stack.value: Rust 1.78 with Clap 4 for command parsing
backend_stack.rationale: Rust provides the memory efficiency and parse throughput needed for sub-second diff of 500 MB CIM XML files without garbage collection pauses.

data_platform.value: No database; input is CIM XML files read from local filesystem; output is JSON or plain text written to stdout or a specified output file path.
data_platform.rationale: Local file I/O is sufficient for all model management workflows.

hosting_target.value: Local CLI binary distribution via GitHub Releases; optional Homebrew tap and Cargo install for engineer self-provisioning.
hosting_target.rationale: Engineers provision tooling on their own workstations; no hosted infrastructure required.

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data processed; no telemetry transmitted. Model file content stays on local workstation.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Local-only processing with no data leaving the engineer workstation.

future_scope.value: cim-merge command for three-way CIM XML merge; SPARQL-style query language for complex CIM object graph traversal; VS Code extension wrapping CLI diff and validate commands.
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP extensions identified through user interviews.

constraints.value: cim-diff must complete in under 10 seconds for a 500 MB CIM XML file pair on a standard engineer workstation (Intel Core i7, 16 GB RAM). No external network calls at runtime. Binary must support Linux x86-64, macOS arm64, and Windows x86-64.
constraints.rationale: Performance budget derived from engineer workflow requirements; cross-platform needed for mixed OS fleet.

observability_baseline.value: Opt-in Sentry crash reporting via --enable-crash-reporting flag. Structured timing output with --verbose flag showing parse time, diff time, and serialization time.
observability_baseline.rationale: Opt-in crash reporting to catch panics on edge-case CIM models; local verbose timing for performance troubleshooting.

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development on main; feature branches with PR review required. Releases cut via GitHub Actions on version tag push.
execution_preference.rationale: Standard open-source release workflow for CLI tooling.

project.goal: High-performance CLI for CIM XML model snapshot diff and validation
project.target_users: EMS grid operations and transmission planning engineers

backend.parsing: quick-xml for streaming SAX-style CIM XML parsing

data.input: CIM XML files from local filesystem up to 500 MB
data.output: JSON or human-readable text to stdout or file

devops.release: GitHub Releases with cross-platform binary matrix build

constraints.performance: cim-diff under 10 seconds for 500 MB file pair
constraints.platforms: Linux x86-64, macOS arm64, Windows x86-64

reasoner_reviews.product: The three-command scope (diff, validate, query) covers the principal EMS model management workflows cleanly. The cim-diff command is the highest-value deliverable given current manual XML comparison pain. cim-query can be deferred to a post-MVP release without blocking engineer adoption.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rust with quick-xml streaming SAX parser is the correct architecture for sub-10-second diff of 500 MB CIM XML files. The unresolved concern from prior rounds is the text output attribute delta truncation: the hardcoded 10-item limit hides changes in attribute-heavy CIM classes like ACLineSegment, making the diff output misleading for engineers reviewing critical network changes.
reasoner_reviews.security: Local-only CLI has minimal attack surface. Input path canonicalization via std::fs::canonicalize prevents directory traversal. The opt-in Sentry crash reporting integration must not transmit CIM file content in breadcrumbs.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Cross-platform binary matrix build in GitHub Actions is well-supported by cargo-cross. The 10-second performance constraint on a 16 GB i7 workstation is achievable with the streaming parser architecture and Rayon parallel parsing.

backend.service_design: Rust binary with Clap 4 subcommand router: cim diff <baseline.xml> <updated.xml> [--output json|text] [--filter <object-type>] [--max-attrs <N>], cim validate <model.xml> [--rules <rules.toml>] [--output json|text], cim query <model.xml> --type <CIM-class> [--where <attr>=<val>]. No daemon or server process.
backend.api_patterns: Internal module API: CimParser trait with parse_stream(reader: BufReader) -> Result<CimObjectIndex>; DiffEngine with diff(baseline: &CimObjectIndex, updated: &CimObjectIndex) -> DiffReport; RuleEngine with validate(index: &CimObjectIndex, rules: &[Rule]) -> Vec<Finding>. Public CLI API is argv only.
backend.failure_handling: Malformed CIM XML emits a structured error to stderr with line number and element context, exits with code 1. Rule file TOML parse errors list all conflicting rules with file and line references before exit. Large file OOM conditions caught via Rust panic handler with guidance to split input.

security.auth_design: No authentication. Model files accessed via OS file permissions only.
security.secrets_management: No secrets. Sentry DSN for opt-in crash reporting stored in compiled binary only; not readable from model files or rule files.
security.abuse_prevention: Input file path canonicalization via std::fs::canonicalize before open to prevent directory traversal. Sentry breadcrumbs explicitly exclude file content — only panic stack traces and CLI argument metadata transmitted.

data.entities: model_snapshots (CIM XML files on local filesystem identified by path and optional rdf:about URI), cim_objects (in-memory index keyed by rdf:ID typed by CIM class name storing attribute map), topology_diffs (added/modified/removed sets of cim_object IDs with attribute-level delta for modified objects), validation_rules (TOML-defined checks: rule_id, cim_class, condition expression, severity, message), change_summaries (per-diff aggregated counts by object type and change kind).
data.storage_design: No persistent storage. CimObjectIndex held in memory during command execution and dropped on exit. Output serialized to stdout as JSON or formatted text.
data.migration_notes: No migration required — stateless local tool. Rule TOML schema versioned in the rule file header; backward-compatible additions only.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions matrix build: cargo build --release for linux-x86_64, macos-arm64, windows-x86_64. Binaries uploaded as GitHub Release assets and to a Homebrew tap repository. cargo publish to crates.io on release tag.
devops.ci_cd_design: PR CI: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test with unit and integration tests using fixture CIM XML files, cargo bench for performance regression check against 100 MB fixture. Release CI: triggered on v* tag push, runs full matrix build.
devops.rollback_strategy: Binary rollback via GitHub Releases previous version download. Homebrew tap pinned by version tag.

ISS-001.title: Rule file conflict resolution order undefined — silent validation gaps possible
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not specify behavior when multiple --rules files define rules with the same rule_id. Round 2 added last-writer-wins semantics documented in CLI help text.

ISS-002.title: cim-diff output for large modified objects truncates attribute deltas — engineers cannot see all changed attributes
ISS-002.detail: The diff report for CIM objects with many attributes (e.g., ACLineSegment with 40-plus attributes) truncates the attribute delta list to the first 10 items in text output mode, hiding potentially critical changes.

focus_issues.title: cim-diff truncates attribute deltas for large CIM objects
focus_issues.detail: Diff report truncates attribute delta list to first 10 items for CIM objects with many attributes in text output mode.

latest_recommendations: Remove truncation limit from text output mode for attribute deltas; use a --max-attrs flag with default 0 (unlimited) for user control
Add --compact flag for engineers who prefer the previous truncated summary view
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: EMS CIM Model Diff and Validation CLI — Round 2

convergence_state.reason: ISS-002 unresolved across two consecutive rounds

previous_audits.summary: Strong Rust architecture with correct CLI-only design, but rule conflict resolution and diff output truncation create usability gaps for production model management.
strengths: Rust streaming SAX parser correctly chosen for 500 MB CIM XML performance budget
CLI subcommand structure with Clap 4 matches the three principal use cases
concerns: Rule file conflict resolution order undefined for duplicate rule_ids across multiple --rules files
No mention of attribute delta truncation behavior in diff output
blocking_issues: Silent validation gap when conflicting rule_ids exist across multiple rule files
recommendations: Define last-writer-wins or fail-fast semantics for duplicate rule_ids with explicit error messaging
Remove or make configurable the attribute delta truncation in text diff output

issue_updates.title: Rule file conflict resolution order undefined
issue_updates.detail: Multiple rule files with duplicate rule_id behavior not specified.

previous_audits.summary: Rule conflict resolution addressed with last-writer-wins; attribute delta truncation still limits diff usefulness for large CIM objects.
strengths: ISS-001 resolved with last-writer-wins semantics and CLI warning on duplicate rule_id detection
Cross-platform binary matrix build in GitHub Actions well-specified
concerns: ISS-002 (attribute delta truncation) still present in text output mode
recommendations: Remove hardcoded 10-item truncation from text output; introduce --max-attrs flag defaulting to unlimited
Document changed attribute counts per object in diff summary statistics

issue_updates.detail: Text output mode still truncates attribute delta list to first 10 items.

best_plan.title: EMS CIM Model Diff and Validation CLI — Round 2
best_plan.executive_summary: High-performance Rust CLI tool for EMS grid operations engineers to diff and validate CIM XML network model snapshots using streaming SAX parsing, enabling sub-10-second comparison of 500 MB model files with configurable TOML rule sets.
best_plan.architecture_overview: Single-binary Rust CLI with Clap 4 subcommand routing. Streaming quick-xml SAX parser builds in-memory CimObjectIndex for diff and validation operations. No server process, no network calls.
best_plan.workflows: cim-diff: two CIM XML files parsed in parallel, CimObjectIndex built for each, DiffEngine computes added/modified/removed sets, results serialized to JSON or text. cim-validate: single model parsed, RuleEngine evaluates TOML rules, findings output with rule_id and CIM object reference.
best_plan.risks_and_tradeoffs: Text output mode truncates attribute delta list to first 10 items for large CIM objects — ISS-002 not yet resolved.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 addresses ISS-002: the hardcoded 10-item truncation of attribute deltas in text output mode hides changes in attribute-heavy CIM classes like ACLineSegment. The fix removes the truncation limit from text output by default and introduces a --max-attrs flag (default 0 = unlimited) for engineers who prefer a compact view. The diff summary statistics section gains a per-object changed-attribute count. No architectural changes required.

fix_report.action_taken: Removed the hardcoded 10-item truncation limit from the TextSerializer in the DiffReport serializer. Added --max-attrs <N> flag to the cim diff subcommand (default 0 = unlimited); when N > 0, output truncates at N attributes per object with a trailing ellipsis and count of remaining changes. Updated diff summary statistics block to include per-object total changed-attribute count regardless of --max-attrs setting.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Engineers can see all changed attributes for ACLineSegment, PowerTransformer, and other attribute-heavy CIM classes in text output mode by default. The --max-attrs flag preserves the option for compact output in summary-focused workflows.

target_output.title: EMS CIM Model Diff and Validation CLI
target_output.executive_summary: High-performance Rust CLI tool enabling EMS grid operations and transmission planning engineers to diff CIM XML network model snapshots, validate topology rule sets, and query model objects — all within a sub-10-second performance budget for 500 MB files. Distributed as a cross-platform binary via GitHub Releases and Homebrew tap with no runtime network access and no server infrastructure. Round 3 removes the attribute delta truncation limit in text output mode and introduces a --max-attrs flag to give engineers full visibility into changed CIM object properties.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single statically-linked Rust binary with Clap 4 subcommand routing for three commands: cim diff, cim validate, and cim query. The CimParser module uses quick-xml in streaming SAX mode to build a CimObjectIndex (HashMap keyed by rdf:ID) without loading the full DOM into memory, keeping peak RAM under 2 GB for 500 MB CIM files. DiffEngine compares two CimObjectIndex instances to produce a DiffReport with typed added/modified/removed sets and per-object attribute-level deltas. RuleEngine evaluates TOML-defined rules against a CimObjectIndex and emits structured Finding records. All output serialized to stdout as JSON (machine-readable) or formatted text (human-readable) with configurable verbosity. The TextSerializer no longer truncates attribute delta lists.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Rust 1.78, compiled with LTO and codegen-units=1 for release binary size optimization. CLI parsing: Clap 4 with derive macros for subcommand and argument definitions. XML parsing: quick-xml 0.31 in async buffered reader mode for streaming SAX-style CIM XML traversal. Serialization: serde_json for JSON output mode; custom TextSerializer for formatted text output (no truncation by default, --max-attrs N for optional cap). Rule files: toml 0.8 crate for TOML deserialization into typed Rule structs. Crash reporting: sentry 0.32 SDK behind --enable-crash-reporting opt-in flag. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with cargo-cross for cross-compilation matrix.
target_output.functional_feature_map: cim-diff command: parses two CIM XML model snapshots in parallel using Rayon thread pool, builds CimObjectIndex per file, computes added/modified/removed sets via DiffEngine, outputs full attribute-level deltas with no truncation (--max-attrs 0 default) or configurable limit (--max-attrs N), includes per-object changed-attribute count in summary statistics. cim-validate command: parses a single CIM XML model, evaluates all rules from one or more TOML rule files against the CimObjectIndex using RuleEngine, outputs findings with severity, rule_id, affected CIM object rdf:ID, and message. cim-query command: parses a single CIM XML model, filters CIM objects by --type (CIM class name) and optional --where attribute equality predicates, outputs matching objects in JSON or text.
target_output.system_components: CimParser module: quick-xml streaming SAX reader building CimObjectIndex HashMap from rdf:ID to CimObject (class_name, attributes HashMap). DiffEngine module: parallel HashMap traversal producing DiffReport (added, removed, modified with full attribute deltas). RuleEngine module: evaluates Vec<Rule> against CimObjectIndex, producing Vec<Finding>. TextSerializer module: renders DiffReport and FindingList to formatted terminal output with configurable --max-attrs limit (default 0 = unlimited) and colored severity labels via the colored crate. JsonSerializer module: renders via serde_json. Clap 4 root command with diff/validate/query subcommands.
target_output.workflows: Model Diff Workflow: Engineer runs cim diff baseline_2026Q1.xml updated_2026Q2.xml --output text; CimParser spawns two Rayon threads parsing each file into a CimObjectIndex; DiffEngine traverses both indexes computing added/modified/removed CIM objects; TextSerializer renders each modified object with its full attribute delta list (all changed attributes shown by default, --max-attrs N to cap with trailing count of remaining changes); summary statistics block shows total counts by object type and per-object changed-attribute totals; command exits with code 0 if diff succeeds, code 2 if parse error. Model Validation Workflow: Engineer runs cim validate substation_model.xml --rules nerc_cip.toml --rules site_rules.toml --output json; rule files loaded and merged with last-writer-wins on duplicate rule_id with a warning to stderr; RuleEngine evaluates each rule against the CimObjectIndex; JSON findings array output with severity field; exit code 1 if any critical finding, 0 otherwise.
target_output.data_model: model_snapshots (local file path: PathBuf, cim_header_uri: optional String, parsed via CimParser into CimObjectIndex). cim_objects (rdf_id: String PK, class_name: String, attributes: HashMap<String, String>). topology_diffs (baseline_snapshot_path, updated_snapshot_path, added: Vec<rdf_id>, removed: Vec<rdf_id>, modified: Vec<ObjectDelta> where ObjectDelta = (rdf_id, changed_attrs: Vec<AttrDelta> where AttrDelta = (attr_name, old_val, new_val))). validation_rules (rule_id: String, cim_class: String, condition: String expression, severity: Enum critical/high/medium/low, message_template: String) deserialized from TOML. change_summaries (object_type: String, added_count: usize, removed_count: usize, modified_count: usize, total_changed_attrs: usize).
target_output.api_design: Public CLI API via argv. cim diff <baseline.xml> <updated.xml> [--output json|text] [--filter <CIM-class>] [--max-attrs <N>] [--verbose] [--enable-crash-reporting]. cim validate <model.xml> [--rules <rules.toml>]... [--output json|text] [--verbose]. cim query <model.xml> --type <CIM-class> [--where <attr>=<val>]... [--output json|text]. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 validation findings found (validate command), 2 parse error, 3 invalid arguments. Internal module API: CimParser::parse_stream(path: &Path) -> Result<CimObjectIndex, ParseError>, DiffEngine::diff(a: &CimObjectIndex, b: &CimObjectIndex) -> DiffReport, RuleEngine::validate(index: &CimObjectIndex, rules: &[Rule]) -> Vec<Finding>.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Input file paths canonicalized via std::fs::canonicalize before any file open call to prevent directory traversal attacks via symlink or relative path manipulation. No network calls at runtime — verified by a compile-time cfg check disabling all TCP socket creation outside the optional Sentry integration. Sentry breadcrumbs explicitly constructed to exclude file content and file paths — only panic stack trace, CLI subcommand name, and crate version transmitted. Release binaries signed via GitHub Actions using gh attestation for supply chain verification.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI on every PR: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test (unit tests for CimParser, DiffEngine, RuleEngine and integration tests using IEC 61970 test suite fixture CIM XML files), cargo bench on the 100 MB fixture to detect performance regressions above 10% from baseline. Release workflow triggers on v* tag: matrix build via cargo-cross for linux-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl (static binary), aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc; binaries uploaded to GitHub Release as cim-linux-x86_64, cim-macos-arm64, cim-windows-x86_64.exe; Homebrew tap updated via dispatch workflow on release publish; cargo publish to crates.io with CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN secret.
target_output.observability: Opt-in Sentry crash reporting activated by --enable-crash-reporting flag: captures Rust panics with stack trace and CLI subcommand context (no file content or paths). --verbose flag adds per-stage timing output to stderr: Parse baseline: 3.2s, Parse updated: 3.4s, Diff compute: 0.8s, Serialize: 0.1s — total: 7.5s. cargo bench performance suite tracks parse and diff throughput per release; results posted as GitHub Actions step summary.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost — local binary with no hosted services. GitHub Actions free tier sufficient for CI and release matrix builds (3 platform targets, approximately 15 minutes total per release). Homebrew tap hosted on GitHub at no cost. crates.io publishing free. Scaling is horizontal per-engineer workstation: each engineer runs their own binary instance with no shared state.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Core MVP (Weeks 1-4): Implement CimParser with quick-xml streaming, CimObjectIndex, DiffEngine, and TextSerializer with full attribute delta output (no truncation). cim-diff and cim-validate subcommands. TOML rule file loading with last-writer-wins conflict resolution. GitHub Actions CI with unit and integration test fixtures. Phase 2 — JSON Output and Performance (Weeks 5-6): JsonSerializer for machine-readable output. cargo bench suite with 100 MB and 500 MB fixture performance gates. --max-attrs flag for configurable text output verbosity. Cross-platform binary matrix build and GitHub Releases workflow. Phase 3 — Query and Distribution (Weeks 7-8): cim-query subcommand with --type and --where predicates. Homebrew tap setup. cargo publish to crates.io. Opt-in Sentry crash reporting integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public module functions must have Rust doc comments with example usage. cargo clippy -- -D warnings must pass in CI. Integration test fixtures must include at least one ACLineSegment, one PowerTransformer, and one Substation CIM object. cim-diff text output truncation threshold defaults to 0 (unlimited) — any PR introducing a hardcoded truncation limit will be rejected. Rule TOML schema changes require a schema version bump and backward compatibility test.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: quick-xml streaming parser encountering malformed CIM XML from legacy EMS exports causes unexpected panic rather than clean error. Mitigation: fuzz testing via cargo-fuzz targeting the CimParser module against mutated CIM XML fixtures. Risk: CimObjectIndex memory footprint exceeds 16 GB RAM on pathological 500 MB models with extremely high attribute density. Mitigation: memory profiling benchmarks on worst-case IEC 61970 test network models. Tradeoff: streaming SAX parser over DOM parser — enables 500 MB performance budget but prevents XPath-style queries across the full CIM graph without building the full in-memory index first.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Attribute delta truncation resolved: --max-attrs flag introduced with default 0 (unlimited); engineers who prefer compact summary output use --max-attrs 10 or similar. Rule file conflict resolution: last-writer-wins with stderr warning on duplicate rule_id detection. Cross-platform binary naming convention confirmed: cim-linux-x86_64, cim-macos-arm64, cim-windows-x86_64.exe as GitHub Release assets.

metadata.notes: Rust CLI for EMS CIM XML diff and validation — round 3 removes attribute delta truncation and adds --max-attrs flag.

profile.domain: Power Grid Operations - Short-Term Frequency Deviation Prediction AI System

project_goal.value: AI system applying machine learning to predict short-term (1-4 hour ahead) grid frequency deviation events on high-renewable-penetration transmission systems, enabling system operators to proactively procure ancillary services before frequency excursions occur
project_goal.rationale: Proactive ancillary service procurement reduces real-time balancing costs and improves grid reliability on systems with high solar and wind penetration

target_users.value: System operators at ISOs and balancing authorities monitoring grid frequency and dispatching ancillary services in real time
target_users.rationale: Primary users acting on model alerts to procure regulation and spinning reserves before predicted frequency excursions

project_class.rationale: Core function is ML inference pipeline for predictive alerts, not a traditional CRUD application

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, analytics, devops, realtime
capabilities.rationale: FastAPI inference API, TimescaleDB for frequency time-series, LLM-assisted alert explanation generation, MLflow model registry, real-time PMU data ingestion, CI/CD model promotion pipeline

complexity_level.rationale: Real-time PMU stream processing, multi-feature ML inference (PMU frequency, generation dispatch, renewable output forecasts), automated model retraining and canary promotion

risk_level.rationale: False positive alerts waste operator time and erode trust; missed frequency excursions risk NERC BAL reliability violations; model data includes CIP-sensitive grid state

data_sensitivity.rationale: PMU frequency readings, generation dispatch schedules, and ancillary service awards constitute CIP-sensitive operational data per NERC CIP-011

external_exposure.rationale: System runs on ISO internal network; inference API accessible only to internal EMS systems and operator workstations

access_model.value: Custom JWT with roles: model_inference (read predictions and alerts), model_admin (trigger retraining, promote model versions), ems_integration (machine-to-machine for EMS alert ingestion)
access_model.rationale: Role separation between EMS integration service account and human model administrators

feature_scope.value: Real-time PMU frequency stream ingestion, 1-4 hour ahead frequency deviation probability forecasts, alert generation when excursion probability exceeds configurable threshold, LLM-generated natural language alert explanations, MLflow model registry with canary promotion, nightly model retraining pipeline, performance regression gating, operator alert dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full model lifecycle from real-time inference to automated retraining with operator-facing alerts

mvp_scope.value: 1-hour ahead frequency deviation prediction, PMU stream ingestion via Kafka, alert generation to EMS via REST webhook, MLflow model registry, nightly retraining job with accuracy gate, FastAPI inference API
mvp_scope.rationale: 1-hour ahead prediction is highest operator utility; 2-4 hour horizon in phase 2

security_baseline.value: Custom JWT for API access, TLS 1.3 for all internal connections, model artifacts encrypted at rest in S3, PMU stream data encrypted in Kafka with TLS, CIP-011 data access logging for all model training data access
security_baseline.rationale: CIP-011 requires protection of bulk electric system operational data used in model training

frontend_stack.value: None for MVP; operator alert view via EMS dashboard integration; internal MLflow UI for model tracking
frontend_stack.rationale: No standalone frontend; operator alerts delivered via existing EMS system; MLflow provides model management UI

backend_stack.rationale: Python ML ecosystem (scikit-learn, PyTorch, pandas); FastAPI for async inference API

data_platform.value: TimescaleDB for frequency and generation time-series, Redis 7 for feature cache, S3 for model artifacts and training datasets
data_platform.rationale: TimescaleDB continuous aggregates for feature engineering on PMU time-series; Redis for sub-second feature vector retrieval at inference time

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 within ISO internal VPC
hosting_target.rationale: ECS Fargate for managed containers within ISO's existing AWS VPC; no internet egress

privacy_retention_policy.value: PMU frequency readings retained 2 years in TimescaleDB hot tier, 5 years in S3 cold tier; model training datasets retained for duration of model in production plus 1 year; model artifacts retained 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: CIP-011 operational data retention and model auditability requirements

future_scope.value: 2-4 hour prediction horizon, renewable output forecast integration, ancillary service cost optimization recommendations, multi-balancing-authority federated model
future_scope.rationale: Extending prediction horizon and adding cost optimization in phase 2

constraints.value: Inference latency <200ms for 1-hour ahead prediction; false positive rate <15% at 90% recall; model retraining must complete within 3-hour window (02:00-05:00 UTC) to avoid daytime grid operation interference; no public internet egress
constraints.rationale: Operator trust requires low false positive rate; nightly retraining must not impact daytime production inference

observability_baseline.value: MLflow for experiment tracking and model registry, Grafana + Prometheus for inference latency and alert rate metrics, structured logging for all inference events and model promotion decisions
observability_baseline.rationale: ML system observability requires both infrastructure metrics and model performance metrics

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints with dedicated model evaluation review before production promotion
execution_preference.rationale: Model performance reviews require domain expert (grid operator) participation before production promotion

compliance_context.value: NERC BAL-001 and BAL-002 reliability standards (frequency response and balancing); NERC CIP-011 (information protection for BES operational data); model predictions that inform operator actions must have documented uncertainty quantification per NERC BAL guidance
compliance_context.rationale: Frequency deviation prediction system influences real-time grid balancing decisions subject to NERC BAL standards

llm_integration.value: GPT-4o via Azure OpenAI (internal ISO Azure tenant) for generating natural language alert explanations: 'Frequency deviation probability 78% in 1-hour window based on declining inertia from wind dispatch reduction in Zone B and forecasted solar ramp in Zone C.' LLM output is advisory only - not used in prediction decision path
llm_integration.rationale: System operators need concise natural language explanations of model alerts to act confidently; LLM generates these from structured feature attribution data

project.goal: ML-based 1-4 hour ahead grid frequency deviation prediction with real-time alert delivery to EMS
project.target_users: System operators at ISO balancing authority

backend.framework: FastAPI for inference API, Celery + Redis for retraining job queue

security.encryption: TLS 1.3 internal, S3 SSE-KMS for model artifacts

data.timeseries: TimescaleDB for PMU frequency_readings, generation_schedules, ancillary_service_awards
data.model_store: S3 for MLflow artifacts, training datasets

open_questions.llm_model: GPT-4o confirmed via Azure OpenAI internal tenant

confirmed_decisions.ml_framework: PyTorch LSTM for frequency sequence modeling

reasoner_reviews.product: The frequency deviation prediction system has a clear operator value proposition: reducing real-time balancing costs by shifting ancillary service procurement to the day-ahead or hour-ahead window when prices are lower. The 15% false positive rate constraint at 90% recall is a realistic target for this domain. The LLM-generated natural language explanations are a differentiating feature for operator adoption - system operators are more likely to act on alerts they can understand without consulting a data scientist.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The model architecture choice of PyTorch LSTM for frequency sequence modeling is reasonable but the round 2 plan left an unresolved ambiguity in feature engineering: the inference pipeline fetches feature vectors from Redis but the feature computation logic that populates Redis is not specified. The continuous aggregate pipeline from PMU -> TimescaleDB -> Redis feature cache must be explicitly documented. Additionally, the LLM alert explanation call path adds ~800ms latency if synchronous - this must be async and decoupled from the 200ms inference SLA.
reasoner_reviews.security: CIP-011 requires that access to BES operational data used for model training be logged. Rounds 1 and 2 specified logging at the FastAPI inference API level but did not address logging of training data access during the nightly retraining job. The Spark/Pandas data loading step in retraining reads PMU records from TimescaleDB and generation schedules - each training run must log the data access event with row count and date range to the CIP_data_access_log.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The LLM explanation generation must be decoupled from the synchronous inference path. Round 2 made this async but the alert delivery to EMS still blocks on LLM completion before sending the webhook. The webhook should deliver the alert immediately with excursion_probability and a placeholder explanation, and patch the explanation in when the LLM completes.

backend.service_design: Two FastAPI services: inference-api (real-time prediction and alert dispatch) and retraining-api (MLflow-integrated job trigger and model promotion). Celery worker pool (3 workers) for LLM explanation jobs and async export tasks. Feature hydration service populates Redis feature vectors from TimescaleDB continuous aggregates every 5 minutes.
backend.llm_integration_design: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via ISO internal Azure tenant. Prompt template: system role 'You are a grid reliability assistant. Explain frequency deviation alerts concisely for system operators.' User message contains structured SHAP feature attribution: top 5 features with their contribution values. Output is 2-3 sentence natural language explanation. LLM call dispatched async via Celery after alert is generated; EMS webhook patched with explanation via PUT /v1/alerts/{id}/explanation when LLM response arrives. Fallback: if LLM times out (>5s), explanation field contains 'Explanation pending - review feature attribution data.'
backend.background_jobs: Nightly retraining CronJob (02:00-05:00 UTC): extract 90 days of labeled frequency events from TimescaleDB, compute feature vectors (rolling mean/std of PMU frequency 5/15/30 min windows, generation dispatch delta, renewable output ramp rate), train LSTM model, evaluate on holdout test set (90-day sliding window), compare F1 score and false positive rate against production model, log to MLflow. Promotion gate: if new model F1 >= production F1 - 0.02 AND false_positive_rate <= 0.15, register as 'candidate' in MLflow; human approval required before 'production' stage.
backend.failure_handling: Inference-api: if Redis feature cache miss (stale), fall back to TimescaleDB direct query with 500ms timeout; if TimescaleDB unavailable, return 503 with Retry-After: 30. Retraining: if training exceeds 4-hour window, terminate Celery task and alert on-call; retain previous production model unchanged.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT HS256 with 12-hour expiry. Roles in JWT claims: model_inference, model_admin, ems_integration. Service accounts for EMS integration use non-expiring API tokens with separate HMAC signing.
security.authorization_model: inference API endpoints require model_inference or ems_integration role. Model promotion endpoints require model_admin role with additional human confirmation step (OTP or manager approval flag). CIP data access: only retraining service account and model_admin can read raw PMU records from TimescaleDB.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: cip_data_access_log (id, service_name TEXT, user_id TEXT, data_type ENUM pmu_readings/generation_schedules/ancillary_awards, date_range_start DATE, date_range_end DATE, record_count INT, access_reason TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMP). Written by inference-api (feature reads), retraining job (training data reads). Retained 3 years.

data.entities: frequency_readings (time TIMESTAMPTZ, pmu_id TEXT, frequency_hz REAL, rocof_hz_per_s REAL, voltage_pu REAL) - TimescaleDB hypertable, 5-second resolution. generation_schedules (time TIMESTAMPTZ, unit_id TEXT, scheduled_mw REAL, fuel_type TEXT, is_intermittent BOOL). ancillary_service_awards (time TIMESTAMPTZ, service_type ENUM regulation_up/regulation_down/spinning/non_spinning, awarded_mw REAL, market_price REAL). model_versions (id UUID, mlflow_run_id TEXT, model_stage ENUM candidate/production/archived, f1_score REAL, false_positive_rate REAL, promoted_at TIMESTAMP, promoted_by TEXT). prediction_alerts (id UUID, model_version_id FK, generated_at TIMESTAMP, horizon_hours INT, excursion_probability REAL, threshold_crossed REAL, feature_attribution_json JSONB, llm_explanation TEXT, delivered_to_ems BOOL, ems_ack_at TIMESTAMP). renewable_output_forecasts (time TIMESTAMPTZ, zone TEXT, forecast_mw REAL, source TEXT).
data.storage_design: TimescaleDB hypertables with 1-day chunks for frequency_readings (5-second data: ~17k rows/day per PMU, estimated 50 PMUs -> 850k rows/day). Continuous aggregates: ca_freq_5min (5-min averages for feature computation), ca_freq_15min, ca_rocof_5min. Redis feature cache: key pattern pmu_features:{pmu_id}:{window_minutes} -> JSON feature vector, TTL 6 minutes (refreshed every 5 min by feature hydration job).
data.data_risks: PMU data gaps during communication outages create feature vector nulls; model must handle missing features via last-observation-carried-forward with staleness flag. Training data imbalance: frequency excursion events are rare (~0.5% of 5-minute intervals); SMOTE oversampling or class-weighted loss required.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate in ISO VPC us-east-1: inference-api service (2 tasks, HPA on CPU 60%), retraining-api + Celery workers (3 tasks, only active 02:00-05:00 UTC via scheduled scaling), feature-hydration-worker (1 task, always-on). MLflow tracking server on ECS with S3 artifact store. TimescaleDB on RDS PostgreSQL 16 with TimescaleDB extension. Redis on ElastiCache. No internet egress - NAT gateway restricted to ISO internal CIDR.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest (unit tests for feature engineering and alert threshold logic), mypy type checking, Docker build push to ECR, ECS rolling deployment. Model promotion pipeline separate from code CI: MLflow model evaluation job on retraining completion; human approval via Slack integration before ECS task definition update to new model version.
devops.rollback_strategy: MLflow model stage rollback: if production model shows false_positive_rate >0.20 in 24-hour window post-promotion, retraining-api auto-demotes to previous production version via MLflow API and pages model_admin on-call.

ISS-001.title: LLM explanation blocks synchronous EMS alert webhook delivery
ISS-001.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 made LLM call async via Celery but the EMS webhook dispatch still blocked on LLM completion. EMS alert delivery SLA requires webhook within 30 seconds of excursion probability threshold crossing. LLM can take 3-8 seconds; alert must be delivered without waiting.

history.note: LLM was synchronous in inference path
Celery async added but webhook still blocked on LLM
Webhook delivers immediately; LLM explanation patched in via PUT /v1/alerts/{id}/explanation

ISS-002.title: Retraining job training data access not logged for CIP-011 compliance
ISS-002.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 logged inference-time feature reads but the nightly retraining job reads raw PMU and generation schedule data from TimescaleDB without logging to cip_data_access_log. CIP-011 requires all access to BES operational data to be logged.

history.note: Not identified
Raised by security reviewer

ISS-003.title: Feature hydration pipeline not specified - Redis feature cache population undefined
ISS-003.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 assumed Redis feature cache was populated but did not specify the feature hydration service architecture. Feature hydration service now specified as always-on ECS task refreshing TimescaleDB continuous aggregates into Redis every 5 minutes.

history.note: Raised during architect review
Feature hydration service specified as separate ECS task
