best_plan.title: Grid Frequency Deviation Prediction AI System - Round 2 Architecture
best_plan.executive_summary: Python 3.12 FastAPI ML inference system with async LLM explanations. Round 2 best plan pending final CIP-011 logging fix.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision for the grid frequency deviation AI system. Only ISS-002 remains: the CIP-011 audit log in the nightly retraining job is missing record_count and date_range_end fields. The plan_quality is 'flawed', so I will address ISS-002 but introduce a residual flaw: the devops_modernization theme implies I should focus on CI/CD modernization, but the plan inappropriately conflates the model promotion CI/CD pipeline with a generic code deployment pipeline, obscuring the model-specific evaluation gates. I will also leave a subtle flaw in the LLM integration: the plan will incorrectly describe the LLM as part of the 'prediction decision path' in one section while claiming it is 'advisory only' elsewhere, creating an internal inconsistency.

fix_report.action_taken: Added cip_data_access_log write to the retraining job data extraction step. The extraction function now runs SELECT COUNT(*) on the training dataset query before loading to Pandas, records record_count in the log entry, and populates date_range_start and date_range_end from the 90-day lookback window. Log entry written after successful extraction and before model training begins.
fix_report.expected_outcome: All retraining data access events logged with complete fields (service_name, data_type, date_range_start, date_range_end, record_count) satisfying CIP-011 audit evidence requirements

target_output.title: Grid Frequency Deviation Prediction AI System - Revised Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 / FastAPI / PyTorch inference system predicting 1-4 hour ahead grid frequency deviation probability on high-renewable transmission systems, delivering alerts to the ISO EMS for proactive ancillary service procurement. PMU frequency streams and generation dispatch data ingested via Kafka into TimescaleDB hypertables; inference-time feature vectors served from Redis continuous aggregate cache. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o generates natural language alert explanations asynchronously via Celery. MLflow manages model lifecycle with automated performance gating and human approval for production promotion. Deployed on ECS Fargate in ISO internal VPC. Revision 3 addresses ISS-002: complete CIP-011 audit logging for retraining data access.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three ECS Fargate services form the runtime: inference-api (FastAPI, 2 tasks) serves 1-hour ahead frequency deviation probability predictions in <200ms by reading pre-computed feature vectors from Redis; feature-hydration-worker (1 task) refreshes TimescaleDB continuous aggregates into Redis every 5 minutes; retraining-api + Celery workers (3 tasks, active 02:00-05:00 UTC only via scheduled scaling) execute nightly model retraining, evaluation, and MLflow registration. Inference flow: PMU frequency_readings stream via Kafka -> TimescaleDB hypertable (5-second resolution) -> TimescaleDB continuous aggregates (5/15/30 min rolling stats) -> Redis feature vectors (TTL 6 min) -> PyTorch LSTM inference -> prediction_alerts table -> immediate EMS webhook -> async Celery LLM explanation job -> PUT /v1/alerts/{id}/explanation patch. NOTE: in the devops pipeline documentation below, the model promotion CI/CD is incorrectly merged with the code deployment pipeline steps, which may confuse model evaluation gating with standard test-and-deploy flows.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Celery 5.3, PyTorch 2.2 (LSTM model), scikit-learn 1.4 (SHAP for feature attribution), Pandas 2.2. Data: TimescaleDB 2.14 on RDS PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7 ElastiCache (AOF), S3 for MLflow artifacts and training datasets. LLM: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via ISO Azure tenant (openai Python SDK). ML Tracking: MLflow 2.11 with S3 artifact store. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate us-east-1, MSK Kafka (3 brokers), ECR, GitHub Actions. Observability: MLflow experiment tracking, Grafana + Prometheus, structured logging.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Real-Time Inference: 200ms prediction of 1-hour ahead frequency deviation probability from Redis-cached PMU feature vectors via LSTM model. Alert Generation: threshold-based alert creation when excursion_probability exceeds configurable threshold (default 0.70); immediate EMS webhook delivery. LLM Explanation: async GPT-4o generates 2-3 sentence natural language explanation from SHAP feature attribution (top 5 contributors); patched into alert via PUT endpoint. Feature Hydration: TimescaleDB continuous aggregates (5/15/30 min rolling mean/std of frequency, ROCOF, generation dispatch delta) refreshed to Redis every 5 minutes. Model Retraining Pipeline: nightly LSTM retrain on 90-day labeled frequency events, SMOTE oversampling for class imbalance, MLflow experiment logging, automated F1/false-positive-rate evaluation, candidate model registration pending human approval. Model Promotion Gate: human approval via Slack integration required before MLflow stage update to 'production' and ECS task definition update.
target_output.system_components: inference-api (FastAPI, ECS 2 tasks): /v1/predict, /v1/alerts, /v1/alerts/{id}/explanation PATCH, JWT auth middleware, Redis feature reader, PyTorch LSTM inference (model loaded from MLflow production stage at startup), prediction_alerts writer, EMS webhook dispatcher. feature-hydration-worker (Python, ECS 1 task): polls TimescaleDB continuous aggregates every 5 minutes, writes feature vectors to Redis with 6-minute TTL. retraining-api (FastAPI + Celery 3 workers, ECS scheduled 02:00-05:00 UTC): training data extractor (TimescaleDB -> Pandas), CIP data access logger, LSTM trainer, MLflow experiment logger, performance evaluator (F1, false positive rate), MLflow model registrar. Kafka (MSK 3 brokers): pmu-frequency-topic (5-second PMU readings from substation concentrators). TimescaleDB: frequency_readings, generation_schedules, ancillary_service_awards hypertables with continuous aggregates. MLflow: experiment tracking, model registry (staging -> candidate -> production -> archived). Redis: feature vector cache, inference latency counters.
target_output.workflows: Real-Time Alert Workflow: PMU concentrator publishes 5-second frequency readings to Kafka pmu-frequency-topic. Kafka consumer in feature-hydration-worker writes to TimescaleDB frequency_readings hypertable. Every 5 minutes, hydration worker computes rolling statistics (5/15/30 min mean frequency, std dev, ROCOF trend) via TimescaleDB continuous aggregate queries, serializes to JSON feature vectors, writes to Redis (key: pmu_features:{pmu_id}:{window}, TTL 360s). Inference-api receives GET /v1/predict?horizon_hours=1 from EMS polling client (every 60 seconds). Retrieves feature vectors from Redis for configured PMU set (fallback to TimescaleDB direct query on cache miss). Runs PyTorch LSTM forward pass (batch size 1, seq_len 12 representing 60-min history). Returns {excursion_probability: 0.83, threshold: 0.70, alert_id: uuid}. If excursion_probability >= 0.70: creates prediction_alerts record, immediately dispatches EMS webhook with {alert_id, excursion_probability, feature_summary, explanation: null}, enqueues Celery LLM task. Celery worker constructs SHAP attribution from top 5 feature contributions, calls Azure OpenAI GPT-4o with structured prompt, receives 2-3 sentence explanation, calls PUT /v1/alerts/{alert_id}/explanation to patch alert record and send follow-up EMS webhook with explanation populated. Nightly Retraining Workflow: ECS Scheduled Task at 02:00 UTC triggers retraining-api. Extraction step: queries TimescaleDB for 90-day window of frequency_readings, generation_schedules, and labeled frequency events (excursion_flag set by retrospective labeling job). Logs to cip_data_access_log: {service_name: 'retraining-job', data_type: 'pmu_readings', date_range_start: T-90d, date_range_end: T-0d, record_count: COUNT from query, access_reason: 'nightly_model_retraining'}. Runs SMOTE to address class imbalance (event rate ~0.5%). Trains LSTM for 50 epochs with early stopping. Evaluates on holdout (last 14 days): computes F1, recall, false positive rate. Logs run to MLflow. If F1 >= production_F1 - 0.02 AND fpr <= 0.15: registers as 'candidate' in MLflow, sends Slack notification to model_admin for approval.
target_output.data_model: frequency_readings (time TIMESTAMPTZ, pmu_id TEXT, frequency_hz REAL, rocof_hz_per_s REAL, voltage_pu REAL) - TimescaleDB hypertable, chunk_interval 1 day. generation_schedules (time TIMESTAMPTZ, unit_id TEXT, scheduled_mw REAL, fuel_type TEXT, is_intermittent BOOL) - hypertable. ancillary_service_awards (time TIMESTAMPTZ, service_type ENUM regulation_up/regulation_down/spinning/non_spinning, awarded_mw REAL, market_price REAL) - hypertable. model_versions (id UUID, mlflow_run_id TEXT, mlflow_model_name TEXT, model_stage ENUM candidate/production/archived, f1_score REAL, false_positive_rate REAL, recall REAL, promoted_at TIMESTAMP, promoted_by TEXT). prediction_alerts (id UUID, model_version_id FK, generated_at TIMESTAMP, horizon_hours INT, excursion_probability REAL, threshold_crossed REAL, feature_attribution_json JSONB, llm_explanation TEXT, delivered_to_ems BOOL, ems_ack_at TIMESTAMP). renewable_output_forecasts (time TIMESTAMPTZ, zone TEXT, forecast_mw REAL, source TEXT). cip_data_access_log (id UUID, service_name TEXT, user_id TEXT, data_type ENUM pmu_readings/generation_schedules/ancillary_awards, date_range_start DATE, date_range_end DATE, record_count INT, access_reason TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL, retained 3 years.
target_output.api_design: Internal REST API on ISO VPC. POST /v1/predict {pmu_ids: [...], horizon_hours: 1} returns {alert_id, excursion_probability, threshold, feature_summary}. GET /v1/alerts?from=&to=&delivered=false returns pending alerts for EMS polling. PUT /v1/alerts/{id}/explanation {explanation: '...', shap_attribution: {...}} patches alert record. GET /v1/models/production returns current production model version, F1 score, and last promotion event. POST /v1/models/{run_id}/promote triggers MLflow stage update to 'production' (requires model_admin JWT + OTP). GET /v1/health returns inference latency p95 and Redis cache hit rate. All endpoints require JWT; ems_integration service accounts use HMAC API tokens.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT HS256 with 12-hour expiry; ems_integration service accounts use HMAC-signed non-expiring API tokens. TLS 1.3 for all internal VPC connections. S3 model artifacts encrypted with SSE-KMS. CIP-011 data access logging: cip_data_access_log written by inference-api for each feature vector read (data_type: pmu_readings, record_count: 1 per feature set) and by retraining job data extraction step with full record_count and date_range fields (ISS-002 resolved). Retraining job CIP log entry: written after COUNT query, before Pandas load, includes data_type pmu_readings and generation_schedules as separate entries with respective record counts. NERC BAL compliance: prediction_alerts table retains all alert events with model version and feature attribution for operator decision audit. NOTE: The LLM alert explanation is described as 'advisory only' in contract but is inadvertently referenced as part of the prediction decision path in the devops CI/CD section - this is an inconsistency in the plan. Model promotion requires human approval (model_admin role + OTP via Slack) before ECS task definition update to new model artifact.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate us-east-1 within ISO VPC. inference-api: 2 tasks (HPA 2-6 on CPU 60%). feature-hydration-worker: 1 task always-on. retraining-api + Celery: 3 tasks scheduled scaling (scale to 0 at 05:30 UTC, scale to 3 at 01:45 UTC). MLflow tracking server: 1 ECS task with RDS PostgreSQL backend and S3 artifact store. MSK Kafka: 3 brokers, pmu-frequency-topic 12 partitions. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: pytest (unit tests for feature engineering, alert threshold logic, LSTM training step mocks), mypy type checking, Docker build and push to ECR, ECS rolling deployment (maxSurge 1). Model promotion is merged into the same GitHub Actions workflow as code deployment - this conflates model evaluation gating with standard code CI/CD: after ECS deployment, the pipeline checks MLflow for any candidate models awaiting promotion and sends a Slack approval request if found, incorrectly treating model promotion as a post-deploy step rather than a separate governed process. Rollback: ECS task definition rollback to previous revision via AWS CodeDeploy on error_rate >2% in 10-minute window post-deploy. MLflow model rollback: retraining-api auto-demotes production model if false_positive_rate >0.20 in 24-hour post-promotion window.
target_output.observability: MLflow experiment tracking: every retraining run logs F1, recall, false_positive_rate, training_duration_minutes, sample_count to MLflow UI. Prometheus metrics from inference-api: prediction_latency_ms histogram (target p95 <200ms), alert_dispatch_rate, redis_cache_hit_ratio, llm_explanation_latency_ms. Grafana dashboards: inference latency p50/p95/p99, alert rate per hour, false positive rate (7-day rolling), Redis cache hit rate, feature hydration freshness (lag between PMU timestamp and Redis update). PagerDuty: inference latency p95 >200ms for 5 minutes (P2), feature hydration lag >10 minutes (P1), retraining job not completed by 05:00 UTC (P2), production model false_positive_rate >0.20 in 24-hour window (P1).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1800/month on ECS Fargate: inference-api 2-6 tasks ($400), feature-hydration-worker ($80), retraining tasks 3 hours/night ($120), MLflow server ($80), MSK 3 brokers ($600), RDS TimescaleDB ($350), ElastiCache Redis ($80), S3 ($20), Azure OpenAI GPT-4o (~$0.15/alert at 1000 alerts/month = $150). TimescaleDB storage: 50 PMUs at 5-second resolution = 850k rows/day, ~300M rows/year, ~15GB compressed with TimescaleDB columnar compression. Inference SLA: PyTorch LSTM forward pass on CPU (no GPU for Fargate) benchmarked at 15ms for seq_len=12; feature vector Redis read <5ms; p95 total <50ms well within 200ms SLA.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 months): Kafka PMU ingestion, TimescaleDB hypertable and continuous aggregates, Redis feature hydration every 5 minutes, PyTorch LSTM 1-hour ahead prediction, threshold-based alert generation with EMS webhook, async GPT-4o alert explanations via Celery, MLflow model registry, nightly retraining with F1 gate, human approval Slack integration, CIP-011 cip_data_access_log for inference and retraining, ECS Fargate deployment in ISO VPC, Grafana inference and retraining dashboards. Phase 2 (months 7-12): 2-4 hour prediction horizon (extend LSTM lookback window to 4 hours), renewable output forecast integration as additional feature, ancillary service cost optimization recommendation alongside probability alert, SHAP explanation improvement with zone-level attribution. Phase 3 (months 13-18): Multi-balancing-authority federated model (separate model instances per BA with shared MLflow registry), real-time model performance monitoring with automatic retraining trigger on F1 degradation, integration with ancillary service bidding system for automated procurement suggestions.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints throughout; mypy strict mode. Pydantic v2 for all API request/response models and feature vector schemas. PyTorch model loaded once at inference-api startup from MLflow production stage artifact; hot-reloading on model promotion event via signal. Table-driven pytest for LSTM feature engineering (rolling window calculations validated against reference NumPy implementations). CIP-011 logging tested with mock TimescaleDB to verify record_count populated correctly. No direct TimescaleDB queries outside of dedicated data_access layer - all reads must write to cip_data_access_log. MLflow run IDs linked to model_versions table records. ADR log in /docs/adr.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: false positive rate exceeds 15% after phase 2 prediction horizon extension - longer horizons have inherently lower precision. Mitigation: maintain separate model instances per horizon; evaluate each against its own recall/FPR targets. Risk: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o rate limits or unavailability delay alert explanations. Mitigation: fallback explanation text served immediately; LLM explanation is advisory only and does not block operator decision. Risk: TimescaleDB chunk exclusion failure on wide date-range queries during retraining causing full table scan - validate query plans in staging. Tradeoff: CPU-only LSTM inference on Fargate vs. GPU - CPU is sufficient at 15ms for seq_len=12 at current PMU count; GPU not justified until PMU count exceeds 500. FLAW: The devops pipeline incorrectly conflates model promotion governance with code CI/CD deployment, treating model evaluation as a post-deploy step rather than a separately governed process with independent audit trail - this misrepresents the model governance architecture.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: CIP-011 training data logging resolved: retraining job extraction step writes complete cip_data_access_log entry with record_count from pre-load COUNT query and date_range fields. Feature hydration architecture resolved: dedicated always-on ECS task with 5-minute refresh cycle and Redis 6-minute TTL. LLM decoupled from inference SLA: immediate EMS webhook with async explanation patch-in. Class imbalance addressed with SMOTE oversampling (event rate 0.5%).

metadata.notes: Round 3 of grid frequency deviation AI system. ISS-002 CIP-011 logging addressed but plan introduces flaws: model promotion governance conflated with code CI/CD pipeline; LLM described as both advisory-only and part of prediction decision path in different sections.

profile.domain: Energy Procurement — Power Purchase Agreement Management

project_goal.value: Enable commercial and industrial energy buyers to submit PPA procurement requests, compare virtual PPA term sheets from multiple renewable energy developers, and manage contract execution workflows with legal and finance sign-off through a structured self-service portal.
project_goal.rationale: Replaces email-based PPA procurement process averaging 14 weeks from RFP to signed contract due to disorganized document exchange.

target_users.value: Corporate sustainability managers and energy procurement leads at Fortune 1000 C&I buyers; renewable energy project developers submitting term sheets; internal admin staff managing platform operations.
target_users.rationale: Three distinct user roles identified in product discovery with different workflows and data access needs.

project_class.rationale: Multi-role web application with distinct buyer and developer portals backed by a structured API.

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, public_api, integrations, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full portal capabilities including public API for utility planning system integration and admin panel for platform operations.

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-party contract execution workflows with legal and credit approval routing, e-signature integration, and FERC bilateral contract compliance requirements.

risk_level.rationale: Platform handles confidential PPA contract terms, MW capacity pricing, and corporate financial exposure data requiring strict access controls.

data_sensitivity.rationale: PPA term sheets contain confidential energy pricing, MW commitment volumes, and corporate financial exposure that must be isolated between buyers and developers.

external_exposure.rationale: Login required for all portal access; public API uses API key authentication for utility system integrations.

access_model.value: RBAC with four roles: buyer (views own requests and term sheets), developer (responds to eligible requests, submits term sheets), legal_reviewer (accesses contract drafts for assigned requests), admin (platform-wide access). Auth0 with organization-scoped tokens. Developer must never see another developer term sheet pricing for the same request.
access_model.rationale: Role isolation is critical — developer A must never see developer B term sheet pricing.

feature_scope.value: PPA Request submission with MW capacity, term length, delivery region, and technology preference; Developer Term Sheet response portal with structured pricing fields; Side-by-side term sheet comparison for buyers; Contract execution workflow with legal review, credit approval, and DocuSign e-signature; Webhook notifications to buyer ERP and utility planning systems; Admin panel for user provisioning and request oversight.
feature_scope.rationale: Core features covering the full PPA procurement lifecycle.

mvp_scope.value: PPA request submission, developer term sheet response, buyer comparison view, and contract initiation with DocuSign integration. Webhook delivery as post-MVP.
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP delivers the end-to-end procurement workflow without the integration layer.

security_baseline.value: Auth0 RBAC with MFA for all roles, TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 encryption at rest for contract documents on S3, row-level data isolation enforced at the API layer, WAF on ALB, audit logging for all contract term access events.
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data sensitivity requires defense-in-depth with audit trails for every access to confidential pricing data.

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise and component ecosystem alignment with Auth0 React SDK.

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async performance fits the webhook delivery and document processing workloads; team Python expertise.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS with S3 for contract documents
data_platform.rationale: Relational model required for multi-party contract workflows; S3 for document binary storage.

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container hosting with auto-scaling; data residency in us-east-1 for FERC compliance.

privacy_retention_policy.value: PPA contract documents retained for 7 years per FERC recordkeeping requirements. Audit logs retained for 3 years. Soft-delete for withdrawn requests with 90-day purge.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FERC bilateral contract records must be retained for audit and dispute resolution.

future_scope.value: Automated FERC EQR (Electric Quarterly Report) filing generation from executed PPA records, carbon credit ledger integration, and secondary market transfer request workflows.
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP compliance automation identified as high-value by legal team.

constraints.value: Developer must never access another developer term sheet pricing for the same PPA request. Contract documents accessible only to the requesting buyer and responding developer parties. US-east-1 data residency.
constraints.rationale: Confidentiality obligations in platform terms of service and FERC market rules.

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM and structured logging. PagerDuty alerts for contract workflow failures and webhook delivery failures. Audit log exported to S3 for compliance review.
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate standard monitoring with audit trail export for FERC compliance.

execution_preference.value: 2-week Agile sprints. Feature flags for gradual rollout of new contract workflow steps.
execution_preference.rationale: Incremental delivery with controlled rollout for contract workflow changes.

compliance_context.value: FERC market rules for bilateral power contracts (Order 889, EQR filing requirements), SOC 2 Type II for financial data handling, contractual confidentiality obligations between platform parties.
compliance_context.rationale: FERC bilateral contract regulations apply; SOC 2 required by enterprise buyer procurement policies.

project.goal: PPA procurement portal for C&I buyers and renewable energy developers
project.target_users: Corporate sustainability managers, energy developers, legal reviewers

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for local UI

security.encryption: AES-256 at rest via S3 SSE-KMS, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.isolation: Row-level data isolation at API layer

constraints.isolation: developer term sheet pricing isolated between competitors

reasoner_reviews.product: PPA procurement lifecycle scope is well-defined. The side-by-side term sheet comparison view is the highest-value buyer feature for reducing decision time. The contract execution workflow with DocuSign integration requires a durable state machine with explicit transition events, not ad-hoc status fields.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with SQLAlchemy 2.0 is appropriate for this workflow-heavy application. The critical architectural gap identified in prior rounds is the contract execution workflow state machine: using a simple status ENUM on the contract_documents table is insufficient. A proper event-sourced transitions table is required. The second gap is term sheet developer isolation relying only on API-layer filtering without database-level enforcement.
reasoner_reviews.security: Row-level data isolation is the most important security control: developer A must never receive term sheet pricing from developer B for the same PPA request. This must be enforced at the PostgreSQL layer using RLS policies with organization_id scoping, not just at the API route level.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The FERC EQR filing requirement means contract documents must survive in a 7-year accessible archive. S3 Intelligent-Tiering with lifecycle to Glacier Deep Archive after 2 years meets this requirement at minimal cost.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with domain modules: ppa_requests, term_sheets, contract_execution (event-sourced workflow state machine), webhooks, admin.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful API versioned at /api/v1. POST /ppa-requests, GET /ppa-requests/{id}/term-sheets (buyer views developer responses scoped to own request), POST /ppa-requests/{id}/term-sheets (developer submits), POST /contracts (buyer initiates execution), GET /contracts/{id}/events (full workflow event history), POST /contracts/{id}/legal-approve, POST /contracts/{id}/credit-approve, GET /admin/requests.
backend.business_modules: ContractWorkflowService: implements state machine transitions (draft -> legal_review -> credit_approval -> e_signature -> executed) with transition event logging to contract_workflow_events table. TermSheetIsolationService: PostgreSQL RLS policy enforces organization_id scoping on term_sheets table. DocuSignAdapter: creates envelopes, sends for signature, receives webhook callbacks on completion.
backend.failure_handling: DocuSign webhook delivery failures retried with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts. Contract state machine transition failures rolled back via SQLAlchemy transaction. Webhook delivery to buyer ERP tracked in webhook_deliveries table with retry queue.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with Auth0 provider at root. Route-based code splitting: buyer portal (requests, comparison, contracts), developer portal (available requests, term sheet builder), admin panel.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Buyer: PPA Request Wizard (MW capacity, term, region, technology preference), Term Sheet Comparison (side-by-side developer offers), Contract Execution Tracker (workflow state timeline, DocuSign status). Developer: Request Browse (filtered by region and technology match), Term Sheet Builder (structured pricing fields). Admin: User Provisioning, Request Oversight Table.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all server state including contract workflow status polling. Zustand for multi-step wizard local state. Auth0 React SDK for token management and role-based route guards.

security.auth_design: Auth0 organizations feature for buyer and developer company isolation. MFA required for all roles on login.
security.authorization_model: RBAC with four roles: buyer, developer, legal_reviewer, admin. PostgreSQL RLS on term_sheets: developer_org_id = current_setting(app.current_org_id) OR buyer sees via ppa_request join. FastAPI middleware calls SET LOCAL app.current_org_id from Auth0 JWT org claim within each database session.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: FastAPI middleware logs every access to term_sheets.settlement_price_mwh and contract_documents fields with user Auth0 sub, timestamp, and ppa_request_id to audit_events table. Audit events exported nightly to S3 for FERC compliance review.

data.entities: ppa_requests, term_sheets (with RLS policy), contract_documents, contract_workflow_events (event-sourced, INSERT-only), approval_workflows.
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ for relational contract data with RLS enabled. S3 with SSE-KMS for contract document PDFs. ElastiCache Redis for Auth0 session cache.
data.retention_and_deletion: Contract documents retained 7 years per FERC recordkeeping. contract_workflow_events table INSERT-only — no UPDATE or DELETE privileges granted to application role.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster with API service (2-6 tasks) and webhook delivery worker (1-2 tasks) behind ALB with WAF. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ in private subnet.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest 90% coverage gate, mypy type checking, bandit security scan, Docker build, push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy. Alembic migrations run as ECS task before deploy.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on all FastAPI routes. PagerDuty escalation for contract workflow failures and DocuSign webhook delivery failures exceeding 3 retries.

ISS-001.title: Contract execution uses a simple status ENUM column — no durable state machine with transition history
ISS-001.detail: The contract_documents table uses a single status column to track the multi-step legal/credit/e-signature workflow. There is no transition event log and no way to reconstruct the approval history for FERC audit purposes.

ISS-002.title: Term sheet isolation relies solely on API-layer filtering — no database-level enforcement
ISS-002.detail: The data model does not enforce that a developer can only SELECT their own term sheets. The isolation relies entirely on the API route permission check. A SQLAlchemy query bug or a future raw query would expose competitor pricing.

focus_issues.title: Contract execution uses simple status ENUM — no durable state machine with transition history
focus_issues.detail: No transition event log or guard conditions on state transitions in the contract execution workflow.

focus_issues.title: Term sheet isolation relies solely on API-layer filtering
focus_issues.detail: No database-level enforcement of developer org isolation on term sheet queries.

latest_recommendations: Replace status ENUM on contract_documents with a contract_workflow_events table recording each transition with actor, timestamp, and prior/next state
Add PostgreSQL row-level security policy on term_sheets table enforcing organization_id = current_setting(app.current_org_id)
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: PPA Procurement Portal — Round 2

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity issues unresolved across two rounds; score plateau at 6.8

previous_audits.summary: Well-structured portal design but two architectural gaps in contract workflow state management and term sheet isolation create significant compliance and security risks.
strengths: Auth0 RBAC with four-role model is appropriate for multi-party PPA platform
DocuSign integration identified correctly as the e-signature mechanism
concerns: Contract execution modeled as a status ENUM with no transition history — insufficient for FERC audit requirements
Term sheet developer isolation relies only on API filter, not database-level enforcement
blocking_issues: Simple status column cannot reconstruct approval timeline for FERC compliance audit
API-only isolation is insufficient for financial data with competitor confidentiality obligations
recommendations: Introduce contract_workflow_events event log table for durable transition history
Implement PostgreSQL RLS on term_sheets table keyed on organization_id

issue_updates.title: Contract execution uses simple status ENUM
issue_updates.detail: No event log for transition history.

issue_updates.title: Term sheet isolation API-only
issue_updates.detail: No database-level enforcement of org isolation.

previous_audits.summary: Minor improvements in API design but both critical architectural issues remain unaddressed. Contract workflow state machine and term sheet database isolation are blocking.
strengths: OpenAPI specification improved with clearer response schemas
DocuSign webhook retry logic added to specialist subplan
concerns: ISS-001: contract_documents still uses status ENUM with no transition event table
ISS-002: no PostgreSQL RLS policy or equivalent database enforcement on term_sheets
blocking_issues: Both ISS-001 and ISS-002 remain unresolved — platform is architecturally flawed for financial data sensitivity
recommendations: Add contract_workflow_events table with event sourcing pattern for full transition history
Implement PostgreSQL RLS on term_sheets enabling set_config() to pass authenticated org context

issue_updates.title: Contract execution uses simple status ENUM
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 plan still uses status column with no event log.

issue_updates.title: Term sheet isolation API-only
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 plan does not mention PostgreSQL RLS or equivalent database control.

previous_plan.title: PPA Procurement Portal — Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: React 18 and FastAPI portal for commercial energy buyers to submit PPA procurement requests, compare term sheets from renewable developers, and execute contracts via DocuSign — with Auth0 RBAC and API-level term sheet isolation.
previous_plan.data_model: ppa_requests, term_sheets with status ENUM, contract_documents with status ENUM (draft/legal_review/credit_approval/e_signature/executed), approval_workflows.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Auth0 RBAC, MFA, TLS 1.3, S3 SSE-KMS, audit logging middleware for term sheet pricing access.
previous_plan.risks_and_tradeoffs: ISS-001: contract status ENUM lacks transition event history for FERC audit. ISS-002: term sheet developer isolation relies on API filter only — no database enforcement.

best_plan.title: PPA Procurement Portal — Round 2
best_plan.executive_summary: React 18 and FastAPI portal for commercial energy buyers to submit PPA procurement requests, compare term sheets from renewable developers, and execute contracts via DocuSign — with Auth0 RBAC and API-level term sheet isolation.
best_plan.data_model: ppa_requests, term_sheets with status ENUM, contract_documents with status ENUM (draft/legal_review/credit_approval/e_signature/executed), approval_workflows.
best_plan.security_and_compliance: Auth0 RBAC, MFA, TLS 1.3, S3 SSE-KMS, audit logging middleware for term sheet pricing access.
best_plan.risks_and_tradeoffs: ISS-001: contract status ENUM lacks transition event history for FERC audit. ISS-002: term sheet developer isolation relies on API filter only — no database enforcement.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must resolve two high-severity architectural flaws persisting across rounds 1 and 2. ISS-001: replace the contract status ENUM with an event-sourced contract_workflow_events table recording each transition with actor, timestamp, and event_type — providing FERC-audit-required transition history. ISS-002: add a PostgreSQL row-level security policy on the term_sheets table using set_config() to pass authenticated organization context, making database-level isolation independent of application-layer filtering.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced the single status column on contract_documents with an event-sourced contract_workflow_events table (id UUID PK, contract_id FK, from_state, to_state, actor_user_id, actor_org_id, event_type ENUM initiated/legal_approved/legal_rejected/credit_approved/credit_rejected/signature_sent/executed/withdrawn, metadata JSONB, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ). The current contract state is derived by querying the latest event. ContractWorkflowService validates allowed transitions via a state machine guard table before inserting a new event. Alembic migration removes the status column and adds the events table as INSERT-only.
fix_report.expected_outcome: FERC-compliant full transition history available for any contract with immutable event records. Contract state reconstruction query available for audit reporting.

fix_report.action_taken: Added a PostgreSQL row-level security policy on the term_sheets table: CREATE POLICY term_sheet_org_isolation ON term_sheets USING (developer_org_id = current_setting(app.current_org_id)::uuid OR ppa_request_id IN (SELECT id FROM ppa_requests WHERE buyer_org_id = current_setting(app.current_org_id)::uuid)). FastAPI middleware calls SET LOCAL app.current_org_id within each database connection context using the Auth0 JWT organization claim. Platform admins connect via a superuser connection that bypasses RLS for oversight queries.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Database-level enforcement of term sheet confidentiality — SQLAlchemy query bugs cannot leak competitor pricing because PostgreSQL RLS enforces isolation before any row is returned.

target_output.title: PPA Procurement Portal
target_output.executive_summary: React 18 and FastAPI platform enabling commercial energy buyers to submit PPA procurement requests, compare renewable developer term sheets, and manage multi-party contract execution through an event-sourced workflow state machine with FERC-compliant audit history. PostgreSQL row-level security enforces developer term sheet confidentiality at the database layer, independent of application filtering. Deployed on AWS ECS Fargate with Auth0 RBAC, MFA, and DocuSign e-signature integration.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier web application: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront communicating over HTTPS to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate behind an ALB with WAF. PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ stores all contract relational data with row-level security policies enforcing developer org isolation on term_sheets. S3 with SSE-KMS stores contract document PDFs. ElastiCache Redis caches Auth0 session data. An ECS Fargate worker task handles webhook delivery to buyer ERP and utility planning systems with an SQS retry queue. Auth0 organizations provide company-level tenant isolation above the database RLS layer.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, Auth0 React SDK, React Router v6. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, pydantic v2, authlib for Auth0 JWT validation. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ with pgcrypto and row-level security. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7. Integrations: DocuSign eSignature SDK, SQS for webhook delivery queue. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, CloudFront, S3, Secrets Manager. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: PPA Request module: buyer submits procurement request specifying MW capacity, term years, delivery region, and technology preference; request made visible to eligible developers filtered by region and technology match. Term Sheet Response module: developer browses eligible requests and submits a structured term sheet with settlement_price_mwh, capacity_factor, contract_structure; RLS policy ensures developer sees only own submissions. Buyer Comparison module: buyer views all term sheets for own request side-by-side with pricing, capacity factor, and contract structure. Contract Execution module: event-sourced workflow from draft through legal_review, credit_approval, e_signature, to executed state; each transition creates an immutable contract_workflow_events record. Admin module: user provisioning via Auth0 Management API, request oversight, audit event S3 export.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront CDN for React SPA static assets. ALB with AWS WAF for API traffic. ECS Fargate API cluster (2-6 tasks, auto-scaling on CPU 60% target). ECS Fargate webhook worker (1-2 tasks, scales on SQS queue depth). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with RLS enabled. ElastiCache Redis 7 for session storage. S3 bucket (public access blocked, SSE-KMS) for contract documents. SQS standard queue for webhook delivery. DocuSign eSignature API for envelope creation and signature routing. Auth0 tenant with Organizations feature for buyer/developer company isolation.
target_output.workflows: PPA Request Lifecycle: Buyer logs in via Auth0, submits POST /api/v1/ppa-requests with capacity and region fields; system creates ppa_request in open status; eligible developers notified via email. Developer Term Sheet Submission: Developer browses GET /api/v1/ppa-requests (filtered by region/technology match), submits POST /api/v1/ppa-requests/{id}/term-sheets with pricing fields; RLS policy on term_sheets ensures settlement_price_mwh readable only by submitting developer and requesting buyer. Contract Execution: Buyer selects a term sheet and calls POST /api/v1/contracts; ContractWorkflowService validates transition from null to draft and inserts contract_workflow_events record with event_type=initiated; legal reviewer calls POST /api/v1/contracts/{id}/legal-approve inserting event_type=legal_approved; credit team approves inserting event_type=credit_approved; DocuSignAdapter creates envelope and sends for signature; DocuSign webhook callback triggers event_type=executed on completion.
target_output.data_model: ppa_requests (id UUID PK, buyer_org_id UUID FK organizations, mw_capacity DECIMAL(10,2), term_years SMALLINT, delivery_region VARCHAR, technology_preference ENUM solar/wind/storage/any, status ENUM open/under_review/closed, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). term_sheets (id UUID PK, ppa_request_id UUID FK, developer_org_id UUID FK, settlement_price_mwh DECIMAL(10,4), capacity_factor DECIMAL(4,3), contract_structure ENUM fixed/variable/cfd, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, selection_status ENUM submitted/selected/rejected; RLS policy: developer_org_id = current_setting(app.current_org_id) OR buyer sees via ppa_request join). contract_documents (id UUID PK, ppa_request_id UUID FK, term_sheet_id UUID FK, buyer_org_id UUID FK, developer_org_id UUID FK, docusign_envelope_id VARCHAR, s3_document_key VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). contract_workflow_events (id UUID PK, contract_id UUID FK, from_state VARCHAR NULLABLE, to_state VARCHAR NOT NULL, actor_user_id VARCHAR Auth0-sub, actor_org_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM initiated/legal_approved/legal_rejected/credit_approved/credit_rejected/signature_sent/executed/withdrawn, metadata JSONB, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL; INSERT-only — no UPDATE or DELETE privileges). approval_workflows (id UUID PK, contract_id UUID FK, step ENUM legal/credit, assigned_user_id VARCHAR, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 spec. POST /ppa-requests (buyer submits request, returns 201 with request_id). GET /ppa-requests (developer gets eligible list filtered by org region/technology match). POST /ppa-requests/{id}/term-sheets (developer submits term sheet, returns 201). GET /ppa-requests/{id}/term-sheets (buyer sees all term sheets for own request — RLS-scoped). POST /contracts (buyer initiates execution from selected term_sheet_id, returns 201 with contract_id and initial workflow event). GET /contracts/{id}/events (returns full contract_workflow_events history for audit). POST /contracts/{id}/legal-approve (legal reviewer approves, inserts event). POST /contracts/{id}/credit-approve (credit team approves, inserts event). POST /webhooks/docusign (DocuSign callback, validates HMAC signature). GET /admin/requests (admin overview, bypasses RLS via superuser connection).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 Organizations for buyer/developer company-level tenant isolation with org-scoped JWT claims. MFA enforced for all roles at Auth0 policy level. PostgreSQL row-level security on term_sheets: CREATE POLICY term_sheet_org_isolation ON term_sheets USING (developer_org_id = current_setting(app.current_org_id)::uuid OR ppa_request_id IN (SELECT id FROM ppa_requests WHERE buyer_org_id = current_setting(app.current_org_id)::uuid)). FastAPI middleware sets SET LOCAL app.current_org_id from Auth0 JWT org claim within each async database session. S3 SSE-KMS for contract documents. Audit middleware logs every access to term_sheets.settlement_price_mwh and contract_documents fields. FERC EQR recordkeeping: contract_workflow_events immutable append-only enforced via PostgreSQL privilege revocation. WAF on ALB with rate limiting and OWASP rule set.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: pytest with 90% coverage gate, mypy strict type checking, bandit security scan, Docker build and push to ECR, ECS rolling deployment with health check gate. Alembic migration (adding contract_workflow_events table, removing status column from contract_documents, enabling RLS on term_sheets) runs as ECS task before API service deploy. Environments: dev (single AZ), staging (Multi-AZ mirror), prod (Multi-AZ, ECS auto-scaling 2-6 tasks). Secrets: Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation for RDS credentials and DocuSign API keys.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM tracing on all FastAPI routes with ppa_request_id and contract_id as custom span tags. Structured JSON logs including org_id, user_id, and request_id correlation. Custom Datadog metrics: contract_workflow_transition_duration_seconds by transition step, term_sheet_submission_rate, docusign_webhook_delivery_lag_seconds. PagerDuty escalation for contract workflow transition failures and DocuSign webhook delivery failures after 5 retries. Audit events exported nightly to S3 for FERC compliance review.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ,800/month for 200 active PPA requests and 50 daily contract events. ECS API service auto-scales 2-6 tasks on CPU 60% target. Webhook worker scales 1-2 tasks on SQS queue depth. RDS Multi-AZ approximately /month; Redis ElastiCache /month. S3 contract document storage negligible at typical PPA document volumes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Auth0 RBAC with four roles and MFA enforcement, PPA request submission and developer term sheet response, PostgreSQL RLS on term_sheets, event-sourced contract_workflow_events replacing status ENUM, DocuSign envelope creation and signature routing, buyer comparison view, GitHub Actions CI with security gates. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Webhook delivery worker with SQS retry queue, admin panel for user provisioning and request oversight, audit event S3 export pipeline, feature flags for contract workflow step rollout. Phase 3 (4 weeks): FERC EQR filing generation from executed contract events, Datadog dashboard for contract lifecycle KPIs.
target_output.development_guidelines: All SQLAlchemy queries on term_sheets and contract_documents must be executed within a database session where SET LOCAL app.current_org_id has been called — enforced by a FastAPI dependency get_scoped_db_session() that sets the config before yielding the session. No raw SQL queries except for admin superuser routes with explicit comment justifying RLS bypass. contract_workflow_events table must never have UPDATE or DELETE privileges granted to the application role — verified in a CI migration lint step.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PostgreSQL RLS policy performance overhead on high-frequency term_sheet queries as request volume scales. Mitigation: EXPLAIN ANALYZE RLS-filtered queries during load testing; partial index on term_sheets(ppa_request_id) WHERE selection_status=submitted. Risk: DocuSign envelope creation fails mid-workflow leaving contract in signature_sent state without a completed envelope. Mitigation: DocuSign adapter checks for existing envelope before creating a new one using the idempotency key pattern. Tradeoff: event-sourced contract workflow over CQRS — simpler to implement at this scale but requires full event replay to reconstruct state for audit queries; mitigated by caching current_state derived from latest event per contract.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Contract workflow state machine implemented as event-sourced contract_workflow_events table with immutable append-only events — satisfies FERC audit trail requirement. Term sheet developer isolation enforced at PostgreSQL RLS layer using set_config() context propagation from Auth0 JWT org claim. DocuSign HMAC webhook signature validation confirmed as the envelope completion callback mechanism.

metadata.notes: PPA procurement portal — round 3 resolves two high-severity architectural flaws: event-sourced contract workflow replacing status ENUM, and PostgreSQL RLS for term sheet developer isolation.

profile.domain: Grid Protection Engineering - Protective Relay Settings Desktop Tool

project_goal.value: Desktop application for grid protection engineers to configure protective relay settings files, perform time-overcurrent coordination studies, and export validated settings packages for deployment to field relay hardware across transmission substations
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual spreadsheet-based coordination studies with a validated, version-controlled settings workflow that enforces IEEE C37.112 coordination requirements

target_users.value: Protection engineers and substation engineers at transmission utilities and engineering consultancies
target_users.rationale: Primary users performing relay coordination studies and settings validation before field deployment

project_class.rationale: Air-gapped workstation deployment required; offline capability and native file system access for relay settings files are mandatory

capabilities.rationale: Electron UI, embedded Python SciPy computation engine, SQLite local storage, TCC curve analytics

complexity_level.rationale: IEEE C37.112 TCC curve mathematics, multi-vendor relay format support (SEL, GE, ABB), version diffing, N-1 coordination validation

risk_level.rationale: Incorrectly configured relay settings can cause false trips or failure to clear faults, risking grid stability and equipment damage

data_sensitivity.rationale: Substation protection configurations are CIP-sensitive operational data per NERC CIP-011

external_exposure.rationale: Air-gapped workstation deployment; no network connectivity required for core functionality

access_model.value: Windows Active Directory with four roles: CIPProtectionReadOnly, CIPProtectionEditor, CIPProtectionApprover, CIPProtectionAdmin
access_model.rationale: AD integration leverages existing utility identity infrastructure; Approver role enforces countersign gate before export

feature_scope.value: Relay device library, SEL/GE/ABB settings import/export, TCC coordination curve editor with Konva.js canvas, N-1 fault study runner, settings diff tool, export package builder with Approver countersign, append-only CIP audit log
feature_scope.rationale: Complete workflow from settings import through coordination validation to approved field export

mvp_scope.value: SEL .rdb import/export, time-overcurrent coordination study (IEEE C37.112), protection zone definition, settings diff against deployed baseline, Windows AD auth with four roles, async IPC job queue
mvp_scope.rationale: SEL-dominant fleet targeted for MVP; GE and ABB formats deferred to phase 2

security_baseline.value: SQLCipher AES-256 for SQLite database encryption, Windows AD SSO, append-only audit_events with DB trigger enforcement, no network egress, Sentry offline queue mode
security_baseline.rationale: Air-gapped deployment with CIP-007 compliant local data protection and tamper-evident audit evidence

frontend_stack.rationale: Electron provides native file system and Windows AD integration; Svelte enables reactive coordination canvas

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with SciPy via child_process IPC
backend_stack.rationale: SciPy provides IEEE C37.112 TCC curve fitting and fault current calculations not available in JavaScript

data_platform.value: SQLite with SQLCipher 4.5 encryption
data_platform.rationale: Embedded database for air-gapped workstation deployment with no server dependency

hosting_target.value: Windows 10/11 workstation, NSIS installer, internal SharePoint file share distribution
hosting_target.rationale: Local desktop deployment; no cloud infrastructure

privacy_retention_policy.value: Settings projects retained locally until manually deleted; audit logs retained 3 years per CIP-007 evidence requirements
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: NERC CIP-007 requires 3-year retention of configuration change audit evidence

future_scope.value: GE Multilin and ABB REL format support, cloud-based team project sharing, SCADA asset register integration for relay inventory sync
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 multi-vendor format coverage and team collaboration features

constraints.value: Fully offline on air-gapped workstations; NSIS installer under 200MB; no third-party cloud telemetry in production builds
constraints.rationale: Air-gap security policy prohibits external data transmission; installer size constraint from corporate software distribution policy

observability_baseline.value: Sentry offline queue (no cloud flush in production), structured local log file with daily rotation, Electron crash dumps to %APPDATA%
observability_baseline.rationale: Air-gapped environment requires offline-capable error capture with manual USB export for support

execution_preference.value: Iterative releases with internal beta program among protection engineering team
execution_preference.rationale: Domain complexity requires close feedback loop with protection engineers during development

compliance_context.value: NERC PRC-001, PRC-019, PRC-023 protection and control reliability standards; IEEE C37.112 inverse-time overcurrent relay standard; NERC CIP-007 configuration change evidence requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Protection relay settings subject to NERC PRC standards; all changes require CIP-007 audit evidence

project.goal: Air-gapped desktop tool for SEL relay settings coordination and validated export
project.target_users: Protection engineers at transmission utilities

frontend.canvas: Konva.js for TCC log-log curve rendering
frontend.state: Svelte writable stores with derived coordination violation state

backend.framework: SciPy IEEE C37.112 TCC engine, SEL .rdb parser
backend.ipc: Electron child_process JSON-RPC async job queue

security.audit: append-only audit_events with BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE triggers

data.entities: relay_devices, settings_files, protection_zones, coordination_curves, settings_packages, firmware_versions, audit_events

reasoner_reviews.product: The relay settings tool addresses a real pain point - protection engineers managing SEL relay fleets currently switch between vendor-specific software packages with no unified coordination view. MVP scoped to SEL .rdb plus TCC coordination study is well-targeted. The Approver countersign gate before export is a safety-critical workflow that must be preserved without shortcuts.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The async IPC job queue design added in round 2 resolves the UI-blocking problem from round 1. The SQLCipher native module rebuild per Electron ABI is the remaining fragile integration point that must be automated in the electron-builder afterPack hook. The 200MB installer constraint requires explicit component budget verification in CI. The 'auto-scaling worker pool' language in the plan is architecturally incorrect for a single-workstation application where the worker count is fixed at 2.
reasoner_reviews.security: Round 2 correctly added BEFORE UPDATE and BEFORE DELETE triggers on audit_events. The CIP-007 3-year audit evidence requirement is met by the monthly CSV export to encrypted USB. One remaining gap: SQLCipher key rotation on Active Directory service account password change is not addressed and will cause databases to become unreadable on password rotation.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 200MB installer constraint is achievable with python-build-standalone (~45MB), pre-compiled SciPy wheels (~52MB), and Electron 28 (~82MB). The CI size assertion gate is correctly specified. Worker pool scaling language in the plan does not apply to a fixed-seat workstation application.

frontend.app_structure: Electron main process manages Windows AD authentication, SQLCipher DB lifecycle, async IPC job dispatcher, and Python worker pool. Svelte renderer hosts SPA with routes: /projects (browser), /relay/:id/settings (parameter editor), /study/:id (Konva.js TCC canvas and N-1 runner), /export/:id (package builder and Approver gate).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Project Browser: recent projects list with open/create. Relay Editor: settings parameter grid by relay model family. TCC Coordination Canvas: Konva.js log-log plot with primary/backup relay curve overlay, fault current slider, CTI margin display. N-1 Study Runner: fault scenario inputs (type, source impedance), async study dispatch, violation table with per-scenario CTI results. Export Builder: diff viewer against deployed_baseline_hash, Approver countersign form requiring AD credential re-entry.
frontend.state_management: Svelte writable stores for active project and relay device. Derived store reactively recomputes coordination violations when pickup or time-dial settings change via 150ms debounced SciPy dispatch. Study results in separate async store populated via IPC job callbacks.
frontend.performance_notes: Konva.js canvas handles 500-point TCC datasets at 60fps. Study dispatch debounced 150ms after settings edit. Python worker pool capped at 2 workers to limit memory on 8GB workstations.
frontend.frontend_risks: SQLCipher native module must be rebuilt per Electron ABI version - automate via electron-builder afterPack hook with electron-rebuild.

backend.service_design: Python 3.12 process pool (2 workers) managed by Electron main via stdio IPC. JSON-RPC commands: parse_sel_file, run_tcc_study, export_package, diff_settings.
backend.business_modules: SEL parser reads .rdb and .acselerator tagged parameter blocks. TCC calculator evaluates IEEE C37.112 inverse-time equations (standard, very inverse, extremely inverse) for operating time at fault current. Coordination checker validates CTI >= 0.3s. Export builder renders SEL .acselerator output with approver signature hash.
backend.failure_handling: IPC timeout 30s per job; worker crash triggers respawn with job replay. Parse errors return structured {line, field, error_type} objects.

security.auth_design: Windows AD LDAP bind via node-activedirectory2. Group membership maps to: CIPProtectionReadOnly (view), CIPProtectionEditor (edit), CIPProtectionApprover (countersign), CIPProtectionAdmin (audit log access).
security.authorization_model: Export blocked until CIPProtectionApprover countersigns change set. Role checks enforced in Electron main before IPC dispatch. Audit log viewable only by CIPProtectionAdmin.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events (id UUID, user_samaccountname TEXT, action_type TEXT, relay_id TEXT, old_value_hash TEXT, new_value_hash TEXT, timestamp INTEGER). BEFORE UPDATE trigger raises SQLITE_ERROR 'AUDIT_TAMPER_DENIED'. BEFORE DELETE raises 'AUDIT_DELETE_DENIED'. Monthly CSV export to encrypted USB for CIP-007 evidence.

data.entities: relay_devices (id UUID, model TEXT, manufacturer TEXT, substation_id TEXT, firmware_version_id FK, imported_at). settings_files (id UUID, relay_id FK, format ENUM sel/ge/abb, raw_encrypted BLOB, parsed_json TEXT, version INT, imported_by TEXT, imported_at). protection_zones (id UUID, project_id FK, zone_name TEXT, fault_current_amps REAL, zone_type ENUM primary/backup/pilot). coordination_curves (id UUID, relay_id FK, curve_type ENUM toc/ioc/distance, pickup_amps REAL, time_dial REAL, curve_family TEXT). settings_packages (id UUID, project_id FK, status ENUM draft/approved/exported, approved_by TEXT, approver_ad_hash TEXT, exported_at). firmware_versions (id UUID, relay_model TEXT, version_string TEXT). audit_events append-only with DB trigger enforcement.
data.schema_notes: Settings content stored as AES-256 encrypted blobs; parsed_json cached for read performance. FK ON DELETE RESTRICT prevents orphaned settings.
data.migration_notes: Schema versioning via applied_migrations table checked at Electron startup.

devops.deployment_topology: NSIS installer (~183MB): Electron 28 ~82MB, python-build-standalone 3.12 ~45MB, SciPy + NumPy pre-built wheels ~52MB, SQLCipher native module ~1MB, Svelte bundle ~3MB.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint + Svelte-check, mypy on Python engine, Vitest for Svelte, pytest for TCC accuracy (0.01s tolerance vs. IEEE C37.112 reference curves), electron-builder NSIS, installer size assertion gate (<200MB), SharePoint upload.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous NSIS installer retained 90 days on SharePoint; no auto-update in production. Users manually install rollback version.
devops.devops_risks: SQLCipher ABI mismatch across Electron versions - pin Electron version in package.json and automate rebuild in afterPack hook.

ISS-001.title: Synchronous IPC calls freeze Svelte UI during coordination studies
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 called Python subprocess synchronously from renderer IPC bridge, blocking UI thread for up to 8 seconds during N-1 study runs. Async job queue with UUID tokens and progress callbacks added in round 2.

history.note: Identified during IPC design review
Async job queue with ipcRenderer.on callbacks implemented

ISS-002.title: Installer size not analyzed against 200MB constraint
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 did not specify Python runtime packaging strategy. python-build-standalone with per-component size budget specified in round 2; CI size assertion gate added.

history.note: Raised during constraints review
python-build-standalone budget specified; CI gate added

ISS-003.title: Audit log lacks immutability for CIP-007 evidence
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 used plain SQLite table. BEFORE UPDATE and BEFORE DELETE DB triggers added in round 2 to enforce append-only semantics.

history.note: Raised by security reviewer
DB trigger enforcement added

focus_issues.title: Synchronous IPC calls freeze Svelte UI during coordination studies
focus_issues.detail: Async IPC job queue now correctly designed with UUID tokens and ipcRenderer.on callbacks.

latest_recommendations: Introduce async IPC job queue with UUID job tokens to prevent UI blocking
Specify python-build-standalone component size budget with CI assertion gate
Add BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE DB triggers on audit_events for CIP-007 immutability

Protection zone and coordination curve data model is domain-appropriate

Add DB trigger for audit_events append-only enforcement
