previous_plan.title: ICCP TASE.2 Python SDK Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python SDK for parsing and building ICCP TASE.2 data objects distributed via PyPI, targeting utility integration developers.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Single-module IccpClient with codec and model classes combined; asyncio transport with sync fallback.
previous_plan.api_design: IccpClient exposing parse_frame(), subscribe_transfer_set(), build_bilateral_table() mixing sync and async methods.
previous_plan.data_model: DataObject, BilateralTable, TransferSet dataclasses with basic field definitions and no slot optimization.

target_output.thinking_summary: Revising the ICCP TASE.2 SDK to resolve ISS-002 by fully separating the synchronous parser (iccp.codec + iccp.model + iccp.parser) from asyncio streaming client (iccp.async_client). Asyncio imported only inside function bodies in iccp.async_client to prevent event loop initialization on import of core modules. Module hierarchy mirrors TASE.2 spec layers: framing, encoding, model, transport.

fix_report.action_taken: Introduced strict module split: iccp.codec handles binary framing and ASN.1 BER helpers with no asyncio imports; iccp.model defines all typed dataclasses with __slots__; iccp.parser is synchronous public API composing codec and model; iccp.async_client imports asyncio only at function scope to prevent event loop initialization on import. Integration tests confirm 'import iccp.parser' triggers no event loop creation.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Batch consumers importing iccp.parser have zero asyncio coupling; CI confirms no event loop creation on import of core modules

target_output.title: ICCP TASE.2 Python SDK - v2 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-dependency Python 3.10+ SDK for parsing, building, and exchanging ICCP TASE.2 data objects between inter-control center communication endpoints. Distributed via PyPI, the library provides typed Python representations of all IEC 60870-6 object classes with a synchronous parse API and an optional asyncio streaming client strictly decoupled from the synchronous path. The iccp.async_client module imports asyncio only inside function bodies to prevent event loop side effects when batch processing consumers import only the core parser.
target_output.architecture_overview: Layered package architecture with four modules enforcing strict separation: iccp.codec handles binary APDU framing and ASN.1 BER tag-length-value encoding with no external imports beyond struct and typing; iccp.model defines __slots__ dataclasses for all TASE.2 object classes including DataStateQualified, DataRealQ, DataStateQTimeQ, BilateralTable, and TransferSet; iccp.parser composes codec and model as the synchronous public API with zero asyncio dependency; iccp.async_client provides the optional asyncio streaming transport importing asyncio exclusively within function bodies preventing event loop initialization at import time. Batch pipeline consumers import only iccp.parser; streaming consumers use iccp.async_client with no additional install dependencies since asyncio is stdlib.
target_output.technology_stack: Core: Python 3.10+, stdlib only (struct, dataclasses, typing, logging, asyncio imported lazily in async_client only). Testing: pytest 8, pytest-asyncio for async_client tests, hypothesis for property-based frame fuzzing, 90% coverage gate. Linting: ruff, mypy --strict. Distribution: pyproject.toml with setuptools, PyPI trusted publisher via GitHub OIDC. CI: GitHub Actions matrix across Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12.
target_output.functional_feature_map: TASE.2 Frame Codec: parse and serialize all TASE.2 APDU types including Transfer Set Report, Data Request, and Bilateral Table negotiation frames with byte-accurate round-trip against reference fixtures. Typed Object Model: Python dataclasses for every IEC 60870-6 object class with quality flag bitmask helpers and to_dict() serialization. Bilateral Table Manager: parse and build bilateral_table negotiation payloads with agreed DataSetRef entries. Transfer Set Subscription: subscribe_transfer_set() async generator yielding DataObject instances per integrity interval. Server Fixture: IccpServerFixture context manager for CI loopback integration tests without live ICCP endpoints.
target_output.system_components: iccp.codec: struct-based APDU framing, ASN.1 BER TLV parser, IccpFrameError hierarchy with byte offset context. iccp.model: DataObject, BilateralTable, TransferSet, Tase2Message dataclasses with __slots__ for memory efficiency. iccp.parser: IccpParser class with parse_frame(bytes)->Tase2Message and build_frame(Tase2Message)->bytes; no asyncio import. iccp.async_client: AsyncIccpClient with connect(host,port), subscribe_transfer_set(ts_id) async generator, disconnect(); asyncio imported only within function bodies. iccp.server_fixture: IccpServerFixture async context manager providing loopback host/port for test use only.
target_output.workflows: Synchronous Receive Path: developer instantiates IccpParser, calls parser.parse_frame(raw_bytes) returning typed Tase2Message; unpacks payload as TransferSetReport to iterate DataObject updates with strongly-typed value and quality_flags; no event loop required for this path. Asyncio Streaming Path: developer instantiates AsyncIccpClient(host, port), calls 'async for data_obj in client.subscribe_transfer_set(ts_id)' receiving DataObject per integrity interval; asyncio module loaded lazily on first async_client function call. CI Integration Test: IccpServerFixture spun up as async context manager; AsyncIccpClient connects to loopback port; verifies byte-accurate round-trip for all object classes against IEC 60870-6 reference binary fixtures stored in tests/fixtures/.
target_output.data_model: DataObject (tag_id: str, object_class: Literal['state','analog','discrete','time_series'], value: float|int|bool, quality_flags: int, timestamp: datetime|None). BilateralTable (local_vcc_id: str, remote_vcc_id: str, agreed_data_sets: list[DataSetRef]). DataSetRef (data_set_name: str, domain_name: str). TransferSet (transfer_set_id: str, data_set_ref: DataSetRef, transfer_interval_ms: int, integrity_check: bool). Tase2Message (message_type: Tase2MessageType, sequence_number: int, payload_bytes: bytes). All dataclasses use __slots__ and provide to_dict() for consumer JSON serialization.
target_output.api_design: Public exports from iccp.__init__: IccpParser, AsyncIccpClient, BilateralTable, DataObject, TransferSet, IccpFrameError, IccpConformanceError. Key signatures: IccpParser.parse_frame(data: bytes) -> Tase2Message; IccpParser.build_frame(msg: Tase2Message) -> bytes; IccpParser.parse_bilateral_table(data: bytes) -> BilateralTable; AsyncIccpClient.connect(host: str, port: int) -> None; AsyncIccpClient.subscribe_transfer_set(ts_id: str) -> AsyncGenerator[DataObject, None]; IccpServerFixture().__aenter__() -> tuple[str, int] loopback host/port.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Defensive parsing: all struct.unpack_from calls wrapped in try/except struct.error raising IccpFrameError with byte offset and message type context; truncated frame detected before unpack attempts. IEC 60870-6 TASE.2 conformance validated via binary reference fixture suite from spec. No credential handling in library; transport security over TCP is consumer responsibility. Library logging via logging.getLogger('iccp') only; no hardcoded log handlers to avoid polluting consumer log config.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: PyPI trusted publisher via GitHub OIDC: tag push to main triggers publish workflow only after CI matrix passes on Python 3.10, 3.11, and 3.12. Version strategy: semver with CHANGELOG.md; breaking API changes require major version bump. GitHub Releases: wheel and sdist attached per release tag with SHA256 checksums. Documentation: Sphinx with autodoc published to ReadTheDocs on each release tag. Conformance fixtures in tests/fixtures/tase2_reference_messages/ committed as binary .tase2 files.
target_output.observability: Library emits structured log events via logging.getLogger('iccp'): DEBUG for frame parse details and object construction steps, INFO for connection state changes in async_client, WARNING for quality flag anomalies detected during object construction, ERROR for frame parse failures with byte offset. No metrics endpoint; consumers integrate log output with Datadog or Splunk via their own handlers. Parse error rates tracked by consumers via IccpFrameError exception counts in their integration telemetry.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost for the library itself. GitHub Actions CI matrix across 3 Python versions consumes approximately 15 minutes per push on free tier. ReadTheDocs documentation build triggered on each release tag. Memory footprint per DataObject with __slots__ is approximately 200 bytes enabling high-volume TASE.2 streaming scenarios at scale.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): iccp.codec binary framing with full APDU type coverage, iccp.model dataclasses for all TASE.2 object classes, iccp.parser synchronous API, conformance test suite, PyPI alpha release. Phase 2 (4 weeks): iccp.async_client with lazy asyncio import, IccpServerFixture for CI, pytest-asyncio conformance tests, ReadTheDocs site, PyPI v1.0 release. Phase 3 (4 weeks): BilateralTable negotiation send path, TransferSet build path, hypothesis-based fuzz testing of codec, Go port feasibility spike.
target_output.development_guidelines: mypy --strict enforced in CI; all public functions have full type annotations. ruff for linting. 'import asyncio' permitted only inside function bodies in iccp.async_client; enforced via pre-commit hook checking for module-level asyncio imports. Test file structure mirrors source tree: tests/test_codec.py, tests/test_model.py, tests/test_async_client.py. Commit messages reference IEC 60870-6 spec section numbers for protocol behavior changes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: IEC 60870-6 spec ambiguities in ASN.1 encoding for edge-case object types causing interoperability failures with specific ICCP server implementations. Mitigation: collect reference message captures from at least two vendor implementations before v1.0 release. Tradeoff: zero-dependency constraint prevents using pydantic for validation but significantly reduces enterprise adoption friction. Risk: asyncio API changes between Python minor versions; mitigation: CI matrix covers 3.10-3.12 and asyncio usage confined to async_client module.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected dataclasses with __slots__ over pydantic to maintain zero-dependency core for enterprise Python environments. Confirmed asyncio stdlib over trio for async transport to minimize dependency surface. Chose pytest-asyncio for async tests over custom event loop fixtures for long-term maintainability.

metadata.notes: Python ICCP TASE.2 SDK revision resolving asyncio decoupling issue with strict module layering.

profile.domain: Utility Asset Management - High-Voltage Transformer Fleet

project_goal.value: Desktop application for utility asset management teams to track condition, maintenance history, and lifecycle status of high-voltage transformer fleets across transmission substations, scaling to portfolios of thousands of assets across multiple operating regions
project_goal.rationale: Replaces fragmented spreadsheet and Access database workflows with a structured asset lifecycle platform supporting DGA trending and risk scoring

target_users.value: Asset managers, maintenance planners, and substation engineers at investor-owned utilities managing transmission transformer fleets
target_users.rationale: Primary users responsible for transformer condition assessment, DGA analysis, and maintenance scheduling

project_class.rationale: On-premises deployment required; data must not leave corporate network; native file system access needed for LIMS CSV imports

capabilities.rationale: Electron UI, Python analytics engine, PostgreSQL asset database, LIMS and EAM system integrations

complexity_level.rationale: Structured CRUD with DGA condition scoring algorithms and LIMS integration; no real-time data streams

risk_level.rationale: Internal operational data; incorrect condition scores could influence maintenance deferral decisions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Transformer asset data is internal operational information not classified as CIP-sensitive BES cyber asset data

external_exposure.rationale: Deployed on internal corporate network; no internet-facing endpoints

access_model.value: Custom JWT with roles: viewer (read-only), planner (maintenance and inspection CRUD), manager (condition assessment and risk overrides), admin (user management)
access_model.rationale: Role separation between planners scheduling maintenance and managers making lifecycle decisions

feature_scope.value: Transformer asset registry with substation/region hierarchy, condition assessment entry, dissolved gas analysis (DGA) trending with IEEE C57.104 threshold alerts, Duval triangle fault classification, maintenance record history, risk scoring (Weibull failure probability), inspection schedule management, LIMS CSV import, lifecycle cost analysis reports
feature_scope.rationale: Full transformer asset lifecycle management workflow from DGA import to risk-based maintenance planning

mvp_scope.value: Transformer asset registry, DGA CSV import from LIMS, IEEE C57.104 L1/L2 gas threshold alerting, Duval triangle classification, condition assessment entry, maintenance record history, basic risk score dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: DGA trending is highest-value feature for replacement of spreadsheet workflows; Q4 pilot with one operating region

security_baseline.value: Custom JWT with bcrypt hashing, TLS 1.3 for internal Electron-to-FastAPI connection, PostgreSQL on-premises with role-based access, asset modification audit log
security_baseline.rationale: Internal application security baseline; data residency on corporate servers

frontend_stack.rationale: Team familiarity with React; Electron provides native file system access for LIMS CSV import and PDF report generation

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for local API server; Python Pandas and SciPy for DGA analysis and Weibull failure probability scoring

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on-premises server
data_platform.rationale: Relational data with strong referential integrity for transformer-to-substation-to-region hierarchy; on-premises data residency

hosting_target.value: On-premises Windows Server 2022 application server; Electron NSIS installer for workstations
hosting_target.rationale: Corporate policy requires on-premises deployment; no cloud dependencies

privacy_retention_policy.value: Asset records retained indefinitely while in service; decommissioned asset records retained 10 years; maintenance logs retained 10 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: ISO 55000 asset lifecycle records and internal audit requirements

future_scope.value: Predictive DGA trend ML model, GIS substation map view, mobile field inspection companion app, multi-region consolidated dashboard, direct LIMS API integration
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 predictive analytics, mobility, and full LIMS integration

constraints.value: Deploy on existing Windows Server 2022; no cloud dependencies; support up to 5000 transformer assets; PostgreSQL already licensed
constraints.rationale: Infrastructure constraints and existing license agreements drive on-premises requirement

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for FastAPI server metrics, structured application logs, Jenkins build pipeline reporting
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate on-premises monitoring standard

execution_preference.value: Iterative quarterly releases with pilot in one operating region before full fleet rollout
execution_preference.rationale: Risk-managed rollout given operational importance of asset data accuracy

project.goal: Desktop asset management platform for HV transformer fleet DGA trending and maintenance planning
project.target_users: Asset managers and maintenance planners at transmission utilities
project.timeline: MVP Q4 pilot, full rollout 12 months

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for UI state
frontend.charts: Recharts for DGA trend lines and risk score history

security.audit: asset_modifications table with before/after field values

data.entities: transformer_assets, dga_records, maintenance_records, condition_assessments, failure_events, inspection_schedules, asset_risk_scores

devops.packaging: Electron Builder NSIS for client, NSSM Windows service for FastAPI

open_questions.lims_format: CSV import confirmed for MVP; direct LIMS API in phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The transformer asset management scope is well-defined. DGA trending against IEEE C57.104 thresholds is the highest-value MVP feature - it directly replaces manual spreadsheet tracking that misses fault gas accumulation trends. The Duval triangle fault type classification adds operator confidence in acting on threshold alerts. Condition-based risk scoring weights require validation with asset management domain experts to ensure they match the utility's maintenance philosophy.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Electron + FastAPI localhost server pattern is appropriate for this use case. The Python analytics module correctly separates DGA trend evaluation and Weibull scoring from the React UI. PostgreSQL is well-suited for the transformer-to-substation-to-region hierarchy. The LIMS CSV import needs configurable column mapping and deduplication by (asset_serial, sample_date) to handle re-submitted lab files. 5000 assets with 4 DGA samples/year over 30 years yields ~600k records - PostgreSQL handles this well with indexes on (asset_id, sample_date).
reasoner_reviews.security: Custom JWT with bcrypt is appropriate for an internal application. The audit log should capture before/after values for condition assessment changes to support asset management audit trails, not just user and timestamp. Role separation between planner and manager is important - planners should not override condition scores set by managers.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: PostgreSQL on existing Windows Server 2022 hardware. Jenkins CI on Windows Server requires Docker-based agents to isolate Python and Node environments. FastAPI as NSSM Windows service starts reliably; include a health check endpoint for service monitoring.

frontend.app_structure: Electron main process launches FastAPI as child process on startup, manages lifecycle, opens Chromium window at localhost:8080. React SPA with React Router v6: /assets (fleet dashboard), /assets/:id (detail tabs), /dga/:id (trend view), /maintenance/:id (history), /inspections (schedule calendar), /reports (risk dashboard).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Fleet Dashboard: filterable transformer grid with risk score band color coding by substation and region. Asset Detail: tabs for asset info, condition assessments, DGA history, maintenance records, inspection schedule. DGA Trend View: Recharts line chart of H2, CH4, C2H2, C2H4, CO concentrations with IEEE C57.104 L1/L2 action level overlays and Duval triangle indicator. Maintenance History: chronological work order timeline. Risk Score Dashboard: fleet ranked by computed risk score with Weibull failure probability breakdown.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all server state (5-minute stale time). Zustand for active asset selection, filter/sort prefs, DGA chart zoom. DGA import wizard managed with React Hook Form.

backend.service_design: FastAPI with routers: assets, dga_records, maintenance, inspections, risk_scoring, imports. Python analytics module evaluates IEEE C57.104 thresholds and Weibull risk scores.
backend.business_modules: DGA Analyzer: reads H2, CH4, C2H2, C2H4, C2H6, CO, CO2 values; evaluates against IEEE C57.104 Table 2 L1/L2 limits (C2H2 L1: 35 ppm, L2: 50 ppm); computes Duval triangle fault type (PD, T1, T2, T3, D1, D2) from C2H2/C2H4/CH4 ratios; flags rate-of-change >20%/month. Risk Scorer: weighted sum of condition inputs (insulation, cooling, bushings, LTC) with age-adjusted Weibull failure probability. LIMS CSV Importer: configurable column mapping, plausibility validation (H2: 0-5000 ppm), deduplication by (serial, sample_date).
backend.api_patterns: GET /assets?region=&risk_gt= returns paginated fleet with risk scores. GET /assets/{id}/dga?from=&to= returns DGA series with threshold annotations. POST /imports/dga accepts CSV multipart upload. GET /assets/{id}/risk-score returns breakdown. PATCH /assets/{id}/condition updates assessment.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT HS256, 8-hour expiry, stored in Electron safeStorage (OS keychain). Bcrypt cost 12 for password hashing.
security.authorization_model: Roles: viewer (GET only), planner (maintenance and inspection CRUD), manager (condition assessment and risk overrides), admin (user management). FastAPI dependency injection checks JWT claims per endpoint.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: asset_modifications (id, user_id FK, table_name, record_id, field_name, old_value TEXT, new_value TEXT, modified_at) captures field-level before/after for all condition assessment and asset data changes.

data.entities: transformer_assets (id UUID PK, serial_number TEXT UNIQUE, substation_id FK, manufacturer TEXT, model TEXT, kva_rating INT, voltage_class_kv REAL, installation_year INT, service_status ENUM active/spare/decommissioned, region_id FK). dga_records (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, sample_date DATE, lab_id TEXT, h2_ppm REAL, ch4_ppm REAL, c2h2_ppm REAL, c2h4_ppm REAL, c2h6_ppm REAL, co_ppm REAL, co2_ppm REAL, tdcg_ppm REAL, l1_exceeded BOOL, l2_exceeded BOOL, duval_fault_type TEXT, imported_by FK). maintenance_records (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, work_order TEXT, work_type TEXT, performed_date DATE, description TEXT). condition_assessments (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, assessed_by FK, assessment_date DATE, insulation_score INT, cooling_score INT, bushing_score INT, ltc_score INT, overall_score INT). failure_events (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, failure_date DATE, failure_mode TEXT, root_cause TEXT, repair_duration_days INT). inspection_schedules (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, scheduled_date DATE, inspection_type TEXT, assigned_to FK, status ENUM scheduled/completed/overdue). asset_risk_scores (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, computed_at TIMESTAMP, risk_score REAL, score_breakdown_json JSONB, model_version TEXT).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on-premises. Index on transformer_assets(region_id, service_status) for fleet dashboard. dga_records indexed on (asset_id, sample_date). asset_risk_scores retains history for score trend visualization.
data.migration_notes: Alembic migrations run at FastAPI startup. No ad-hoc DDL in application code.

devops.deployment_topology: Windows Server 2022: PostgreSQL 16 service, FastAPI as NSSM Windows service on localhost:8080, Grafana + Prometheus agents. Workstations: Electron NSIS installer (~120MB).
devops.ci_cd_design: Jenkins: flake8 + mypy, pytest for FastAPI and DGA analyzer (reference IEEE C57.104 values), ESLint + Vitest for React, electron-builder NSIS, upload to SharePoint.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping FastAPI /metrics. Grafana: API p95 latency, DGA import success/failure rate, risk score computation latency. Application logs to Windows Event Log.

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass architecture for a transformer fleet asset management desktop app targeting utilities with up to 5000 HV transformers. Key choices: Electron + FastAPI localhost server for on-premises with Python analytics, PostgreSQL for relational asset hierarchy, IEEE C57.104 DGA evaluation in Python analytics module with Duval triangle classification. Plan quality 'moderate' - core domain sections are strong but observability and cost sections are slightly thinner.
target_output.title: HV Transformer Fleet Asset Management Desktop Application
target_output.executive_summary: An Electron 28 / React 18 / Python 3.12 FastAPI desktop application for transmission utility asset managers to track condition, DGA history, and maintenance lifecycle of high-voltage transformer fleets scaled to 5000 assets across multiple operating regions. Runs on-premises against PostgreSQL 16 with no cloud dependencies. Core capabilities include DGA trending against IEEE C57.104 L1/L2 fault gas thresholds with Duval triangle fault type classification, condition-based Weibull risk scoring, LIMS CSV import, and maintenance schedule management.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron 28 client launches a FastAPI Python application server as managed child process, communicating over a localhost TLS 1.3 connection. React 18 SPA manages fleet visualization, DGA trend charts (Recharts log-scale), and maintenance workflows via React Query against the FastAPI REST API. FastAPI delegates DGA threshold evaluation (IEEE C57.104 Table 2), Duval triangle fault classification, and Weibull failure probability scoring to a Python analytics module using Pandas and SciPy. PostgreSQL 16 on-premises stores the transformer-to-substation-to-region hierarchy, DGA records, maintenance history, condition assessments, and risk score time series. Grafana and Prometheus provide FastAPI server and PostgreSQL monitoring.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 28, React 18, TypeScript, Recharts for DGA trend and risk dashboards, React Query for server state, Zustand for UI state, React Hook Form for DGA import wizard. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Pydantic v2, Pandas 2.2, SciPy 1.13 for Weibull distribution fitting. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on-premises Windows Server 2022. Auth: Custom JWT HS256 with bcrypt cost 12, Electron safeStorage. Packaging: Electron Builder NSIS. CI: Jenkins. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Transformer Asset Registry: fleet CRUD with substation/region hierarchy, filterable by voltage class, manufacturer, service status, risk score band. DGA Trend Viewer: per-asset dissolved gas history with IEEE C57.104 L1/L2 action level overlays and Duval triangle fault type indicator (PD/T1/T2/T3/D1/D2 classification). LIMS CSV Importer: configurable column mapping for common lab formats, plausibility validation, deduplication by serial+sample_date. Condition Assessment: scored inputs (1-10) for insulation, cooling system, bushings, and LTC health. Risk Score Dashboard: fleet ranked by Weibull-based risk score with factor breakdown. Inspection Schedule Calendar: upcoming and overdue inspection tracking with engineer assignment.
target_output.system_components: Electron Main Process: FastAPI child process lifecycle, TLS cert management, JWT storage in safeStorage, NSIS packaging. React SPA Renderer: fleet dashboard, asset detail tabs, DGA chart, maintenance timeline, import wizard. FastAPI Server: REST routes for assets/dga/maintenance/inspections/risk_scoring/imports, JWT middleware, background task for async risk score recalculation. Python Analytics Module: IEEE C57.104 threshold evaluator, Duval triangle classifier (C2H2/C2H4/CH4 ratios), Weibull failure probability model, LIMS CSV parser with configurable column map. PostgreSQL 16: transformer_assets, dga_records, maintenance_records, condition_assessments, failure_events, inspection_schedules, asset_risk_scores, asset_modifications audit table. Prometheus + Grafana on Windows Server.
target_output.workflows: DGA Lab Result Import Workflow: Asset manager receives monthly DGA lab CSV from LIMS, opens import wizard, maps columns to canonical gas fields (H2, CH4, C2H2, C2H4, C2H6, CO, CO2). Importer validates each gas value against plausibility bounds (H2: 0-5000 ppm, C2H2: 0-500 ppm), deduplicates by (serial_number, sample_date). Commits clean records to dga_records, triggers background risk score recalculation for affected assets. Displays summary: accepted, deduplicated, rejected counts with error detail per rejected row. DGA Threshold Alert Review Workflow: Planner opens fleet dashboard filtered by l1_exceeded=true. Selects flagged transformer, opens DGA trend view showing H2 and C2H2 concentrations trending toward IEEE C57.104 L2 limits (C2H2 L2: 50 ppm). Evaluates rate-of-change (>20%/month escalation flag). Reviews Duval triangle classification (D1 = low-energy discharge - partial discharge with carbonization). Creates maintenance record recommending follow-up oil sampling at 30-day interval and scheduling infrared scan.
target_output.data_model: transformer_assets (id UUID PK, serial_number TEXT UNIQUE, substation_id FK, manufacturer TEXT, model TEXT, kva_rating INT, voltage_class_kv REAL, installation_year INT, service_status ENUM active/spare/decommissioned, region_id FK). dga_records (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, sample_date DATE, lab_id TEXT, h2_ppm REAL, ch4_ppm REAL, c2h2_ppm REAL, c2h4_ppm REAL, c2h6_ppm REAL, co_ppm REAL, co2_ppm REAL, tdcg_ppm REAL, l1_exceeded BOOL, l2_exceeded BOOL, duval_fault_type TEXT, imported_at TIMESTAMP, imported_by FK). maintenance_records (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, work_order TEXT, work_type TEXT, performed_by TEXT, performed_date DATE, description TEXT). condition_assessments (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, assessed_by FK, assessment_date DATE, insulation_score INT, cooling_score INT, bushing_score INT, ltc_score INT, overall_score INT, notes TEXT). failure_events (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, failure_date DATE, failure_mode TEXT, root_cause TEXT, repair_duration_days INT). inspection_schedules (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, scheduled_date DATE, inspection_type TEXT, assigned_to FK, status ENUM scheduled/completed/overdue). asset_risk_scores (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, computed_at TIMESTAMP, risk_score REAL, score_breakdown_json JSONB, model_version TEXT). asset_modifications (id UUID PK, user_id FK, table_name TEXT, record_id TEXT, field_name TEXT, old_value TEXT, new_value TEXT, modified_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: REST API at http://localhost:8080/api/v1. GET /assets?region=&risk_gt=&status= paginated transformer list with current risk scores. GET /assets/{id} full asset detail. GET /assets/{id}/dga?from=&to= DGA time series with L1/L2 threshold annotations. POST /assets/{id}/condition creates condition assessment. POST /imports/dga multipart CSV upload returns import summary. GET /assets/{id}/risk-score current score with factor breakdown. GET /reports/fleet-risk risk distribution across fleet. PATCH /assets/{id} for asset data updates. All endpoints require JWT Bearer.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT HS256 tokens (8-hour expiry) issued on login. Bcrypt cost 12 password hashing. JWT stored in Electron safeStorage. Role enforcement via FastAPI dependency injection: viewer (GET), planner (maintenance and inspection CRUD), manager (condition assessments and risk overrides), admin (user management). TLS 1.3 on localhost with self-signed cert generated at install time. PostgreSQL accessed via limited-privilege app_user. asset_modifications audit table captures field-level before/after for all condition and asset changes. Records retained 10 years per policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Application server (Windows Server 2022): PostgreSQL 16 Windows service, FastAPI registered as NSSM Windows service (auto-start, bound to 127.0.0.1:8080), Prometheus node exporter and FastAPI instrumentator, Grafana on port 3000. Electron NSIS installer (~120MB) distributed via internal SharePoint. Jenkins CI: flake8 + mypy, pytest (FastAPI TestClient + PostgreSQL test DB, IEEE C57.104 reference threshold values), Vitest for React, electron-builder, artifact upload. Database migrations via Alembic at FastAPI startup. PostgreSQL nightly pg_dump to internal NAS, 30-day retention.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scraping FastAPI /metrics every 15 seconds. Grafana: API p95 latency by endpoint, DGA import job counts, risk score recalculation latency, PostgreSQL connection pool. structlog JSON to Windows Event Log and rotating file. Logged: user logins, DGA imports (accepted/rejected counts), risk score recalculations, asset data modifications.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: On-premises, no cloud cost. PostgreSQL on existing Windows Server hardware. Fleet dashboard query for 5000 assets <2s with indexed (region_id, service_status) scan. DGA records for 5000 assets at 4 samples/year over 30 years = 600k rows; indexed on (asset_id, sample_date) for single-asset trend queries. Risk score recalculation for full fleet <5 minutes nightly.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 months): Transformer asset registry with substation/region hierarchy, DGA CSV import with IEEE C57.104 L1/L2 alerting and Duval triangle classification, condition assessment entry, maintenance record history, Weibull risk score dashboard, JWT auth with four roles, Jenkins CI, PostgreSQL on-premises deployment. Phase 2 (months 7-12): Predictive DGA trend ML model, inspection schedule calendar, lifecycle cost analysis report, GIS substation map view, direct LIMS API integration. Phase 3 (months 13-18): React Native mobile field inspection app, multi-region consolidated dashboard, automated capital replacement recommendation workflow.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for Electron and React. mypy strict for Python backend and analytics. Pydantic v2 models as API contract source of truth. pytest with reference IEEE C57.104 threshold values for DGA analyzer. Alembic for all schema changes. React Query invalidation on DGA import completion triggers risk score refresh. No ad-hoc DDL in application code.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: LIMS CSV format varies across lab vendors - configurable mapping reduces but does not eliminate per-vendor setup. Mitigate: ship 3 pre-configured templates for LabWare, STARLIMS, and common custom formats. Risk: condition scoring weights opaque to users - wrong weights lead to misinformed maintenance deferral. Mitigate: expose weight configuration to admin role with version history. Tradeoff: async risk recalculation after DGA import vs. synchronous - async prevents wizard from blocking on 5000-asset recalculation; accepted with stale-score indicator in UI until recomputation completes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: PostgreSQL chosen over SQLite for concurrent multi-user access from multiple workstations. Python SciPy for Weibull failure probability over R due to existing Python stack. DGA import as CSV for MVP with direct LIMS API in phase 2. IEEE C57.104-2008 selected per utility's existing practices.

metadata.notes: First-pass architecture for HV transformer fleet asset management. Strong DGA workflow, data model, and security sections; observability and cost sections slightly thinner, consistent with moderate quality target.

profile.domain: Grid Cybersecurity Tooling

project_goal.value: CLI tool for grid cybersecurity engineers to audit SCADA communication server configurations against IEC 62351 and NERC CIP baselines, generating CVSS-scored structured remediation reports
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual config review checklists with automated structured audit output

target_users.value: Grid cybersecurity engineers and CIP compliance officers auditing SCADA communication server configurations at generation and transmission utilities
target_users.rationale: Primary users are OT security teams running CIP-007 compliance evidence programs

project_class.rationale: Offline command-line execution against local or exported config files; no browser UI

capabilities.rationale: Config parsing, finding storage, and optional CMDB integration via plugin interface

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-rule evaluation engine with CVSS scoring and CIP requirement mapping

risk_level.rationale: Tool handles confidential OT security configuration data and CIP audit findings

data_sensitivity.rationale: SCADA configuration exports contain network topology and security posture details

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on air-gapped or restricted OT network workstations; no external connectivity

access_model.value: Local binary execution; no auth service; OS-level file permissions protect audit output files
access_model.rationale: CLI tools in OT environments rely on workstation access controls, not app-level auth

feature_scope.value: Config file ingestion for DNP3 and IEC 60870-5 servers, rule evaluation against configurable CIP baseline, CVSS scoring per finding, machine-readable JSON report, human-readable summary, remediation command generation
feature_scope.rationale: Full audit cycle from config import to prioritized remediation output

mvp_scope.value: Single-target config audit with built-in CIP-007 baseline rules, JSON findings report, human-readable summary to stdout
mvp_scope.rationale: Single-device audit covers highest-priority CIP-007 patch and port management checks

security_baseline.value: Audit report files written with 0600 permissions; no config data transmitted to external endpoints; input validation on config file parsing to prevent path traversal
security_baseline.rationale: Tool handles sensitive OT config data and must not exfiltrate findings

frontend_stack.value: N/A - CLI tool with stdout/stderr output only
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI for a command-line tool

backend_stack.rationale: Single static binary distribution with fast startup and strong stdlib for config parsing

data_platform.value: No database; findings stored as JSON files on local filesystem; optional SQLite for multi-run trending
data_platform.rationale: Air-gapped environments cannot use cloud databases; local file output is portable

hosting_target.value: Static binary distribution via GitHub Releases for Linux/Windows; no cloud hosting
hosting_target.rationale: OT workstations require offline installable binaries

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit report files contain no PII; retention governed by consumer CIP evidence management procedures
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Tool produces CIP audit evidence artifacts managed by the consumer organization

future_scope.value: Multi-target batch audit mode, CMDB integration plugin for asset enrichment, delta reporting between audit runs
future_scope.rationale: Batch mode and trending scale the tool to fleet-level compliance programs

constraints.value: Static binary for Linux amd64 and Windows amd64; no runtime dependencies; must run on air-gapped workstations; NERC CIP-007 baseline built-in
constraints.rationale: OT environment deployment constraints require fully self-contained binary

observability_baseline.value: Verbose flag enables debug logging to stderr; audit run metadata appended to audit_runs.log in output directory
observability_baseline.rationale: CLI observability via structured log file, not APM

execution_preference.value: Rule-driven TDD: write failing rule unit tests before implementing each CIP check
execution_preference.rationale: Security rule correctness is critical; TDD ensures each check maps to spec requirements

compliance_context.value: NERC CIP-007 (ports and services, patch management), IEC 62351 security standards, NIST SP 800-82 OT security guidance
compliance_context.rationale: Tool is purpose-built for CIP-007 compliance evidence generation

project.goal: Automated CLI audit tool for SCADA communication server CIP compliance
project.target_users: Grid cybersecurity engineers

backend.framework: Cobra for command routing, custom rule engine

security.input_validation: Config file path traversal prevention, schema validation on input files

data.output: JSON findings report plus human-readable stdout summary

reasoner_reviews.product: The CIP-007 focus is correct for v1.0 - patch management and ports/services checks cover highest-frequency audit findings at utilities. Remediation command generation is a strong differentiator over spreadsheet-based tools.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go static binary is ideal for OT workstation deployment. Round 1 left the rule engine architecture underspecified - need to define how CIP baseline YAML files are embedded via Go embed.FS vs loaded externally, and how custom rule sets override built-in baselines.
reasoner_reviews.security: Output file permissions (0600) and path traversal protection are correct. The tool must also sanitize config content before including in JSON reports to prevent stored XSS if reports are later loaded in browser-based evidence portals.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Air-gapped deployment means baseline rule updates must ship as binary releases or as signed YAML bundles transferred via USB. Network calls in the binary are a CIP compliance red flag.

backend.service_design: Cobra command tree: audit (single target), batch (multi-target, Phase 2), report (format conversion), rules (list and validate baselines). Rule engine: RuleSet loaded from embed.FS or verified external bundle; each Rule struct defines check_type (port_whitelist/cipher_check/auth_mode/patch_level), target_config_key, expected_value, cip_requirement_ref, cvss_base_score, ot_modifier.
backend.api_patterns: CLI: scada-audit audit --config server.cfg --baseline cip007 --output findings.json --format json|text --verbose. Exit codes: 0=no findings, 1=medium/low only, 2=high findings, 3=critical findings for CI pipeline integration.
backend.failure_handling: Config parse errors surface as structured AuditError with line number and field; rule evaluation panics recovered by deferred recover() emitting error-severity finding; non-zero exit on critical findings enables CI integration.

data.entities: AuditTarget (host_id, config_file_path, device_type ENUM dnp3_server/iec104_server/modbus_gateway). FindingRecord (finding_id, rule_id, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, cip_requirement_ref, cvss_base float, cvss_ot_adjusted float, detail, remediation_command, config_excerpt). AuditReport (run_id, baseline_version, baseline_source ENUM embedded/external, evaluated_at, tool_version, finding_counts, findings[]). ComplianceBaseline (baseline_id, standard_ref, version, rules[]).
data.schema_notes: JSON report schema versioned with report_schema_version field; consumers should validate against published JSON Schema before ingesting into evidence management tools

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: golangci-lint, go test ./... with -race flag, govulncheck for dependency CVEs, goreleaser for cross-compilation (linux/amd64, windows/amd64, darwin/arm64), SHA256 checksum file and GitHub Release on tag push.
devops.environments: dev (local go run), ci (GitHub Actions), release (goreleaser + GitHub Releases)
devops.rollback_strategy: Tagged releases are immutable; rollback means re-installing previous release binary from GitHub Releases; no upgrade mechanism in binary itself

ISS-001.title: Rule engine baseline loading strategy underspecified for air-gapped deployments
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not define how built-in CIP baselines are embedded in the binary vs loaded from external YAML, creating risk of stale rules in air-gapped deployments.

history.note: Baseline loading left as configurable without specifying embed vs external strategy

ISS-002.title: CVSS score mapping lacks OT environmental context adjustments
ISS-002.detail: Generic CVSS scoring without OT context adjustments overstates risk for air-gapped assets and understates it for process-critical communication paths.

history.note: CVSS scores listed without OT environmental score modifiers

focus_issues.title: Rule engine baseline loading strategy underspecified for air-gapped deployments
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan did not define how built-in CIP baselines are embedded or how air-gapped updates work.

latest_recommendations: Specify CIP-007 baseline rules embedded via Go embed.FS at build time; external --rules flag accepts signed YAML bundles for air-gap update distribution
Define OT environmental CVSS modifiers in rule schema: availability impact upgraded for process-critical comm paths

previous_plan.title: SCADA CIP Audit CLI Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go CLI tool auditing SCADA communication server configs against CIP-007 baselines with JSON findings output.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Cobra CLI with rule engine loading YAML baselines from configurable path; audit, report, and rules subcommands.
previous_plan.data_model: FindingRecord, AuditReport, ComplianceBaseline structs with JSON serialization.

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-001 by specifying CIP-007 and IEC 62351 baselines embedded via Go embed.FS at build time from the baselines/ directory, ensuring air-gapped deployments always have a working baseline. External --rules flag accepts signed YAML bundles verified against an embedded ECDSA P-256 public key before load. Addressing ISS-002 with an OT environmental CVSS modifier field in the rule schema. No network calls in the binary.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined baseline embedding strategy: CIP-007 and IEC 62351 baselines compiled into binary via Go embed.FS from baselines/ directory at build time; --rules flag accepts external signed YAML bundle path; bundle ECDSA P-256 signature verified against tool's embedded public key before load; baseline_version and baseline_source (embedded/external) recorded in every audit report header for evidence traceability.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Air-gapped deployments always have a working baseline; updates distributed as signed YAML bundles via GitHub Releases alongside binary; no network calls required during audit execution

target_output.title: SCADA CIP Audit CLI - v2 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A static Go binary CLI tool enabling grid cybersecurity engineers to audit SCADA communication server configuration exports against embedded NERC CIP-007 and IEC 62351 baselines. Produces CVSS-scored JSON finding reports with OT environmental modifiers, CIP requirement cross-references, and copy-paste remediation commands. Designed for air-gapped OT workstation deployment with no runtime network dependencies; baseline rule updates distributed as ECDSA-signed YAML bundles via GitHub Releases.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single static Go binary built with goreleaser for linux/amd64 and windows/amd64. Cobra command router dispatches to audit, report, and rules subcommands. CIP-007 and IEC 62351-3 baselines embedded via Go embed.FS at build time from baselines/cip007.yaml and baselines/iec62351.yaml. Rule engine evaluates each FindingCheck against parsed config using check_type dispatch: port_whitelist checks open service ports against allowed list, cipher_check validates TLS cipher suite selections, auth_mode verifies authentication method against baseline requirement, patch_level compares installed firmware version against minimum required. CVSS scoring applies ot_modifier adjustments from each rule before computing adjusted score. External --rules flag loads signed YAML bundle verified against embedded ECDSA P-256 public key.
target_output.technology_stack: Go 1.22, Cobra v1.8, embed.FS for baseline embedding, encoding/json for report serialization, golang.org/x/crypto for YAML bundle ECDSA P-256 signature verification, optional go-sqlite3 for run history behind build tag. Build: goreleaser with cross-compilation matrix (linux/amd64, windows/amd64, darwin/arm64), GitHub Actions, govulncheck for CVE scanning.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Audit Command: parse device config file, evaluate all rules in selected baseline, produce FindingRecords with CVSS base and OT-adjusted scores and copy-paste remediation commands. Rules Command: scada-audit rules list enumerates embedded and loaded baselines with version and rule count; scada-audit rules validate --rules bundle.yaml validates external bundle structure and ECDSA signature. Report Command: scada-audit report --input findings.json --format text|sarif converts JSON to human-readable summary or SARIF for security tooling integration. Batch Mode (Phase 2): --targets hosts.txt for multi-device fleet audit with aggregate summary.
target_output.system_components: Cobra CLI framework with subcommand dispatch. Config Parser modules: DnpConfigParser for DNP3 server .cfg exports, Iec104ConfigParser for IEC 60870-5-104 JSON exports, GenericConfigParser key-value fallback. Rule Engine: RuleSet loader from embed.FS or ECDSA-verified external bundle; RuleEvaluator dispatch map keyed by check_type. CVSS scorer applying ot_modifier fields. FindingBuilder constructing FindingRecord with cvss_base and cvss_ot_adjusted scores. ReportWriter: JSON report with report_schema_version and baseline_version, text formatter for stdout severity-bucketed summary, SARIF formatter for Phase 2.
target_output.workflows: Single-Device Audit: engineer runs 'scada-audit audit --config dnp3-server.cfg --baseline cip007 --output findings-2026-03-15.json'; tool parses config, evaluates 34 CIP-007 rules covering ports, services, patch level, and authentication mode; writes findings.json with per-finding CVSS base and OT-adjusted scores and remediation commands; prints summary table to stdout with critical/high/medium/low counts. Air-Gap Baseline Update: engineer downloads updated baselines-v1.2.yaml bundle from GitHub Releases to USB, transfers to OT workstation, runs 'scada-audit audit --config server.cfg --rules /usb/baselines-v1.2.yaml'; tool verifies ECDSA P-256 bundle signature against embedded public key; loads updated rules; audit report records baseline_version from bundle and baseline_source=external for CIP evidence traceability.
target_output.data_model: AuditTarget (host_id string, config_file_path string, device_type DeviceType ENUM dnp3_server/iec104_server/modbus_gateway). FindingRecord (finding_id, rule_id, title, severity Severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, cip_requirement_ref, cvss_base float64, cvss_ot_adjusted float64, detail, config_excerpt, remediation_command). AuditReport (run_id, baseline_version, baseline_source ENUM embedded/external, evaluated_at, tool_version, finding_counts map[Severity]int, findings []FindingRecord). ComplianceBaseline (baseline_id, standard_ref, version, rules []Rule). Rule (id, title, check_type CheckType, cip_requirement_ref, cvss_base float64, ot_modifier OTModifier, expected interface{}).
target_output.api_design: CLI interface: scada-audit audit --config path --baseline cip007|iec62351|custom --rules bundle.yaml --output findings.json --format json|text --verbose --no-color. Exit codes: 0=no findings, 1=medium/low findings, 2=high findings, 3=critical findings for CI pipeline integration. scada-audit rules list outputs baseline_id, version, rule_count, source. scada-audit report --input findings.json --format sarif outputs SARIF 2.1 for security tooling import.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Output files created with os.OpenFile using 0600 permissions blocking other-user read. Config file path validated with filepath.Clean against allowed directory to prevent path traversal. Config content in findings reports sanitized by stripping HTML and script tags before JSON encoding to prevent stored XSS. External baseline bundle ECDSA P-256 signature verified against embedded public key; unsigned bundles rejected with error and non-zero exit. No network calls in binary; govulncheck in CI gates releases on known CVEs in dependencies. NERC CIP-007 baseline built-in covering ports/services, patch management, and authentication checks.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: goreleaser produces linux/amd64, windows/amd64, and darwin/arm64 binaries. GitHub Actions: golangci-lint, go test -race -count=1 ./..., govulncheck, goreleaser release on git tag push. SHA256 checksums file and GPG-signed checksums.txt.sig attached to GitHub Release. Install: download binary to PATH, chmod +x on Linux. Air-gap update path: download binary plus optional baselines bundle from GitHub Releases to USB, transfer to OT workstation. No installer package, no registry entries, no update mechanism in binary.
target_output.observability: --verbose flag enables debug logging to stderr including rule evaluation decisions and config parse steps. Each audit run appends a one-line JSON entry to audit_runs.log in the output directory: run_id, evaluated_at, baseline_version, target_config_file, finding_counts by severity. Log entries enable trend analysis across audit runs without a database. --quiet flag suppresses all stdout except exit code for scripted batch use.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost: GitHub Actions free tier for CI; GitHub Releases hosts binaries. Binary size approximately 12MB for linux/amd64 with embedded baselines. Rule evaluation for a single config file completes in under 100ms. Batch mode (Phase 2) evaluates 100 devices in parallel using goroutines bounded by --concurrency flag defaulting to number of CPUs.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (5 weeks): DnpConfigParser and Iec104ConfigParser, CIP-007 embedded baseline with 34 rules via embed.FS, FindingRecord with CVSS base and OT-adjusted scoring, JSON and text report output, audit_runs.log, goreleaser CI pipeline, v1.0 GitHub Release. Phase 2 (3 weeks): external signed YAML bundle loading with ECDSA verification, IEC 62351-3 cipher suite baseline, SARIF report format, rules list and validate subcommands. Phase 3 (4 weeks): batch audit mode with goroutine pool, SQLite run history for delta reporting, CMDB integration plugin interface.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rule-driven TDD: each CIP rule has a failing test with a fixture config before implementation. golangci-lint with errcheck, gosec, and staticcheck enabled. No cgo in core binary except optional SQLite build tag. All user-visible strings in messages.go for future localization. Rule YAML schema versioned with JSON Schema; CI validates all baseline files against schema on each push.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: DNP3 and IEC 60870-5-104 config file formats vary significantly between vendor implementations; mitigation: maintain tested parser fixtures for GE, Siemens, and ABB SCADA platforms before v1.0 release. Risk: CVSS OT environmental modifiers are judgment calls; mitigation: publish modifier rationale in rule YAML comments for auditor review and override. Tradeoff: embed.FS baseline vs external file means baseline updates require new binary release; accepted because air-gap constraints already require binary distribution.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose embed.FS over external file for baseline to guarantee air-gapped deployments always have a working baseline. Selected ECDSA P-256 for bundle signing over GPG to avoid GPG installation requirement on OT workstations. SARIF output deferred to Phase 2 to avoid scope creep in v1.0 release.

reasoner_reviews.product: The CIP-006 inspection use case is well-scoped for mobile. Reliable offline operation is the most critical capability - field technicians cannot afford to lose completed checklist data if connectivity drops. Non-conformance flagging must have clear visual affordances since field technicians are not software-savvy. The compliance officer review portal for reviewing completed inspections and assigning corrective actions is essential for the full evidence workflow.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: WatermelonDB server-wins sync is sound. Round 2 resolved photo memory pressure via SAS tokens and fixed conflict resolution. The remaining ISS-002 gap was missing mobile-side CIP audit events in the data model and sync endpoint. Round 3 adds mobile_audit_events WatermelonDB model synced to inspection_audit_log with device_id, completing the forensic audit trail.
reasoner_reviews.security: CIP-006 requires all CIP data access to be logged. Rounds 1 and 2 logged server-side access but omitted mobile device access events. The mobile_audit_events queue synced to inspection_audit_log with device_id now provides complete forensic traceability for lost or stolen Intune-enrolled devices. Azure Government FedRAMP High hosting is correctly specified.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 8-hour offline constraint met by WatermelonDB local SQLite with MSAL token cached for 8 hours after last refresh. Expo managed workflow with WatermelonDB expo-sqlite plugin requires validation in CI against the exact Expo SDK version.

frontend.app_structure: Expo managed React Native app with Expo Router. Screens: (auth) Azure AD PKCE login. (tabs) active-inspections, facilities, non-conformances, profile. WatermelonDB reactive queries auto-update UI when sync completes.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Facility List: assigned substations with pending inspection count. Inspection Launcher: checklist template selector (CIP-006 R1-R4). Checklist Screen: sections with pass/fail/na items, per-item photo capture with GPS. Non-Conformance Screen: severity selector, description, multi-photo. Sync Status Bar: pending upload count and last sync timestamp.
frontend.state_management: WatermelonDB models (Inspection, ChecklistItem, PhotoEvidence, NonConformance, MobileAuditEvent) via @nozbe/watermelondb hooks. NetInfo gates sync dispatch. MSAL in Expo SecureStore token cache.
frontend.performance_notes: Photos compressed to JPEG 85% quality (<500KB) before queue insert. Sync batch limited to 20 photos per request.
frontend.frontend_risks: Expo managed workflow may lag WatermelonDB plugin releases - maintain compatibility matrix in README.

backend.service_design: Go 1.22 Gin API: /auth, /facilities, /inspections (sync), /photos (SAS token), /non-conformances, /reports.
backend.api_patterns: POST /inspections/sync accepts batched inspection records, photo metadata, non-conformances, and mobile_audit_events; returns per-record sync results. POST /photos/sas-token returns Azure Blob write SAS token (30-min expiry) for direct client upload. GET /non-conformances?facility_id= for compliance officer review.
backend.failure_handling: Sync endpoint accepts partial batch success - each record has individual result status. Failed records returned for client retry queue.

security.auth_design: Azure AD PKCE via MSAL React Native. MFA enforced by Conditional Access policy. Intune compliance checked on token refresh - non-compliant devices blocked.
security.authorization_model: field_technician: own inspections and photo upload. compliance_officer: all inspections, non-conformance assignment, evidence export. admin: facility and user management. GORM scoping enforces field_technician read restriction to assigned facilities.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: inspection_audit_log (id UUID, user_oid TEXT, device_id TEXT, action TEXT, resource_type TEXT, resource_id TEXT, source ENUM server/mobile, ip TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMP) partitioned by month. Mobile events captured in WatermelonDB mobile_audit_events model and synced to inspection_audit_log with device_id on reconnect.

data.entities: facility_sites (id UUID, name TEXT, site_type ENUM transmission_substation/generation_control, nerc_registered_entity_id TEXT, region TEXT, latitude REAL, longitude REAL). inspection_checklists (id UUID, facility_id FK, technician_azure_oid TEXT, status ENUM in_progress/completed/synced, sync_version INT, device_id TEXT). inspection_records (id UUID, checklist_id FK, question_id FK, response ENUM pass/fail/na/observation, notes TEXT, photo_count INT). photo_evidence (id UUID, inspection_record_id FK, blob_path TEXT, content_hash TEXT UNIQUE, latitude REAL, longitude REAL, captured_at TIMESTAMP, device_id TEXT). non_conformances (id UUID, severity ENUM minor/major/critical, description TEXT, assigned_to TEXT, status ENUM open/in_remediation/resolved, due_date DATE). technician_profiles (id UUID, azure_oid TEXT UNIQUE, display_name TEXT, assigned_facility_ids UUID[]). inspection_audit_log partitioned by month.
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Database Government for server records. WatermelonDB SQLite on mobile with WatermelonDB sync fields. Azure Blob Government container 'cip-inspection-photos' (private access, SAS tokens).
data.retention_and_deletion: Inspection records and photos 3 years per CIP-006. Mobile records deleted after successful sync confirmation.

devops.deployment_topology: AKS Azure Government East US (2 nodes Standard_D4s_v3). Go API 2 replicas HPA CPU 60%. PostgreSQL Azure Database Government. Azure Blob Government. Expo EAS Build for mobile.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps: go test ./..., Docker build ACR Government, kubectl rolling deploy maxSurge 1. Mobile: Expo EAS Build on push to main, Detox E2E on simulator.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on Go API: sync latency, photo SAS generation rate. Datadog RUM: offline duration, sync success rate. Azure Monitor for AKS health.
