target_output.title: CIP-006 Physical Security Inspection Mobile Application
target_output.executive_summary: An Expo React Native 0.73 / Go 1.22 / PostgreSQL 16 mobile application enabling utility field technicians to complete NERC CIP-006 physical security inspections offline at transmission substations. Geo-tagged JPEG photos and structured checklist responses queue in WatermelonDB SQLite and sync to a Go API on Azure Government AKS on reconnect. Azure AD MFA and Intune device compliance enforce CIP-appropriate access control. Round 3 resolves ISS-002 by adding WatermelonDB mobile_audit_events queue synced to server inspection_audit_log with device_id for complete CIP forensic coverage.
target_output.architecture_overview: Expo React Native client operates offline-first using WatermelonDB backed by encrypted SQLite (key from Expo SecureStore). Field technicians capture checklist responses and geo-tagged JPEGs that queue locally for up to 8 hours. On cellular reconnect, app dispatches batch sync to Go Gin API on Azure Government AKS: inspection records sync via POST /inspections/sync with server-wins conflict resolution (server sync_version increment, stale client edits rejected); photos upload directly to Azure Blob Government via SAS tokens bypassing server memory; mobile_audit_events (inspection opened, photo viewed, checklist submitted on-device) included in sync batch and persisted to inspection_audit_log with device_id for CIP forensic traceability.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.73, Expo SDK 51, TypeScript, Expo Router v3, WatermelonDB 0.27 with expo-sqlite, Expo Camera and Location, MSAL React Native for Azure AD PKCE. Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, GORM v2, golang-jwt with Azure AD JWKS. Database: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Database Government. Storage: Azure Blob Government. Auth: Azure AD Conditional Access MFA + Intune. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines, Expo EAS Build. Monitoring: Datadog APM + RUM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: CIP-006 Checklist Engine: configurable templates per facility type (transmission substation R1-R4: perimeter integrity, access control, monitoring systems, visitor log review). Photo Evidence Capture: Expo Camera with mandatory GPS via Expo Location; JPEG 85% quality compression; content hash deduplication. Mobile Audit Event Queue: append-only WatermelonDB mobile_audit_events capturing all CIP data access on-device with device_id. Offline Queue Management: sync status bar with pending record count and last sync timestamp. Non-Conformance Workflow: severity classification, multi-photo attachment, compliance officer review queue. Evidence Package Export: PDF of completed checklist with embedded photos and GPS coordinates for CIP-006 audit submission.
target_output.system_components: Expo React Native App: WatermelonDB offline store (facility_sites, inspection_checklists, inspection_records, photo_evidence, non_conformances, mobile_audit_events append-only queue), MSAL token cache in Expo SecureStore, Expo Camera + Location, NetInfo-triggered sync manager. Go API (AKS 2 replicas): Gin routes /auth /facilities /inspections /photos /non-conformances /reports, Azure AD JWKS validation middleware, GORM PostgreSQL ORM, inspection audit logger. PostgreSQL 16 Azure Database Government: server-authoritative records, inspection_audit_log partitioned by month. Azure Blob Government: private photo container with SAS token generation. Datadog APM + RUM.
target_output.workflows: On-Site Inspection Workflow: Technician authenticates via Azure AD PKCE (MSAL token cached 8 hours). Selects assigned substation (pre-cached in WatermelonDB). Launches CIP-006 R1 perimeter checklist. For each item: records pass/fail/na, optionally captures Expo Camera photo with GPS tag. WatermelonDB mobile_audit_events appends {action: 'inspection_section_viewed', resource_id: checklist_id, device_id, timestamp} for each section opened. Flags failing items as non-conformances with severity and description. Completes all sections, submits (status -> completed). Sync and Evidence Upload Workflow: Technician regains cellular coverage. NetInfo triggers WatermelonDB sync: batches inspection_records, photo_evidence metadata, non_conformances, and mobile_audit_events into POST /inspections/sync. Go API validates JWT and Intune device compliance claim, processes records server-wins, inserts mobile_audit_events to inspection_audit_log with device_id and source='mobile'. For each photo: Go generates Azure Blob SAS write token (30-min expiry), returns URL; Expo uploads JPEG directly to Blob bypassing API server. WatermelonDB _status fields updated on sync completion.
target_output.data_model: facility_sites (id UUID, name, site_type ENUM transmission_substation/generation_control, nerc_registered_entity_id, region, latitude REAL, longitude REAL). inspection_checklists (id UUID, facility_id FK, technician_azure_oid TEXT, status ENUM in_progress/completed/synced, sync_version INT, device_id TEXT). inspection_records (id UUID, checklist_id FK, question_id FK, response ENUM pass/fail/na/observation, notes TEXT, photo_count INT). photo_evidence (id UUID, inspection_record_id FK, blob_path TEXT, content_hash TEXT UNIQUE, latitude REAL, longitude REAL, captured_at TIMESTAMP, device_id TEXT). non_conformances (id UUID, inspection_record_id FK, severity ENUM minor/major/critical, description TEXT, assigned_to TEXT, status ENUM open/in_remediation/resolved, due_date DATE). technician_profiles (id UUID, azure_oid TEXT UNIQUE, display_name TEXT, assigned_facility_ids UUID[]). inspection_audit_log (id UUID, user_oid TEXT, device_id TEXT, action TEXT, resource_type TEXT, resource_id TEXT, source ENUM server/mobile, ip TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMP) partitioned by month. WatermelonDB mobile: mirrors above plus mobile_audit_events (id, action, resource_type, resource_id, device_id, timestamp) as append-only offline audit queue.
target_output.api_design: REST API on Azure Government AKS. POST /auth/validate validates Azure AD JWT and Intune compliance. GET /facilities returns assigned facility list. GET /facilities/{id}/checklist-templates returns CIP-006 question sets. POST /inspections/sync accepts {checklists:[...], records:[...], photos:[...], non_conformances:[...], audit_events:[...]} returns per-record sync results. POST /photos/sas-token?blob_path= returns Azure Blob SAS write token. GET /non-conformances?facility_id=&status=open for compliance review. POST /reports/evidence-package/{checklist_id} async PDF generation. All endpoints require Bearer JWT; field_technician scope restricted to assigned facilities.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD PKCE MFA enforced via Conditional Access. Intune device compliance checked on token refresh. WatermelonDB SQLite encrypted with key from Expo SecureStore (hardware-backed on supported devices). TLS 1.3 for API calls. Azure Blob private container, SAS tokens 30-min write expiry. RBAC via Azure AD app roles enforced in Go middleware. Audit trail: inspection_audit_log captures all server-side CIP data access. Mobile_audit_events queue synced to inspection_audit_log with device_id and source='mobile' on every sync - covers inspection opens, photo views, and checklist submissions on-device, enabling forensic audit of lost/stolen Intune devices by filtering inspection_audit_log by device_id. CIP-006 records retained 3 years; geo-coordinates marked CIP-restricted in API responses.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS Azure Government East US (2 nodes Standard_D4s_v3). Go API: 2 replicas HPA CPU 60% max 6. Rolling update maxSurge 1 / maxUnavailable 0. PostgreSQL Azure Database Government (4 vCPU, 16GB). Azure Blob Government lifecycle: photos >3 years to cool tier, deleted at 3.1 years. Azure DevOps: go test with race detector, Docker build ACR Government, kubectl rolling deploy. Mobile: Expo EAS Build for TestFlight (iOS) and Firebase App Distribution (Android) on push to main. Detox E2E for sync and photo capture flows. Datadog APM with dd-trace-go.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on Go API: sync_record_count, sync_conflict_count, photo_sas_latency span tags. Dashboards: sync success rate, non-conformance open count by facility, inspection completion rate, inspection_audit_log write latency. Datadog RUM: offline duration, sync success/failure, crash reports. Azure Monitor: AKS pod restarts, PostgreSQL connection pool. PagerDuty: API error >2% (P1), photo SAS generation failure rate >5% (P2).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800/month: AKS 2x Standard_D4s_v3 ~$400, Azure Database Government ~$250, Azure Blob (~50GB photos/year) ~$30, ACR ~$20, Datadog ~$100. Scales to 200 concurrent field technicians. Photo storage: 500KB avg, 20 photos/inspection, 4 inspections/technician/month at 200 technicians = 800MB/month growth.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (5 months): Azure AD PKCE MFA, WatermelonDB 8-hour offline, CIP-006 transmission substation R1-R4 checklist, geo-tagged photo capture with SAS upload, server-wins sync with client inspection lock, non-conformance flagging, mobile_audit_events queue synced to inspection_audit_log with device_id, compliance officer review portal, AKS Azure Government deployment. Phase 2 (months 6-10): Generation control room checklist templates, CIP-008 incident report integration, automated PDF evidence package generation, inspection schedule push notifications. Phase 3 (months 11-15): IoT badge reader integration, predictive inspection scheduling, offline map tile caching for substation navigation.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for React Native. Go modules with explicit versioning. WatermelonDB schema changes via explicit migrations - no auto-migration. Sync logic tested with simulated mid-sync network drop via Jest + MSAL mock. Expo SDK upgrades gated by WatermelonDB expo-sqlite compatibility check. Go endpoints use table-driven tests with PostgreSQL testcontainers. Datadog trace spans for all sync operations and SAS generation. ADR log in /docs/adr.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Expo managed workflow version lag behind WatermelonDB plugin releases. Mitigation: maintain compatibility matrix; target upgrade within 2 weeks of Expo SDK release. Risk: Intune compliance policy propagation latency (~15 min) - non-compliant devices may briefly pass token refresh. Accepted given Intune is corporate MDM standard. Tradeoff: server-wins over three-way merge - simpler and safe given client-side inspection lock prevents concurrent edits; accepted for MVP. Tradeoff: Expo managed over bare ejection - easier OTA updates at cost of native module flexibility; WatermelonDB expo-sqlite covers requirements.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Mobile audit trail resolved: WatermelonDB mobile_audit_events synced to inspection_audit_log with device_id provides CIP-006 forensic completeness. Conflict resolution confirmed: server-wins with client inspection lock. Photo upload confirmed: Azure Blob SAS token bypass eliminates API memory pressure. Expo managed workflow validated with WatermelonDB expo-sqlite plugin.

metadata.notes: Round 3 of CIP-006 physical security inspection mobile app. All issues resolved including mobile-side CIP audit trail via WatermelonDB mobile_audit_events. Strong domain specificity throughout.

profile.domain: Open-Source Grid Tooling Documentation

project_goal.value: Replace the legacy Sphinx-generated documentation site for the open-source Python CIM XML parser library with a modern Astro-based static portal featuring full-text search, versioned API references, and automated deployment on every merge.
project_goal.rationale: Developer adoption gated on documentation quality; legacy Sphinx site lacks search and has poor mobile rendering.

target_users.value: Grid software engineers, utility integration developers, and ISO/RTO tooling teams consuming the open-source Python CIM XML parser library.
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified through GitHub issue tracker and Discourse forum analytics.

project_class.rationale: No server-side runtime required; all content pre-rendered at build time and served from CDN edge nodes.

capabilities.rationale: Static content delivery with cookieless page-view analytics and automated CI/CD deployment pipeline.

complexity_level.rationale: No database, no auth, no server-side logic; complexity confined to build pipeline and MDX content authoring.

risk_level.rationale: Public read-only static site with no user data collection beyond anonymized page-view analytics.

data_sensitivity.rationale: No personal data collected; Plausible Analytics is cookieless and GDPR-exempt.

external_exposure.rationale: Documentation publicly accessible to any developer without authentication.

access_model.value: Public read-only with no authentication. CMS write access restricted to library maintainers via GitHub organization membership gating the Decap CMS OAuth flow.
access_model.rationale: Documentation must be freely accessible; authoring gated by GitHub org membership.

feature_scope.value: Versioned API reference pages auto-generated from Python docstrings, MDX-authored conceptual guides covering IEC 61970 CIM class hierarchy, changelog entries per release, downloadable example scripts, and Algolia DocSearch full-text search across all content.
feature_scope.rationale: Core developer experience features identified through community surveys.

mvp_scope.value: Astro site with migrated Sphinx RST content converted to MDX, Algolia DocSearch integration, GitHub Actions deploy to Vercel, and Plausible Analytics embedded script.
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable replacement that unblocks legacy Sphinx decommission.

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Vercel Edge TLS, CSP headers blocking inline scripts, no cookies set by site code, Dependabot for weekly npm dependency security scans.
security_baseline.rationale: Standard static site security posture for public developer documentation.

frontend_stack.rationale: Astro island architecture optimizes for zero client JS by default; MDX enables interactive code examples.

backend_stack.value: None — static site with no server-side runtime
backend_stack.rationale: All content pre-rendered at build time; no API or backend server required.

data_platform.value: No database; content sourced from MDX files in the Git repository and Decap CMS writing directly to Git via GitHub API.
data_platform.rationale: File-based content management is sufficient for a documentation site maintained via Git workflows.

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with automatic preview deployments per PR branch
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel provides zero-config Astro support, global CDN edge delivery, and preview URLs.

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data stored. Plausible Analytics retains anonymized page-view aggregates for 24 months before automatic purge.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Cookieless analytics aggregates are not subject to GDPR retention obligations.

future_scope.value: Interactive CIM IEC 61970 class hierarchy graph explorer rendered client-side; Python REPL in API reference pages via Pyodide.
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP enhancements without a server-side runtime.

constraints.value: No server-side runtime permitted. Astro build must complete under 3 minutes. Algolia DocSearch must qualify for free open-source tier.
constraints.rationale: Zero hosting budget on Vercel hobby tier; build time constraint ensures fast PR feedback loops.

observability_baseline.value: Plausible Analytics for page-view trends. Vercel deployment webhook to maintainers Slack channel on production deploy success or failure.
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight observability appropriate for a static documentation site.

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development; merge to main triggers production deploy. Feature branches get automatic Vercel preview deployments.
execution_preference.rationale: Matches open-source contribution model.

project.goal: Modernize CIM XML library documentation with search and automated deployment
project.target_users: Grid software engineers and utility integration developers

devops.environments: preview per PR, production on main merge

constraints.budget: Vercel hobby tier and Algolia DocSearch free tier

reasoner_reviews.product: Scope is well-bounded for a 4-week migration: port Sphinx RST to MDX, add Algolia DocSearch, and automate Vercel deployments. The CIM library community benefits most from improved API reference discoverability. Interactive diagram explorer should remain post-MVP.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 is the correct framework for this content profile — zero-JS-by-default keeps TTFB well under 200ms from Vercel edge nodes. The critical unresolved concern is the Algolia DocSearch crawler schedule: without a triggered re-index, new API reference pages remain undiscoverable for up to 24 hours post-deployment.
reasoner_reviews.security: The Algolia Crawler API key for triggering re-indexes must be stored exclusively in GitHub Actions secrets. CSP headers must block all inline script execution. Algolia search-only key may be committed to the repository as it is scoped read-only to a public index.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 3-minute build budget with 400-plus generated API reference pages requires Vercel build cache on the pydoc-markdown output directory.

backend.service_design: No server-side runtime. A custom Astro integration astro-cim-apidocs runs during build to invoke pydoc-markdown against the library source and emit MDX files for each public class and function. Build-time only.
backend.api_patterns: No API endpoints. Algolia DocSearch crawler indexes deployed static HTML via a triggered call to the Algolia Crawler REST API from the GitHub Actions post-deploy step.
backend.failure_handling: Build failures gate the Vercel deployment via GitHub Actions required status check. Broken MDX frontmatter or failed pydoc-markdown invocation causes a build-time error.

security.auth_design: No user authentication on the public site. Decap CMS write access authenticated via GitHub OAuth.
security.secrets_management: Algolia search-only API key committed to repository (acceptable — public read-only index). Algolia Crawler API key stored in GitHub Actions secrets only.
security.abuse_prevention: No server to abuse. Algolia rate limiting enforced at the Algolia plan tier.

data.entities: documentation_pages (MDX files in docs/guides/ keyed by slug), api_reference_sections (generated MDX per public Python class/function), changelog_entries (CHANGELOG.md parsed into Astro content collection), code_examples (.py files in examples/).
data.storage_design: All content stored as MDX and Markdown files in the Git repository. No database.
data.retention_and_deletion: Content lifecycle managed via Git history. vercel.json redirects configured for removed page slugs.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions triggers on push to main and on pull requests. Steps: checkout, bun install, Python 3.12 setup and pydoc-markdown install, bun run build with Vercel cache, Vercel CLI deploy, post-deploy Algolia Crawler API trigger.
devops.ci_cd_design: Single workflow file deploy.yml. Build step has timeout-minutes: 3 enforced. Preview deployment comment posted to PR by Vercel GitHub integration.
devops.rollback_strategy: Vercel instant rollback via vercel rollback CLI or Vercel dashboard.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 documentation site with MDX; versioned API reference auto-generated from Python docstrings at build time; Pagefind search
frontend.pages_and_flows: Getting Started, Versioned API Reference, Tutorials, Benchmark Comparisons, Contributing Guide, Changelog
frontend.ui_modules: VersionSwitcher, APIReferenceBlock (auto-generated), PagefindSearchModal, BenchmarkComparisonTable, CodeBlock with copy button
frontend.performance_notes: API reference pages pre-rendered as static HTML; Pagefind index loaded on demand; Python docstring HTML sanitized at build time to prevent XSS
frontend.accessibility_notes: API reference tables need scope attributes for row/column headers; all code examples need descriptive aria-label identifying language

ISS-001.title: Build time exceeds 3-minute constraint with full API reference generation
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan omitted build time analysis for astro-cim-apidocs. Round 2 added Vercel build cache, bringing build time within budget.

ISS-002.title: Algolia DocSearch crawler schedule not documented — new pages undiscoverable for up to 24 hours post-deploy
ISS-002.detail: The Algolia DocSearch crawler runs on Algolia internal schedule, not triggered by deployment. Merged API reference pages remain missing from search results until the next crawler pass.

focus_issues.title: Algolia DocSearch crawler schedule not documented
focus_issues.detail: New API reference pages may be absent from search results for up to 24 hours after deployment.

latest_recommendations: Add a post-deploy GitHub Actions step calling the Algolia Crawler REST API to trigger immediate re-indexing
Document search freshness SLA in CONTRIBUTING.md — expected 5-15 minute window after deploy
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: CIM XML Library Documentation Portal — Round 2

convergence_state.reason: ISS-002 unresolved across two consecutive rounds

previous_audits.summary: Solid Astro architecture respecting static-only constraints, but build time risk and search crawler timing gap not addressed.
strengths: Correct static_website constraints respected
Vercel deployment topology with preview URLs per PR is appropriate
concerns: Build time with 400-plus generated MDX pages not analyzed against 3-minute constraint
Algolia DocSearch crawler schedule not mentioned
blocking_issues: No analysis of build duration for full API reference generation
recommendations: Profile astro-cim-apidocs build time and add Vercel build cache
Trigger Algolia re-index via Crawler API as post-deploy CI step

issue_updates.title: Build time risk unaddressed
issue_updates.detail: No build time estimate or mitigation for 400-plus MDX page generation.

previous_audits.summary: Build time concern resolved with Vercel cache configuration; Algolia crawler schedule documentation still missing.
strengths: Vercel build cache configuration added resolving ISS-001
CI/CD pipeline steps now fully specified with timeout-minutes enforcement
concerns: ISS-002 (Algolia crawler schedule) not addressed in plan
recommendations: Add Algolia Crawler API POST call as post-deploy step
Document expected search freshness SLA in CONTRIBUTING.md

issue_updates.title: Algolia crawler schedule not documented
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 plan omits Algolia crawler schedule discussion.

previous_plan.title: CIM XML Library Documentation Portal — Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static documentation site for the open-source Python CIM XML parser library, replacing the legacy Sphinx site with automated Vercel deployment and build-time API reference page generation.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site deployed to Vercel Edge CDN. Content from MDX files in Git and API reference MDX generated at build time by astro-cim-apidocs invoking pydoc-markdown.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: bun install, Python env, Astro build with Vercel cache, Vercel CLI deploy. Preview per PR, production on main merge.
previous_plan.risks_and_tradeoffs: Algolia DocSearch crawler timing creates post-deploy search staleness window of up to 24 hours — not yet addressed.

best_plan.title: CIM XML Library Documentation Portal — Round 2
best_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static documentation site for the open-source Python CIM XML parser library, replacing the legacy Sphinx site with automated Vercel deployment and build-time API reference page generation.
best_plan.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site deployed to Vercel Edge CDN. Content from MDX files in Git and API reference MDX generated at build time by astro-cim-apidocs invoking pydoc-markdown.
best_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: bun install, Python env, Astro build with Vercel cache, Vercel CLI deploy. Preview per PR, production on main merge.
best_plan.risks_and_tradeoffs: Algolia DocSearch crawler timing creates post-deploy search staleness window of up to 24 hours — not yet addressed.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 targets ISS-002: Algolia DocSearch crawler schedule creates a search staleness window after each production deployment. Fix is a post-deploy GitHub Actions step calling the Algolia Crawler REST API to trigger immediate re-indexing, plus CONTRIBUTING.md documentation of the 5-15 minute search freshness SLA.

fix_report.action_taken: Added a post-deploy GitHub Actions step in deploy.yml that calls POST https://crawler.algolia.com/api/1/crawlers/{ALGOLIA_CRAWLER_ID}/reindex using ALGOLIA_CRAWLER_KEY stored in GitHub Actions secrets, triggered only on successful Vercel production deployment. Added search freshness SLA documentation to CONTRIBUTING.md noting a 5-15 minute expected index update window.
fix_report.expected_outcome: New API reference pages become searchable within 15 minutes of production deployment, eliminating the uncontrolled 24-hour staleness window.

target_output.title: CIM XML Library Documentation Portal
target_output.executive_summary: Astro 4 static documentation site for the open-source Python CIM XML parser library, delivered via Vercel Edge CDN with zero server-side runtime. The build pipeline auto-generates API reference pages from Python docstrings using the astro-cim-apidocs integration, deploys preview URLs per PR, and triggers an Algolia DocSearch crawler re-index immediately after production deployment to ensure new CIM class documentation pages are searchable within 15 minutes.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site with no server-side runtime, deployed to Vercel Edge Network for global CDN delivery with sub-200ms TTFB. Content originates from MDX files in the Git repository under docs/ and API reference MDX auto-generated at build time by the astro-cim-apidocs Astro integration. Decap CMS provides a GitHub OAuth-gated web editor for non-technical contributors. Algolia DocSearch indexes the deployed static HTML via a triggered POST to the Algolia Crawler REST API from the GitHub Actions post-deploy step. Plausible Analytics provides cookieless page-view tracking.
target_output.technology_stack: Static build: Astro 4.x with MDX, TypeScript 5, Tailwind CSS via @astrojs/tailwind. API reference generation: pydoc-markdown 4.x invoked by astro-cim-apidocs Astro integration at build time. Search: Algolia DocSearch v3 with search-only API key in client bundle. Analytics: Plausible Analytics script tag (cookieless). CMS: Decap CMS with GitHub OAuth backend. Hosting: Vercel Edge Network. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Vercel CLI action and Algolia Crawler API post-deploy trigger. Package management: bun 1.3 with bun.lock committed.
target_output.functional_feature_map: API Reference module: versioned documentation pages for every public CIM XML parser class and function, auto-generated from Python docstrings and cross-linked to source on GitHub. Conceptual Guides module: MDX-authored pages covering CIM schema overview, IEC 61970 class hierarchy, and parser usage patterns. Changelog module: CHANGELOG.md parsed into structured per-version Astro content collection entries. Code Examples module: runnable .py scripts embedded as syntax-highlighted blocks. Search: Algolia DocSearch full-text with version-scoped filtering.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge CDN serving pre-built static HTML, CSS, and JS. GitHub Actions runner executing the Astro build pipeline with Vercel build cache for pydoc-markdown output. astro-cim-apidocs custom Astro integration generating API reference MDX files into src/content/api/ at build time. Algolia DocSearch crawler triggered via Algolia Crawler REST API POST from post-deploy CI step. Decap CMS SPA served from /admin path. Plausible Analytics script from plausible.io CDN.
target_output.workflows: Documentation Deployment: Maintainer merges a PR; GitHub Actions triggers, bun installs dependencies, Python 3.12 sets up pydoc-markdown, astro-cim-apidocs generates fresh API reference MDX, bun run build produces static output within the 3-minute budget using Vercel cache, Vercel CLI deploys to production, post-deploy step calls POST /api/1/crawlers/{id}/reindex on the Algolia Crawler API, Slack webhook notifies maintainers that production is live and re-index triggered. CMS Authoring: Non-technical contributor opens /admin, authenticates via GitHub OAuth, edits a conceptual guide, commits MDX changes to the repository.
target_output.data_model: documentation_pages (slug: string, title: string, mdx_content: string, version_tag: optional string, last_modified_git_commit: SHA) stored as MDX files in docs/guides/. api_reference_sections (module_path: string, class_or_function_name: string, docstring_text: string, parameters: JSON array, returns_description: string, source_line_number: int) generated as MDX in src/content/api/ at build time. changelog_entries (version: semver, release_date: ISO date, change_type: ENUM feature/fix/breaking, description: string) parsed from CHANGELOG.md. code_examples (.py files in examples/ directory).
target_output.api_design: No server-side API endpoints. Client-side integrations only: Algolia DocSearch widget calls https://[appid]-dsn.algolia.net/1/indexes/*/queries (Algolia-hosted, search-only key). Plausible Analytics script calls https://plausible.io/api/event (Plausible-hosted). GitHub Actions post-deploy step: POST https://crawler.algolia.com/api/1/crawlers/{ALGOLIA_CRAWLER_ID}/reindex with Authorization: Basic header using GitHub Actions secret. Decap CMS: GitHub OAuth flow and GitHub Contents API calls handled by Decap CMS client library at /admin.
target_output.security_and_compliance: TLS enforced by Vercel Edge. Content-Security-Policy header in vercel.json: default-src self; script-src self https://plausible.io; connect-src self https://*.algolia.net https://plausible.io; style-src self unsafe-inline. No cookies set by site code. Algolia search-only API key committed to repository — acceptable as it is scoped read-only to the public documentation index. Algolia Crawler API key stored exclusively in GitHub Actions secrets. Dependabot configured for weekly npm dependency security alerts.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow deploy.yml triggered on push to main and on pull requests. Steps: (1) actions/checkout with full history, (2) bun install --frozen-lockfile, (3) Python 3.12 setup with pip install pydoc-markdown, (4) bun run build with timeout-minutes: 3 and Vercel build cache mounted, (5) vercel deploy --prod on main branch only; preview deployment on PRs. Post-deploy step (main only): curl POST to Algolia Crawler API using ALGOLIA_CRAWLER_KEY GitHub Actions secret. Rollback: vercel rollback CLI command.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics dashboard tracks page views by documentation slug, unique visitor trends, top referrer domains. Vercel deployment webhook posts to maintainers Slack on production deploy success and failure. GitHub Actions build duration annotation per workflow run — maintainers alerted when build time approaches the 2.5-minute threshold. Algolia Crawler API re-index trigger response code logged as GitHub Actions step output; non-200 response surfaces as workflow step failure.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Hosting: zero cost on Vercel hobby tier. Algolia DocSearch: free tier for qualifying open-source projects via DocSearch program; fallback /month paid plan. Plausible Analytics: /month community plan. Build minutes: GitHub Actions free tier sufficient for 50-80 weekly deploys averaging 2.5 minutes each. Vercel Edge CDN handles arbitrary traffic spikes without configuration changes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Content Migration (Weeks 1-2): Convert Sphinx RST documentation to MDX, configure astro-cim-apidocs against library source, set up Vercel project with preview deployment, validate build time under 3-minute budget. Phase 2 — Search and Analytics (Week 3): Apply for Algolia DocSearch open-source eligibility, integrate DocSearch widget, implement post-deploy Algolia Crawler API trigger, embed Plausible Analytics, configure CSP headers. Phase 3 — CMS, Polish, and Decommission (Week 4): Deploy Decap CMS at /admin, write CONTRIBUTING.md with search freshness SLA section, validate content parity with legacy Sphinx site, decommission legacy site with 301 redirects.
target_output.development_guidelines: All MDX content files must pass astro check in CI before merge. astro-cim-apidocs generated output in src/content/api/ is gitignored and must not be hand-authored. API reference changes come exclusively from library source docstrings. Algolia search-only key may be committed; Algolia Crawler API key must be stored only in GitHub Actions secrets. bun.lock must be committed and updated via bun install.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: astro-cim-apidocs build time grows beyond 3-minute budget as CIM library API surface expands. Mitigation: Vercel build cache scoped by library source hash; incremental generation via git diff. Risk: Algolia DocSearch open-source eligibility rejection. Mitigation: Algolia paid plan fallback at /month. Tradeoff: Decap CMS over a custom admin interface — reduces development scope at the cost of limited workflow customization.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Algolia re-index timing resolved by adding a post-deploy Algolia Crawler API trigger in GitHub Actions, delivering a 5-15 minute search freshness SLA after each production deployment. Search-only Algolia key committed to repository confirmed acceptable for a public read-only documentation index. Vercel hobby tier confirmed sufficient for expected weekly deploy frequency.

metadata.notes: Static CIM XML library documentation site — round 3 resolves Algolia DocSearch crawler staleness via post-deploy re-index trigger.

profile.domain: Grid Operations — SCADA Front-End Processor Infrastructure Automation

project_goal.value: Automate provisioning, configuration, and lifecycle management of SCADA front-end processor (FEP) instances across distributed control system environments, replacing manual configuration procedures with infrastructure-as-code workflows that enforce NERC CIP-010 change management controls.
project_goal.rationale: Manual FEP configuration introduces human error risk and undocumented configuration drift across 40-plus FEP instances.

target_users.value: Grid operations DevOps engineers and SCADA administrators responsible for FEP instance configuration and lifecycle management across control center environments.
target_users.rationale: Primary operators identified in discovery sessions with the grid operations OT team.

project_class.rationale: Infrastructure automation tool driven by IaC workflows — not a web application or hosted service.

capabilities.rationale: Ansible-driven FEP provisioning, ticketing system integration, scheduled health check batch jobs, and GitLab CI IaC pipeline.

complexity_level.rationale: Ansible playbook orchestration with pre-flight checks and protocol binding configuration is complex but well-bounded.

risk_level.rationale: Misconfiguration of SCADA FEP protocol bindings could interrupt grid data acquisition, constituting an operational risk to control room situational awareness.

data_sensitivity.rationale: FEP configuration files contain communication channel parameters for SCADA substations — confidential OT asset information under NERC CIP-011 data protection requirements.

external_exposure.rationale: All tool operations confined to the internal OT network; no external network exposure.

access_model.value: Keycloak RBAC with roles: fep_operator (submit provisioning requests, view change records), fep_admin (approve and execute provisioning jobs), compliance_auditor (read-only access to change records and health check logs). MFA required for fep_admin role.
access_model.rationale: Change execution requires elevated fep_admin role approval per NERC CIP-010 change management process.

feature_scope.value: YAML-based FEP provisioning request workflow with DNPIII and IEC 60870-5-101 protocol binding configuration; Ansible playbook execution engine with pre-flight connectivity checks; scheduled health check batch jobs polling all registered FEPs; change record creation and tracking linked to ticketing system; configuration drift detection; environment inventory database of all registered FEP instances.
feature_scope.rationale: Core automation capabilities replacing the manual FEP configuration runbook.

mvp_scope.value: YAML provisioning request ingestion, Ansible playbook execution for FEP DNPIII channel configuration, pre-flight connectivity check with abort semantics, change record creation in PostgreSQL, and health check scheduled job.
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP covers the highest-frequency FEP configuration operation with CIP-010 change record compliance.

security_baseline.value: Keycloak RBAC with MFA for fep_admin, Ansible Vault for FEP credential secrets, TLS 1.3 for all internal service communication, change records immutable append-only for NERC CIP-010 audit evidence, FEP configuration files stored in encrypted GitLab repository.
security_baseline.rationale: NERC CIP-010 requires documented change management with access controls and audit evidence for BES cyber asset configurations.

frontend_stack.value: None — automation tool operated via YAML configuration files and GitLab CI pipeline triggers
frontend_stack.rationale: No web UI; operations staff interact via YAML IaC files in the Git repository and GitLab CI pipeline.

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Ansible 9 for playbook execution; FastAPI for internal REST API serving health check and change record endpoints
backend_stack.rationale: Ansible is the OT team standard for infrastructure automation; FastAPI provides a lightweight API for health check polling and ticketing system webhook callbacks.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for change records and FEP environment inventory; no caching layer required
data_platform.rationale: Relational structure needed for change record audit queries.

hosting_target.value: On-premises Kubernetes cluster in the control center OT DMZ
hosting_target.rationale: Air-gapped OT network requires on-premises hosting; Kubernetes provides container lifecycle management.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Change records retained indefinitely as NERC CIP-010 audit evidence. Health check logs retained for 3 years. FEP configuration files versioned in Git with full history.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: NERC CIP-010 requires retention of configuration change evidence for audit purposes.

future_scope.value: Automated rollback to last known-good FEP configuration on health check failure; integration with the SCADA historian for correlation of FEP configuration changes with data quality events.
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP automation of rollback reduces mean time to restore SCADA data acquisition after failed configuration changes.

constraints.value: Provisioning playbook execution must complete within 5 minutes per FEP instance. Tool must operate in the OT DMZ without internet access. FEP configuration files must never leave the air-gapped environment.
constraints.rationale: 5-minute execution budget matches the change management window. Air-gap and data localization requirements are NERC CIP compliance constraints.

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for provisioning job duration and health check success rate metrics. PagerDuty integration for health check failure alerts indicating SCADA data acquisition loss.
observability_baseline.rationale: SCADA data acquisition health is operationally critical — health check failures must alert the on-call operations team.

execution_preference.value: GitOps: FEP configuration changes committed to Git as YAML files, reviewed by fep_admin via merge request, and applied via GitLab CI pipeline on merge to main.
execution_preference.rationale: GitOps model provides the change review and audit trail required by NERC CIP-010.

compliance_context.value: NERC CIP-010 (configuration change management for BES cyber assets), IEC 62351 security standards for communication channel configuration, NERC CIP-005 (electronic security perimeter — FEP instances are protected cyber assets within the ESP).
compliance_context.rationale: FEP instances are classified as NERC CIP BES cyber assets; all configuration changes must follow CIP-010 change management process with documented evidence.

project.goal: IaC automation for SCADA FEP provisioning and lifecycle management with CIP-010 compliance
project.target_users: Grid operations DevOps engineers and SCADA administrators

backend.api: FastAPI for health check and change record REST endpoints

data.secrets: Ansible Vault encrypted in GitLab repository

constraints.network: air-gapped OT DMZ, no internet access
constraints.data_residency: all config files remain in air-gapped environment

reasoner_reviews.product: The YAML-driven provisioning workflow with GitLab CI GitOps model is well-aligned with NERC CIP-010 change management — the merge request review provides the documented approval record. The scheduled health check batch job is critical for early detection of SCADA data acquisition loss.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The Ansible-runner execution model within a Kubernetes pod is sound for the air-gapped OT environment. The critical unresolved issue from round 1 is the pre-flight connectivity check timeout behavior: if the pre-flight check fails, the playbook must abort cleanly and create a failed change record rather than proceeding with partial configuration.
reasoner_reviews.security: Ansible Vault encryption for FEP credential secrets is correct. The Keycloak MFA enforcement for fep_admin is critical. The change record immutability constraint (append-only) must be enforced at the PostgreSQL privilege level, not just application logic.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 5-minute execution budget is achievable for DNPIII channel configuration. IEC 60870-5-101 serial configuration may be slower — playbooks should use configurable timeout parameters per FEP type.

backend.service_design: Two Python services: (1) fep-api — FastAPI application exposing REST endpoints for health check status queries and change record retrieval. (2) fep-scheduler — Python CronJob running scheduled health check polls against all registered FEP instances using ansible-runner with a lightweight ping playbook.
backend.business_modules: ProvisioningJobRunner: invokes ansible-runner with the FEP provisioning playbook, checks pre-flight connectivity result, creates change_records entry on completion with abort semantics on pre-flight failure. HealthCheckPoller: queries fep_instances, runs ansible ping against each, updates communication_channels status, creates change_records for detected drift.
backend.background_jobs: Kubernetes CronJob running HealthCheckPoller every 5 minutes. Drift detection CronJob running hourly comparing deployed vs desired configuration hashes.
backend.failure_handling: Pre-flight connectivity check failure aborts playbook execution and creates a change_record with status=failed and failure_reason=pre_flight_connectivity_timeout. PagerDuty alert triggered on any change_record with status=failed for operational FEP instances.

security.auth_design: Keycloak server deployed in OT DMZ Kubernetes namespace. Three roles: fep_operator, fep_admin (MFA required), compliance_auditor.
security.secrets_management: FEP device credentials encrypted in Ansible Vault files in the GitLab repository. Vault password in Kubernetes Secret. PostgreSQL credentials in Kubernetes Secret.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All change_records entries are INSERT-only — application PostgreSQL role has INSERT and SELECT privileges only, no UPDATE or DELETE. Ansible playbook event logs persisted to provisioning_jobs.event_log_json.

data.entities: fep_instances (id UUID PK, hostname VARCHAR, site_name VARCHAR, fep_type ENUM scada_frontend/historian_connector, ip_address INET, operational_status ENUM active/maintenance/decommissioned, ansible_connect_timeout_seconds SMALLINT DEFAULT 30, desired_config_hash VARCHAR, deployed_config_hash VARCHAR, last_health_check_at TIMESTAMPTZ), communication_channels (id UUID PK, fep_id UUID FK, channel_name VARCHAR, protocol ENUM dnpiii/iec101/modbus_rtu, remote_address VARCHAR, port SMALLINT NULLABLE, status ENUM up/down/unknown), provisioning_jobs (id UUID PK, fep_id UUID FK, initiated_by_user_id VARCHAR, playbook_name VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/running/succeeded/failed, pre_flight_result ENUM passed/failed NULLABLE, failure_reason VARCHAR NULLABLE, event_log_json JSONB, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ), change_records (id UUID PK, fep_id UUID FK, provisioning_job_id UUID FK NULLABLE, change_type ENUM provisioning/configuration_drift/health_status_change, description TEXT, new_config_hash VARCHAR NULLABLE, actor_user_id VARCHAR, ticketing_ref VARCHAR NULLABLE, failure_reason VARCHAR NULLABLE, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Kubernetes StatefulSet. No external cloud storage. FEP configuration YAML files versioned in GitLab repository.
data.retention_and_deletion: change_records: INSERT-only with no purge policy — retained indefinitely as NERC CIP-010 audit evidence. provisioning_jobs.event_log_json: retained 3 years.

devops.deployment_topology: On-premises Kubernetes cluster in OT DMZ. Namespaces: fep-automation, keycloak, monitoring. GitLab CI runner deployed in fep-automation namespace.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI pipeline triggered on merge to main branch of fep-configurations repository. Stages: (1) validate — yamllint and ansible-lint, (2) pre-flight — ansible-runner dry-run (--check mode), (3) deploy — ansible-runner production execution with pre-flight abort logic, (4) verify — GET /health-checks/{fep_id} to confirm channel status.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes fep-api /metrics for provisioning_job_duration_seconds, health_check_success_ratio, configuration_drift_count. Grafana dashboard: provisioning job success rate, FEP health check heatmap by site. PagerDuty for health_check_success_ratio < 0.8 and provisioning_job status=failed.
devops.rollback_strategy: Post-MVP: automated rollback to last deployed_config_hash. MVP: manual rollback via GitLab MR revert and pipeline re-run.

ISS-001.title: Pre-flight connectivity check failure path undefined — playbook may proceed on connection timeout
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan describes a pre-flight connectivity check but does not specify the behavior when the check fails. If the playbook proceeds after a failed pre-flight, partial configuration of an operational FEP could corrupt SCADA data acquisition.

focus_issues.title: Pre-flight connectivity check failure path undefined
focus_issues.detail: Undefined behavior when pre-flight check fails — playbook may proceed with partial FEP configuration, risking SCADA data acquisition interruption.

latest_recommendations: Define explicit pre-flight failure handling: if ansible-runner ping to FEP fails, set pre_flight_result=failed, create change_record with status=failed and failure_reason, abort playbook — no FEP tasks run
Add per-FEP-type ansible_connect_timeout configuration (DNPIII TCP vs IEC 60870-5-101 serial)

best_plan.title: SCADA FEP Provisioning Automation Tool — Round 1
best_plan.executive_summary: Ansible-driven IaC automation tool for SCADA FEP provisioning in an air-gapped OT DMZ Kubernetes environment, with GitOps merge-request approval model for NERC CIP-010 compliance.
best_plan.workflows: GitLab CI pipeline: yamllint + ansible-lint, ansible dry-run, ansible execute, post-provision health check. Pre-flight connectivity check described but failure behavior not specified.
best_plan.data_model: fep_instances, communication_channels, provisioning_jobs, change_records (INSERT-only) in PostgreSQL 16.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001: the undefined pre-flight connectivity check failure path. The fix defines explicit abort semantics — if ansible-runner ping to the target FEP fails, ProvisioningJobRunner sets pre_flight_result=failed on the provisioning_jobs record, creates an immutable change_record with failure_reason=pre_flight_connectivity_failure, and halts before any FEP configuration task executes. Per-FEP-type connection timeout added to the inventory YAML schema.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined explicit pre-flight failure abort semantics in ProvisioningJobRunner: if the ansible-runner ping task returns unreachable or times out beyond ansible_connect_timeout, the job sets provisioning_jobs.pre_flight_result=failed and status=failed, creates an INSERT into change_records with change_type=provisioning and failure_reason=pre_flight_connectivity_failure, and returns exit code 2 to the GitLab CI pipeline causing the deploy stage to fail without executing any FEP configuration tasks. Added ansible_connect_timeout_seconds parameter to fep_instances YAML schema (default: 30s for TCP-based DNPIII, 60s for serial IEC 60870-5-101) configurable per FEP instance.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partial FEP configuration on unreachable hosts eliminated. Every failed pre-flight creates an immutable change_record audit entry satisfying NERC CIP-010 evidence requirements. GitLab CI pipeline fails clearly with exit code 2.

target_output.title: SCADA FEP Provisioning Automation Tool
target_output.executive_summary: Python and Ansible automation tool for grid operations DevOps teams to provision, configure, and lifecycle-manage SCADA front-end processor instances across OT control system environments via GitOps infrastructure-as-code workflows. Deployed on on-premises Kubernetes in the OT DMZ, the tool enforces NERC CIP-010 change management controls through merge-request approval, explicit pre-flight abort on connectivity failure, and immutable INSERT-only change records in PostgreSQL. Scheduled health check batch jobs detect SCADA data acquisition loss and alert the on-call operations team via PagerDuty.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two Python services deployed on on-premises Kubernetes in the OT DMZ: fep-api (FastAPI, exposing health check status and change record REST endpoints) and fep-scheduler (Python CronJob executing Ansible health check polls every 5 minutes). FEP provisioning is triggered by GitLab CI pipeline on merge to the fep-configurations repository: the pipeline invokes ansible-runner which performs a pre-flight ping with configurable per-FEP-type timeout before executing any configuration tasks. If the pre-flight fails, ProvisioningJobRunner records the failure to PostgreSQL and aborts — no configuration tasks run. All FEP device credentials are encrypted in Ansible Vault files; the vault password is mounted into the ansible-runner container from a Kubernetes Secret. PostgreSQL stores change records (INSERT-only), FEP inventory, and health check state.
target_output.technology_stack: Automation: Python 3.12, Ansible 9 with ansible-runner 2.4, ansible-lint for CI validation. API: FastAPI with SQLAlchemy 2.0 async. Auth: Keycloak 23 with TOTP MFA for fep_admin role. Data: PostgreSQL 16 on Kubernetes StatefulSet. CI/CD: GitLab CI with yamllint, ansible-lint, ansible dry-run, and production deploy stages. Container registry: local Harbor registry (air-gapped). Monitoring: Prometheus with Alertmanager, Grafana, PagerDuty via Alertmanager webhook. Secrets: Ansible Vault 2.12 for FEP credentials, Kubernetes Secrets for service credentials.
target_output.functional_feature_map: FEP Provisioning module: engineer submits a YAML configuration file with fep_hostname, protocol bindings (DNPIII or IEC 60870-5-101), channel parameters, and GitLab MR description as the CIP-010 change justification; fep_admin reviews and approves MR; GitLab CI runs yamllint, ansible-lint, dry-run, pre-flight abort check, production playbook, and post-provision channel status verification. Health Check module: Kubernetes CronJob polls all active fep_instances every 5 minutes, updates communication_channels.status, creates change_records for status transitions, triggers PagerDuty alert on channel_down. Configuration Drift Detection module: hourly CronJob compares desired_config_hash against deployed_config_hash. Change Record API module: FastAPI GET /change-records?fep_id={id} endpoints for compliance officer audit queries.
target_output.system_components: fep-api Kubernetes Deployment: FastAPI serving health check status, change record query, and ticketing system webhook endpoints. fep-scheduler Kubernetes CronJob: runs HealthCheckPoller and DriftDetector on schedule. ansible-runner sidecar container: invoked by ProvisioningJobRunner within the fep-api pod for playbook execution; mounts Ansible Vault password from Kubernetes Secret. PostgreSQL 16 StatefulSet with local PVC on OT network storage. Keycloak Deployment with LDAP integration to control center Active Directory. Prometheus and Grafana in monitoring namespace. GitLab CI runner pulling ansible-runner image from Harbor.
target_output.workflows: SCADA FEP Provisioning Workflow: DevOps engineer creates a YAML file in the fep-configurations Git repository; opens a GitLab MR with change justification; fep_admin reviews and approves; GitLab CI pipeline triggers on merge: (1) yamllint and ansible-lint validate syntax, (2) ansible-runner --check dry-run against target FEP, (3) ProvisioningJobRunner creates provisioning_jobs record with status=running, invokes ansible-runner production playbook with pre-flight ping timeout per FEP type (30s TCP / 60s serial), (4) if pre-flight fails: ProvisioningJobRunner sets pre_flight_result=failed, status=failed, inserts change_record with failure_reason=pre_flight_connectivity_failure, returns exit code 2 aborting pipeline — no configuration tasks execute, (5) if pre-flight passes: Ansible executes DNPIII or IEC 60870-5-101 binding configuration tasks, sets status=succeeded on completion, inserts change_record with new_config_hash, (6) GitLab CI verify stage calls GET /health-checks/{fep_id} to confirm channel status. Health Check Workflow: Kubernetes CronJob runs HealthCheckPoller every 5 minutes; any channel transitioning to status=down triggers an INSERT into change_records with change_type=health_status_change and a PagerDuty alert via Alertmanager.
target_output.data_model: fep_instances (id UUID PK, hostname VARCHAR UNIQUE, site_name VARCHAR, fep_type ENUM scada_frontend/historian_connector, ip_address INET, operational_status ENUM active/maintenance/decommissioned, ansible_connect_timeout_seconds SMALLINT DEFAULT 30, desired_config_hash VARCHAR, deployed_config_hash VARCHAR, last_health_check_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). communication_channels (id UUID PK, fep_id UUID FK, channel_name VARCHAR, protocol ENUM dnpiii/iec101/modbus_rtu, remote_address VARCHAR, port SMALLINT NULLABLE, status ENUM up/down/unknown, last_checked_at TIMESTAMPTZ). provisioning_jobs (id UUID PK, fep_id UUID FK, initiated_by_user_id VARCHAR, playbook_name VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/running/succeeded/failed, pre_flight_result ENUM passed/failed/skipped NULLABLE, failure_reason VARCHAR NULLABLE, event_log_json JSONB, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). change_records (id UUID PK, fep_id UUID FK, provisioning_job_id UUID FK NULLABLE, change_type ENUM provisioning/configuration_drift/health_status_change, description TEXT, new_config_hash VARCHAR NULLABLE, actor_user_id VARCHAR, ticketing_ref VARCHAR NULLABLE, failure_reason VARCHAR NULLABLE, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL — INSERT-only, no UPDATE or DELETE privilege for application role).
target_output.api_design: Internal REST API at fep-api service (cluster-internal only, no Ingress). GET /fep-instances returns inventory list with operational_status and last_health_check_at. GET /fep-instances/{id}/channels returns communication_channels status. GET /change-records?fep_id={id}&since={ISO-date} returns paginated change_records for audit queries. GET /health-checks/{id} returns current channel status snapshot for post-provision CI verification. POST /webhooks/ticketing receives change completion callbacks to update ticketing_ref on change_records. All endpoints protected by Keycloak JWT bearer token.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak RBAC with TOTP MFA enforced for fep_admin role. GitLab MR approval by fep_admin role member is the documented CIP-010 change authorization record. Ansible Vault AES-256 encryption for all FEP device credentials; vault password in Kubernetes Secret with RBAC restricting access to fep-automation service account only. change_records table: application PostgreSQL role granted INSERT and SELECT only — no UPDATE or DELETE privileges enforcing immutable audit evidence. TLS 1.3 for all Kubernetes service communication. Network policy: fep-api pods may only initiate connections to PostgreSQL StatefulSet and FEP IP range in OT network; no egress to internet. Pre-flight abort semantics: zero configuration tasks execute on an unreachable FEP, preventing partial misconfiguration of operational SCADA assets.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline stages: (1) validate — yamllint on YAML files, ansible-lint on playbooks, (2) dry-run — ansible-runner --check mode against target FEP flagged as requiring approval, (3) deploy — ansible-runner production execution via ProvisioningJobRunner with pre-flight abort logic, 5-minute timeout enforcement (Kubernetes job activeDeadlineSeconds=300), (4) verify — GET /health-checks/{fep_id} status check. Kubernetes manifests managed in a separate infrastructure repository. PostgreSQL schema managed via Alembic migrations run as Kubernetes Job on deploy. Harbor registry seeded with all required container images from air-gapped mirror procedure. Rollback: GitLab MR revert of configuration YAML commit followed by pipeline re-run.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes fep-api /metrics (custom Prometheus Python client): provisioning_job_duration_seconds histogram labeled by fep_type and outcome, health_check_success_ratio gauge per fep_id, configuration_drift_detected_total counter. Grafana dashboards: provisioning job success rate by site, FEP health check status heatmap. Alertmanager rules: health_check_success_ratio < 0.8 for any active FEP triggers PagerDuty alert; provisioning_job status=failed triggers PagerDuty alert. Structured JSON logs from fep-api and fep-scheduler with correlation_id, fep_id, and job_id fields.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: On-premises infrastructure with no cloud costs. Kubernetes cluster resource allocation: fep-api requests 0.5 CPU / 512 MB RAM, fep-scheduler requests 0.25 CPU / 256 MB RAM, PostgreSQL StatefulSet 2 CPU / 4 GB RAM with 50 GB PVC. Scaling ceiling: sized for up to 200 FEP instances; health check CronJob runtime scales linearly and completes within the 5-minute interval for up to 150 instances at 2-second average ansible ping per FEP.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): fep-api service with PostgreSQL schema, ProvisioningJobRunner with pre-flight abort semantics (ISS-001 fix), GitLab CI pipeline with yamllint, ansible-lint, dry-run, deploy, and verify stages, DNPIII channel provisioning playbook, change_records INSERT-only table, Keycloak RBAC with fep_admin MFA. Phase 2 (3 weeks): IEC 60870-5-101 serial channel provisioning playbook, per-FEP-type ansible_connect_timeout in inventory YAML, Health Check CronJob with PagerDuty alerting, Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboard. Phase 3 (1 week): Configuration drift detection CronJob, compliance_auditor role and GET /change-records audit API endpoint, ticketing system webhook integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Ansible playbook tasks for FEP configuration must be preceded by a connectivity pre-flight block that returns a machine-parseable failure if the target is unreachable — no FEP configuration task may run if pre_flight_result is not explicitly passed. yamllint and ansible-lint must pass in CI with zero warnings (--strict). change_records Alembic migration must include a grant statement GRANT INSERT, SELECT ON change_records TO fep_app_role — any migration adding UPDATE or DELETE grants will fail the CI lint check. All FEP device credentials must be Ansible Vault-encrypted — a CI check scans for plaintext passwords in YAML files.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ansible-runner Kubernetes pod OOM during concurrent provisioning if event_log_json JSONB growth is unbounded. Mitigation: cap ansible-runner event log collection to the last 500 events per job; full logs written to ephemeral volume and archived to local NAS on completion. Risk: Keycloak unavailability blocks fep_admin MFA authentication. Mitigation: Keycloak HA deployment with 2 replicas; emergency break-glass procedure documented using a local admin account with enhanced logging. Tradeoff: GitOps merge-request approval over a dedicated approval UI — reduces development scope at the cost of limited workflow customization.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Pre-flight failure abort semantics defined: unreachable FEP halts playbook before any configuration tasks, records failed change_record, exits CI pipeline with code 2. Per-FEP-type ansible_connect_timeout added to fep_instances YAML schema (30s TCP default, 60s serial). Ticketing system integration confirmed in-scope for Phase 3 via POST /webhooks/ticketing callback. Change record immutability enforced at PostgreSQL privilege level, not application logic alone.

metadata.notes: SCADA FEP provisioning automation tool — round 2 resolves pre-flight connectivity failure abort semantics for NERC CIP-010 compliant OT IaC workflows.

profile.domain: Virtual Power Plant Operations

project_goal.value: Scalable web application for VPP operator to aggregate, dispatch, and monitor distributed energy resources including residential batteries, EV chargers, and flexible HVAC loads across thousands of enrolled customer sites
project_goal.rationale: Core VPP platform enabling grid services from aggregated residential DER portfolio

target_users.value: VPP operations team, account managers, enrolled residential and C&I customers monitoring participation
target_users.rationale: Multi-tenant platform serving operator staff and enrolled end customers

project_class.rationale: Browser-based operator dashboard and customer portal with real-time data

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, integrations, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full-stack with real-time dispatch, device integrations, and analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Thousands of concurrent device connections with real-time dispatch latency requirements

risk_level.rationale: Customer energy data is personal; dispatch errors affect grid services but not safety-critical

data_sensitivity.rationale: Customer site energy telemetry and enrollment data is personal information under CCPA

external_exposure.rationale: All portal access requires Cognito authentication; no public unauthenticated endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC with roles: vpp_operator (full dispatch access), account_manager (read portfolio), customer (own site only); Cognito user pools per role group
access_model.rationale: Operators need fleet-wide dispatch; customers scoped to their enrolled sites only

feature_scope.value: DER enrollment and onboarding, real-time dispatch event management, device telemetry dashboard, portfolio analytics, settlement record generation, admin panel for customer management
feature_scope.rationale: Full VPP lifecycle from enrollment through dispatch and settlement

mvp_scope.value: DER enrollment for residential batteries and EV chargers, dispatch event creation and monitoring, real-time telemetry dashboard, capacity portfolio summary
mvp_scope.rationale: Core dispatch loop needed before settlement and analytics features

security_baseline.value: Cognito MFA for operator accounts, TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest, WAF on ALB, per-customer data isolation at query level
security_baseline.rationale: Personal data and grid services require strong auth and tenant data isolation

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; React Query for real-time data polling; Recharts for telemetry visualization

backend_stack.rationale: Async I/O for concurrent device API calls; Pydantic v2 for DER device schema validation

data_platform.value: TimescaleDB for telemetry time-series; PostgreSQL 16 for transactional data; Redis 7 for dispatch state
data_platform.rationale: TimescaleDB hypertables for efficient interval telemetry queries; Redis for low-latency dispatch coordination

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-west-2
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with auto-scaling for variable dispatch load

privacy_retention_policy.value: Customer telemetry retained 2 years; enrollment PII retained while active plus 1 year post-termination; CCPA deletion within 45 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FERC Order 2222 data requirements and CCPA consumer rights

future_scope.value: HVAC flexible load integration, ISO direct dispatch API, carbon intensity-aware dispatch scheduling
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 DER types and ISO integration expand grid service capability

constraints.value: Dispatch command delivery to 10,000 enrolled DERs within 30 seconds of event trigger; support Enphase, SolarEdge, Tesla Powerwall, and ChargePoint device APIs
constraints.rationale: Grid service SLA requires fast dispatch; named device integrations cover residential DER market

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, structured JSON logging, dispatch event completion rate and latency tracked as custom metrics, PagerDuty for dispatch failure alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Dispatch SLA monitoring is critical for grid service contract compliance

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; DER integration plugins developed in parallel with core dispatch engine
execution_preference.rationale: Parallel tracks for dispatch core and device integrations reduce critical path

compliance_context.value: FERC Order 2222 (DER market participation), CCPA data rights for California customers, CPUC Rule 21 for California interconnection
compliance_context.rationale: VPP operations in CAISO territory subject to FERC and CPUC rules

project.goal: VPP operator platform for DER aggregation, dispatch, and monitoring
project.target_users: VPP operators, account managers, enrolled customers

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for UI state

backend.api: REST with WebSocket for telemetry stream

security.data_isolation: customer_id injected from JWT claims on all queries

data.telemetry: TimescaleDB hypertables partitioned by site and hour

reasoner_reviews.product: Dispatch event management and real-time telemetry are the core value props. Settlement record generation must be accurate for ISO market participation - the billing accuracy requirement is underemphasized in Round 1.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The 30-second dispatch SLA for 10,000 DERs is the critical design constraint. Round 1 used synchronous device API calls which cannot meet this SLA at typical device API latencies of 200-500ms per call. Need async task fan-out with per-device retry and completion tracking in Redis.
reasoner_reviews.security: Customer data isolation at query level is critical with multi-tenant personal energy data. customer_id must be injected from JWT claims, never accepted from request body. CCPA deletion workflow also needs a concrete implementation path.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 30-second dispatch SLA for 10,000 devices requires parallel async dispatch with a task completion aggregator. Enphase and Tesla Powerwall APIs have different rate limits - per-vendor throttling must be addressed.

backend.service_design: FastAPI services: dispatch_service (event creation, asyncio.gather fan-out with per-vendor semaphore, Redis completion tracking), telemetry_service (device webhook ingest and polling), enrollment_service (DER onboarding), settlement_service (kWh calculation per dispatch event). Dispatch fan-out semaphores: Enphase 50, SolarEdge 30, Tesla 20, ChargePoint 40 concurrent calls.
backend.api_patterns: POST /dispatch/events, GET /dispatch/events/{id}/status streaming completion rates per vendor cohort, POST /enrollment/sites, GET /telemetry/sites/{id}/readings, POST /privacy/deletion-requests. WebSocket /ws/telemetry/{site_id} for live dashboard feeds.
backend.background_jobs: Celery workers: telemetry_ingestion_job (poll device APIs every 15 minutes), settlement_calculation_job (run after dispatch event close), ccpa_deletion_job (anonymize PII within 45 days).
backend.failure_handling: Dispatch fan-out: per-device retry with exponential backoff; devices failing after 3 retries marked dispatch_failed in Redis hash; completion rate reported as responded/total_enrolled. Dead-letter queue for permanently failed dispatch attempts.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting; operator and customer views under separate route trees with RBAC route guards
frontend.pages_and_flows: Operator: Fleet Dashboard (live telemetry map), Dispatch Console (create and monitor events with fan-out progress), Portfolio Analytics (historical capacity delivered). Customer: My Site Dashboard, Participation History, Incentive Credits.
frontend.state_management: React Query for server state with 5-second refetch interval for telemetry; Zustand for dispatch console local state; WebSocket context provider for live telemetry stream

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools: operators pool with MFA required, customers pool with MFA optional; JWT claims include role and customer_id (Cognito sub)
security.authorization_model: FastAPI Depends(get_current_customer) injects customer_id from JWT sub claim into all data queries; operators bypass customer scope via Depends(get_operator_or_customer) returning scope=all for vpp_operator role
security.privacy_controls: POST /privacy/deletion-requests queues Celery ccpa_deletion_job that anonymizes enrollment PII and purges telemetry_readings for the customer_id within 45-day SLA

data.entities: der_devices (id UUID, site_id UUID, device_type ENUM battery/ev_charger/hvac, vendor ENUM enphase/solar_edge/tesla/chargepoint, external_device_id, capacity_kw, enrolled_at, status). enrolled_sites (id UUID, customer_id UUID, address_encrypted, total_capacity_kw, enrollment_status). dispatch_events (id UUID, trigger_at, close_at, target_capacity_kw, iso_signal_ref, status, actual_delivered_kw). telemetry_readings (site_id, device_id, metric ENUM soc_pct/power_kw/setpoint_kw, value, recorded_at) TimescaleDB hypertable. settlement_records (id, dispatch_event_id, site_id, delivered_kwh, incentive_amount_cents, calculated_at).
data.storage_design: TimescaleDB hypertable on telemetry_readings partitioned by 1-hour chunks with site_id dimension index; Redis hash dispatch_event:{id}:completions storing per-device success/fail/pending bitmap for fan-out tracking

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: api_service 2-8 tasks CPU autoscaling, celery_worker 2-6 tasks queue-depth autoscaling, TimescaleDB on RDS Multi-AZ, Redis on ElastiCache, ALB with WAF, CloudFront for React SPA assets
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy, pytest 80% coverage, Docker build ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with health check gate; React: npm test, build, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on API and Celery containers; custom metrics: dispatch_completion_rate, dispatch_p99_latency_ms per vendor, telemetry_ingest_lag_seconds; PagerDuty alert on completion_rate < 90% or p99 > 25s

ISS-001.title: Dispatch fan-out cannot meet 30-second SLA with synchronous device API calls
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan called device APIs synchronously per dispatch event, which cannot complete 10,000 calls within 30 seconds given typical device API latencies of 200-500ms per call.

history.note: dispatch_service called device APIs in sequential loop

ISS-002.title: Customer data isolation not enforced at service layer - horizontal privilege escalation risk
ISS-002.detail: FastAPI endpoints in Round 1 plan accepted customer_id as a query parameter without injection from auth token, allowing horizontal privilege escalation between customer accounts.

history.note: customer_id taken from request body not JWT claim

focus_issues.title: Dispatch fan-out cannot meet 30-second SLA with synchronous device API calls
focus_issues.detail: Sequential device API calls at 200-500ms each cannot complete 10,000 dispatches in 30 seconds.

focus_issues.title: Customer data isolation not enforced - horizontal privilege escalation risk
focus_issues.detail: customer_id taken from request body enables any authenticated user to query any customer data.

latest_recommendations: Replace synchronous dispatch with asyncio.gather fan-out bounded by per-vendor semaphores; track completion in Redis bitmap
Inject customer_id exclusively from JWT claims via FastAPI Depends(get_current_customer)

concerns: Synchronous dispatch loop cannot meet 10K/30s SLA
customer_id from request body enables privilege escalation

ISS-002: customer_id from request body is a horizontal privilege escalation vulnerability
recommendations: Use asyncio.gather with per-vendor semaphore bounded concurrency
Inject customer_id from JWT claims in all FastAPI endpoint dependencies
