previous_plan.title: VPP DER Aggregation Platform Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: React/FastAPI VPP platform for dispatch and monitoring of enrolled DER portfolio across customer sites.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: React SPA, FastAPI on ECS, TimescaleDB for telemetry, PostgreSQL for transactional data, Redis for cache.
previous_plan.api_design: POST /dispatch/events, GET /dispatch/events/{id}, GET /telemetry/sites/{id}/readings with customer_id query param.

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-001 by replacing synchronous dispatch with asyncio.gather fan-out partitioned by vendor with per-vendor semaphores (Enphase 50, SolarEdge 30, Tesla 20, ChargePoint 40) and Redis bitmap per dispatch event tracking completion. Resolving ISS-002 by injecting customer_id exclusively from Cognito JWT sub claim via FastAPI Depends(get_current_customer), eliminating horizontal privilege escalation. Both fixes are required before this plan can be approved for the VPP platform serving FERC Order 2222 grid services.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced sequential device API call loop with asyncio.gather fan-out: dispatch_service creates task group partitioned by vendor; each vendor partition runs with asyncio.Semaphore(vendor_limit) bounding parallel calls; per-device completion written to Redis hash dispatch_event:{id}:completions; GET /dispatch/events/{id}/status streams completion rates per vendor cohort from Redis with 1-second polling until close_at or 100% completion.
fix_report.expected_outcome: 10,000 DER dispatch commands delivered within 25 seconds under p99 conditions at modeled vendor API latencies

fix_report.action_taken: All FastAPI handlers touching customer-scoped data use Depends(get_current_customer) which decodes Cognito JWT and returns customer_id from the sub claim; customer_id never accepted from request body or query string for data scoping; vpp_operator role uses Depends(get_operator_or_customer) returning scope=all; all data access events logged in audit table with authenticated customer_id from token.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Horizontal privilege escalation between customer accounts is impossible; operators retain fleet-wide access; audit log captures all data access with authenticated identity

target_output.title: VPP DER Aggregation Platform - v2 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale AWS-hosted web application enabling a virtual power plant operator to enroll thousands of residential DER devices, dispatch capacity events with sub-30-second fan-out to enrolled fleets via asyncio.gather with per-vendor semaphore-bounded concurrency, and monitor real-time portfolio telemetry. React 18 and FastAPI on ECS Fargate with TimescaleDB for telemetry time-series and Redis for dispatch state. Resolves async dispatch SLA design and customer data isolation to support FERC Order 2222 grid service participation.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront communicates over HTTPS to FastAPI on ECS Fargate behind ALB with WAF. Dispatch fan-out service uses asyncio.gather partitioned by vendor with per-vendor semaphores (Enphase 50, SolarEdge 30, Tesla 20, ChargePoint 40 concurrent calls) to deliver setpoint commands to up to 10,000 DER devices within a 30-second SLA. TimescaleDB hypertables on RDS store 15-minute telemetry readings partitioned by 1-hour chunks with site_id dimension index. Redis 7 on ElastiCache coordinates dispatch completion bitmaps per event and WebSocket pub-sub for live dashboard feeds. Cognito user pools enforce RBAC with customer_id scope injected exclusively from JWT sub claims at every data access point.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Recharts for telemetry charts, React Query 5-second refetch, Zustand for dispatch console state, WebSocket context for live feeds. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, asyncio with asyncio.Semaphore per vendor, Pydantic v2, SQLAlchemy 2 async, Celery for background jobs. Database: TimescaleDB on RDS (hypertables), PostgreSQL 16 (transactional), Redis 7 ElastiCache for dispatch state and pub-sub. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF OWASP rules, CloudFront, Cognito user pools.
target_output.functional_feature_map: DER Enrollment: site registration with utility account verification, device type selection (battery/EV charger), vendor API credential linking, capacity confirmation workflow. Dispatch Console: create event with target_capacity_kw and close_at, real-time fan-out progress by vendor cohort in React, early close on 100% completion. Fleet Telemetry Dashboard: live SoC and power_kw per enrolled site on 5-second refresh via WebSocket pub-sub. Portfolio Analytics: delivered capacity vs committed per dispatch event, site participation rates, top/bottom performer identification. Settlement Engine: delivered kWh per site per dispatch event, incentive credit generation, billing system export. CCPA Compliance: deletion request processing within 45 days via Celery job.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF OWASP rules fronting ECS Fargate API cluster. FastAPI API service (2-8 tasks, CPU autoscaling). Dispatch fan-out engine: asyncio.gather partitioned by vendor with semaphore pool. Redis hash dispatch_event:{id}:completions per-device bitmap. Celery worker cluster (2-6 tasks, SQS queue-depth autoscaling): telemetry_ingestion_job, settlement_calculation_job, ccpa_deletion_job. TimescaleDB RDS hypertable partitioned by 1-hour chunks with site_id dimension index. PostgreSQL 16 RDS for transactional tables. CloudFront for React SPA. Cognito user pools for operators (MFA required) and customers.
target_output.workflows: Dispatch Event Execution: VPP operator creates event via POST /dispatch/events with target_capacity_kw and close_at; backend resolves enrolled DERs by capacity using portfolio selection; dispatch_service spawns asyncio.gather fan-out grouped by vendor with per-vendor semaphore; per-device completion written to Redis hash; GET /dispatch/events/{id}/status streams completion rates per vendor cohort in real time; settlement_calculation_job queued on event close to compute delivered_kwh per site. Customer Telemetry Ingestion: Celery telemetry_ingestion_job runs every 15 minutes; for each enrolled site calls device vendor API with stored credential; normalizes SoC%, power_kw, setpoint_kw to canonical schema; bulk inserts to TimescaleDB hypertable with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency; Redis PUBLISH pushes latest readings to connected WebSocket dashboard clients.
target_output.data_model: der_devices (id UUID PK, site_id UUID FK, device_type ENUM battery/ev_charger/hvac, vendor ENUM enphase/solar_edge/tesla/chargepoint, external_device_id TEXT, capacity_kw NUMERIC, enrolled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM active/suspended/decommissioned). enrolled_sites (id UUID PK, customer_id UUID, address_encrypted TEXT, total_capacity_kw NUMERIC, enrollment_status ENUM pending/active/suspended). dispatch_events (id UUID PK, trigger_at TIMESTAMPTZ, close_at TIMESTAMPTZ, target_capacity_kw NUMERIC, iso_signal_ref TEXT, status ENUM pending/active/closed/failed, actual_delivered_kw NUMERIC). telemetry_readings (site_id UUID, device_id UUID, metric ENUM soc_pct/power_kw/setpoint_kw, value NUMERIC, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ) TimescaleDB hypertable. settlement_records (id UUID PK, dispatch_event_id UUID FK, site_id UUID FK, delivered_kwh NUMERIC, incentive_amount_cents INT, calculated_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1. POST /dispatch/events {target_capacity_kw, close_at, iso_signal_ref} -> {event_id, enrolled_count}. GET /dispatch/events/{id}/status -> {completion_rate, by_vendor: {enphase: {sent, completed, failed}}}. POST /enrollment/sites {utility_account_id, address, device_type, vendor, external_device_id}. GET /telemetry/sites/{id}/readings?metric=soc_pct&from=&to= -> timeseries. GET /portfolio/analytics?from=&to= -> delivered_capacity_summary. POST /privacy/deletion-requests {customer_id} -> {request_id, expected_completion_at}. WebSocket /ws/telemetry/{site_id} streams latest readings on 5-second Celery publish cycle. All handlers use Depends(get_current_customer) injecting customer_id from JWT sub claim.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito JWT validated at ALB and FastAPI middleware; customer_id extracted from Cognito sub claim via Depends(get_current_customer); vpp_operator role from Cognito group claim bypasses customer scope. All telemetry and enrollment queries include WHERE customer_id = :customer_id from injected scope - never from request parameters. AES-256 at rest via RDS encryption. TLS 1.3 on ALB. WAF OWASP Core Rule Set. CCPA: POST /privacy/deletion-requests queues Celery job that anonymizes enrolled_sites PII and purges telemetry_readings for customer within 45 days. FERC Order 2222 data logs retained 2 years.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate: api_service 2-8 tasks (CPU 60% scale-out), celery_worker 2-6 tasks (SQS depth scale-out). RDS Multi-AZ us-west-2 for TimescaleDB and PostgreSQL. ElastiCache Redis cluster mode 2 shards. GitHub Actions: ruff, mypy, pytest 80% coverage, Docker ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with ALB health check gate. Environments: dev (single-AZ), staging (multi-AZ), production (multi-AZ with analytics read replica). Secrets Manager for vendor API credentials with 30-day rotation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on API and Celery containers with trace propagation. Custom metrics: dispatch_completion_rate (% DERs responded per event), dispatch_p99_latency_ms per vendor, telemetry_ingest_lag_seconds per site, settlement_calculation_duration_seconds. PagerDuty: dispatch_completion_rate < 90% after 20 seconds triggers P1 alert; dispatch_p99 > 25000ms triggers P2; Celery queue depth > 500 triggers P2. CloudWatch structured JSON logs with dispatch_event_id correlation field.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Approximately $3,200/month for 10,000 enrolled sites at steady state. TimescaleDB hypertable compression after 7 days reduces storage approximately 80%. ECS auto-scaling handles dispatch spike loads. Cost review trigger at 25,000 enrolled sites to evaluate RDS instance class upgrade and Redis shard count.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Cognito auth with RBAC, DER enrollment for battery and EV charger, async dispatch fan-out for Enphase and Tesla Powerwall, basic telemetry dashboard with React Query polling, settlement record generation, GitHub Actions CI/CD. Phase 2 (6 weeks): SolarEdge and ChargePoint integrations, WebSocket live telemetry, portfolio analytics dashboard, CCPA deletion workflow, admin panel. Phase 3 (6 weeks): HVAC flexible load integration, ISO direct dispatch API connector, carbon-intensity-aware dispatch scheduling.
target_output.development_guidelines: All FastAPI handlers accessing customer data MUST use Depends(get_current_customer) or Depends(get_operator_or_customer); enforced via pre-commit hook parsing route decorators for handlers missing the dependency. asyncio dispatch fan-out unit tested with mock vendor APIs covering vendor rate limit boundary conditions. TimescaleDB hypertable queries reviewed for index usage in staging before production promotion. Pydantic v2 models for all request and response schemas; no raw dict access in handler bodies.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vendor device API instability (Tesla Powerwall rate limits, Enphase maintenance windows) degrading dispatch completion below SLA. Mitigation: per-vendor circuit breaker; dispatch SLA contract excludes vendor-attributed failures tracked in incident reports. Risk: TimescaleDB chunk bloat at scale beyond 50K sites. Mitigation: compression policy after 7 days, chunk_time_interval tuning based on query patterns from staging load tests. Tradeoff: Celery over asyncio for background jobs adds operational complexity but provides durable retry semantics for telemetry ingestion unavailable in pure asyncio.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed asyncio.gather with semaphore over Celery for dispatch fan-out due to sub-second latency requirement; Celery task overhead too high for 30-second SLA. Selected TimescaleDB over InfluxDB for SQL compatibility with existing analytics team tooling. customer_id injection from JWT confirmed as mandatory coding standard enforced by pre-commit hook.

metadata.notes: VPP DER aggregation platform revision resolving async dispatch SLA and customer data isolation vulnerabilities.

profile.domain: Wholesale Energy Markets - ISO/RTO Settlement Data Aggregation API

project_goal.value: API service aggregating and normalizing wholesale electricity market settlement data from CAISO, PJM, MISO, NYISO, and ERCOT markets into a unified query interface for energy retailers and trading desks performing billing reconciliation and imbalance charge analysis
project_goal.rationale: Eliminates per-market OASIS portal scraping by providing a single normalized settlement data API with consistent charge-code taxonomy

target_users.value: Energy retailers, power marketers, and trading desks querying settlement data for portfolio reconciliation, billing dispute resolution, and imbalance charge analysis
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of ISO settlement data for financial reconciliation workflows

project_class.rationale: Backend API with batch ingestion pipeline and analytics query layer; no end-user frontend required

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, public_api, batch_jobs, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Go API service, ClickHouse time-series, daily OASIS batch ingestion, analytics aggregation endpoints, API key auth

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-market normalization across 5 ISOs with different charge code taxonomies, 100M+ record query performance, concurrent trading client polling

risk_level.rationale: Financial settlement data used for billing reconciliation; incorrect data leads to monetary disputes; API serves regulated energy market participants

data_sensitivity.rationale: Settlement prices, volumes, and imbalance charges are financial data used in energy trading and retail billing

external_exposure.rationale: API serves authenticated energy market participants via dedicated partner accounts

access_model.value: API key with HMAC-SHA256 signing and per-account market access scopes; per-account rate limiting; IP allowlisting
access_model.rationale: Partner API pattern with granular market scope control; accounts scoped to specific ISO markets they are licensed for

feature_scope.value: Multi-ISO settlement ingestion (CAISO, PJM, MISO, NYISO, ERCOT), charge-code-level normalized query API, date-range settlement summaries, imbalance charge breakdowns, variance analysis vs. estimated invoices, bulk export to GCS, ingestion monitoring
feature_scope.rationale: Full settlement data lifecycle from ISO ingestion to retailer reconciliation queries

mvp_scope.value: CAISO and PJM ingestion, charge-code query API with HMAC auth and market scopes, date-range summary endpoint, bulk CSV export, ingestion monitoring
mvp_scope.rationale: CAISO and PJM represent highest query volume; MISO/NYISO/ERCOT in phase 2

security_baseline.value: API key HMAC-SHA256 signing with 5-minute replay window, TLS 1.3, per-account rate limiting in Redis, IP allowlisting, query audit log for FERC compliance, ClickHouse data encrypted at rest
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data requires strong auth, rate limiting against scraping, and FERC market data access audit trail

frontend_stack.value: None - API service only; Grafana for internal operational monitoring
frontend_stack.rationale: No end-user UI; Grafana for ingestion and query observability

backend_stack.rationale: Go performance for high-concurrency query serving; Go concurrency for parallel ISO OASIS fetch jobs

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for settlement time-series, Redis 7 for query cache and rate limiting, PostgreSQL 16 for account and audit metadata
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse columnar storage for 100M+ settlement records with efficient date-range aggregation; Redis for hot caching; PostgreSQL for transactional metadata

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot on GCP us-central1
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot for managed Kubernetes; GCP us-central1 for low latency to ISO OASIS endpoints

privacy_retention_policy.value: Settlement data retained 7 years; query audit logs retained 3 years; account data retained for account lifetime plus 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Financial record retention requirements for energy market participants

future_scope.value: MISO, NYISO, ERCOT ingestion, real-time preliminary settlement feeds, variance alert webhooks, FTP-push batch delivery to retailer billing systems
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 full ISO coverage and real-time preliminary data

constraints.value: ClickHouse p95 <500ms for 90-day date-range queries; 99.9% API availability; ingestion complete before 08:00 local market time daily
constraints.rationale: Trading desks require settlement data before market open for overnight reconciliation runs

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for API and ingestion metrics, structured JSON logging with correlation IDs, PagerDuty for ingestion failures and latency breaches
observability_baseline.rationale: Financial data reliability requires proactive alerting on ingestion and query SLA breaches

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development with feature flags, weekly production deploys, GitHub Actions CI
execution_preference.rationale: Financial data service requires stable controlled deploys

compliance_context.value: FERC market data transparency rules requiring query audit trail; SOC 2 Type II certification required for financial data handling
compliance_context.rationale: Energy market participants require SOC 2 Type II compliance for financial data vendors; FERC audit rights over market data access

project.goal: Unified CAISO/PJM settlement data API for energy retailer reconciliation
project.target_users: Energy retailers and trading desks

security.auth: API key HMAC-SHA256 with market scope enforcement

data.metadata_db: PostgreSQL 16 for accounts, api_keys, ingestion_jobs, query_audit_log
data.cache: Redis 7 for query result cache and rate limit counters

reasoner_reviews.product: The settlement API addresses a genuine pain point - energy retailers maintain ISO-specific OASIS scraping pipelines that break on format changes. CAISO and PJM in MVP covers the two highest-volume markets. The charge-code-level normalization is the highest-value and hardest part of the data model - CAISO and PJM use different charge code taxonomies and the canonical mapping will require iterative refinement with retail customers.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ClickHouse is correct for 100M+ settlement records with date-range aggregation. Partition key on (market, toYYYYMM(interval_end)) enables efficient date-range pruning. The ingestion jobs must handle staggered final settlement publication schedules: CAISO publishes IFM Day-Ahead at T+2 business days and RTD final at T+45; PJM at T+3. settlement_type ENUM (initial/recalculated/final) must track multiple versions of the same interval. Redis query cache keys must include account_id to prevent cross-account data leakage.
reasoner_reviews.security: Financial query audit log must capture full query parameters (markets, date range, charge codes) per query, not just account and timestamp, to satisfy FERC audit rights. API key HMAC signing must include nonce or Date header check to prevent replay attacks. Market scope enforcement must occur in ClickHouse WHERE predicates, not only at API gateway, to prevent market scope injection.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: ClickHouse p95 <500ms for 90-day ranges on 100M+ rows is achievable with partition pruning and materialized views. Ingestion-before-08:00 SLA requires 04:00 start with 4-hour buffer; alert on duration >2 hours before SLA is at risk.

backend.service_design: Three Go binaries: api-server (Gin, query and account endpoints), ingestion-worker (OASIS fetch and ClickHouse load), batch-exporter (bulk export to GCS). Shared packages: clickhouse_client, redis_cache, account_store.
backend.api_patterns: GET /v1/settlements?market=CAISO&charge_category=energy&from=2026-01-01&to=2026-03-31 paginated charge-code records. GET /v1/settlements/summary?market=PJM&from=&to= aggregated totals by charge_category. POST /v1/exports {market, date_from, date_to, format: csv|parquet} async export job. GET /v1/markets data freshness per ISO. POST /v1/admin/accounts create retailer account.
backend.business_modules: CAISO OASIS Client: fetches IFM Day-Ahead and RTD settlement XML/CSV via CAISO OASIS API. PJM Data Miner Client: fetches settlement statements via PJM Data Miner v2 REST API. Canonical Normalizer: maps CAISO 'IFM_DA_ENE' -> energy, 'AS_CAISO_PROC' -> ancillary_services; PJM 'ENERGY_IMBALANCE' -> energy, 'OPERATING_RESERVE' -> ancillary_services. ClickHouse Loader: 10k-record batch INSERT with deduplication on (market, account_id, charge_code, interval_end).
backend.scaling_notes: Ingestion workers as GKE CronJobs at 04:00 UTC. API server HPA CPU 60% target, min 3 replicas. ClickHouse query parallelism capped at 8 threads per query.

security.auth_design: API key HMAC-SHA256: client signs (method + path + Date_header + body_hash) with account signing secret; server recomputes; Date header checked within 5 minutes for replay prevention.
security.authorization_model: Each API key scoped to allowed_markets list. Go middleware validates market scope. ClickHouse queries include WHERE market IN (allowed_markets) predicate enforced server-side.
security.abuse_prevention: Redis token bucket: 1000 req/hour query endpoints, 10 req/hour export jobs. IP allowlist per account; non-allowlisted IPs return 403.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: query_audit_log (id UUID, account_id FK, markets TEXT[], charge_codes TEXT[], date_from DATE, date_to DATE, result_rows INT, latency_ms INT, ip INET, timestamp TIMESTAMP) retained 3 years.

data.entities: market_settlements (ClickHouse): market LowCardinality(String), account_id String, settlement_type Enum('initial','recalculated','final'), charge_code String, charge_category LowCardinality(String), interval_end DateTime, quantity_mwh Float64, price_per_mwh Float64, charge_amount_usd Float64 - ENGINE MergeTree PARTITION BY (market, toYYYYMM(interval_end)) ORDER BY (market, account_id, charge_code, interval_end). imbalance_records (ClickHouse): same partition scheme. mv_daily_charge_totals: materialized view for pre-aggregated daily totals. PostgreSQL: retailer_accounts, api_keys (with signing_secret_encrypted), ingestion_jobs, settlement_intervals, query_audit_log.
data.storage_design: ClickHouse: MergeTree partition pruning on (market, month). Redis: query result cache keyed by sha256(account_id+market+dates+charge_category), 30-min TTL.
data.data_risks: CAISO OASIS schema changes without notice (~2x/year) - mitigate with parse failure alerting and recorded response fixture test suite.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot us-central1: api-server Deployment (3-10 replicas HPA), ingestion-worker CronJobs (CAISO 04:00 UTC, PJM 04:30 UTC), batch-exporter Deployment. ClickHouse 3-node StatefulSet. PostgreSQL Cloud SQL 16. Redis single-node AOF.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go test -race, golangci-lint, Docker distroless build, push Artifact Registry, GKE rolling deploy with Istio canary 10%. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo on error_rate >1%.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus: API latency p50/p95/p99, ingestion job duration, rate limit rejection count. Grafana dashboards. PagerDuty: ingestion failure (P1), query p95 >500ms sustained 5 min (P2), ingestion not done by 07:00 UTC (P1).

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass architecture for a multi-ISO wholesale electricity settlement data API. Key decisions: ClickHouse columnar store partitioned by (market, month) for 100M+ records; Go for concurrency; Redis query cache with account-scoped keys to prevent cross-account leakage; HMAC-SHA256 API keys with market scope enforcement in ClickHouse WHERE predicates. Plan quality 'strong' - all sections detailed and domain-specific to ISO settlement data.
target_output.title: Multi-ISO Wholesale Electricity Settlement Data API
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 / ClickHouse / PostgreSQL API service aggregating final settlement statements from CAISO and PJM (MISO, NYISO, ERCOT in phase 2) into a normalized query API for energy retailers and trading desks. Daily batch ingestion jobs pull charge-code-level data from OASIS APIs, normalize to canonical charge taxonomy (energy, capacity, ancillary_services, transmission, uplift), and load into ClickHouse partitioned by market and month. Energy retailers query date-range summaries and imbalance charge breakdowns via HMAC-signed API keys scoped to permitted markets. Deployed on GKE Autopilot with SOC 2 Type II controls and FERC-compliant query audit logging.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-binary Go service on GKE Autopilot: api-server handles query and account endpoints; ingestion-worker CronJobs fetch CAISO OASIS and PJM Data Miner settlement data daily; batch-exporter processes bulk export requests to GCS. ClickHouse (3-node StatefulSet) stores market_settlements and imbalance_records partitioned by (market, toYYYYMM(interval_end)) with ORDER BY (market, account_id, charge_code, interval_end) for filter-then-aggregate efficiency. Frequently queried summaries cached in Redis (30-min TTL, cache keys include account_id to prevent cross-account leakage). PostgreSQL 16 (Cloud SQL) stores account metadata, API keys, ingestion job history, and the FERC-compliant financial query audit log.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin 1.9, GORM v2 for PostgreSQL, clickhouse-go v2 driver. Data: ClickHouse 23.8 LTS 3-node StatefulSet, PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL, Redis 7 AOF. Auth: API key HMAC-SHA256. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot us-central1, Cloud SQL, Artifact Registry, Istio. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: Grafana + Prometheus, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Settlement Query API: charge-code records by market/date-range/charge-category with pagination; aggregated summaries by charge category. Imbalance Charge Breakdown: interval-level imbalance records per account. Variance Analysis: settlement vs. user-supplied estimated invoice comparison with variance flags. Bulk Export: async CSV/Parquet export to GCS bucket shared with account. Market Coverage Status: last ingested settlement date and data latency per ISO. Account Management: API key provisioning, market scope, rate limits, IP allowlist.
target_output.system_components: api-server (Go Gin 3-10 replicas): HMAC auth middleware, market scope enforcer, Redis cache layer, ClickHouse query builder with scope-injection guard. ingestion-worker (GKE CronJob): CAISO OASIS XML/CSV client, PJM Data Miner REST client, canonical normalizer, 10k-batch ClickHouse loader with deduplication. batch-exporter (1 replica): async Parquet/CSV writer to GCS. ClickHouse 3-node cluster: market_settlements, imbalance_records, mv_daily_charge_totals materialized view. PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL: retailer_accounts, api_keys, ingestion_jobs, query_audit_log. Redis 7: query result cache and rate limit token buckets. Grafana + Prometheus.
target_output.workflows: Daily Settlement Ingestion: GKE CronJob triggers at 04:00 UTC (CAISO) and 04:30 UTC (PJM). Worker creates ingestion_job record (status: running). CAISO worker fetches IFM Day-Ahead for T-2 business days (initial) and T-45 (recalculated final) via CAISO OASIS API. PJM worker fetches settlement statement CSV for T-3 via Data Miner v2. Canonical normalizer maps: CAISO 'IFM_DA_ENE' -> charge_category 'energy'; PJM 'OPERATING_RESERVE' -> 'ancillary_services'. ClickHouse bulk INSERT 10k-record batches, deduplication on (market, account_id, charge_code, interval_end). Job updates status completed with records_loaded count. PagerDuty alert if not completed by 07:00 UTC. Retailer Query: Retailer sends GET /v1/settlements/summary?market=CAISO&from=2026-01-01&to=2026-03-31&charge_category=energy with HMAC signature (recomputed over method+path+Date+body_hash, Date within 5 minutes). API server validates signature, checks 'CAISO' in account allowed_markets scope. Checks Redis cache (key: sha256(account_id+CAISO+dates+energy)). Cache miss: ClickHouse query SELECT SUM(charge_amount_usd), SUM(quantity_mwh) FROM market_settlements WHERE market='CAISO' AND account_id=? AND toYYYYMM(interval_end) BETWEEN 202601 AND 202603 AND charge_category='energy' GROUP BY charge_category (partition-pruned, <200ms). Caches 30 min. Writes query_audit_log. Returns JSON.
target_output.data_model: market_settlements (ClickHouse ENGINE MergeTree): market LowCardinality(String), account_id String, settlement_type Enum('initial','recalculated','final'), charge_code String, charge_category LowCardinality(String), interval_end DateTime, quantity_mwh Float64, price_per_mwh Float64, charge_amount_usd Float64, ingested_at DateTime - PARTITION BY (market, toYYYYMM(interval_end)) ORDER BY (market, account_id, charge_code, interval_end). imbalance_records (ClickHouse): market, account_id, interval_end, imbalance_mwh, imbalance_charge_usd - same partition. mv_daily_charge_totals: materialized view aggregating daily totals by (market, account_id, charge_category, date). PostgreSQL: retailer_accounts (id UUID, name, allowed_markets TEXT[], status ENUM active/suspended). api_keys (id UUID, account_id FK, key_prefix TEXT, key_hash TEXT, signing_secret_encrypted TEXT, ip_allowlist INET[], scope TEXT[]). ingestion_jobs (id UUID, market, settlement_type, target_date, status, records_loaded, started_at, completed_at). query_audit_log (id UUID, account_id FK, markets TEXT[], date_from, date_to, charge_codes TEXT[], result_rows INT, latency_ms INT, ip INET, timestamp).
target_output.api_design: REST at https://api.settlements.example.com/v1. Auth: X-API-Key header + X-Signature: HMAC-SHA256(method+path+Date+body_hash, signing_secret) + Date within 5 minutes. GET /v1/settlements?market=CAISO&settlement_type=final&charge_code=IFM_DA_ENE&date_from=&date_to= paginated records. GET /v1/settlements/summary?market=&date_from=&date_to=&group_by=charge_category aggregated totals. GET /v1/settlements/imbalance?market=&date_from=&date_to= imbalance intervals. POST /v1/exports {market, date_from, date_to, format} returns {job_id, status_url}. GET /v1/exports/{job_id} returns status and GCS download URL. GET /v1/markets returns data freshness per ISO. POST /v1/admin/accounts (admin-only) creates retailer with market scopes.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API key HMAC-SHA256 signing: client signs (HTTP_METHOD+path+Date_header+SHA256(body)), server recomputes and validates, Date within 5 minutes prevents replay. Signing secrets encrypted AES-256 GCM in PostgreSQL, decrypted to Redis cache (5-min TTL) at validation. Market scope enforced in both API middleware AND ClickHouse WHERE market IN (allowed_markets) predicate - prevents market scope injection. Rate limiting: Redis token bucket 1000 req/hour query, 10 req/hour exports, 50 req/minute burst. IP allowlist per account; non-listed IPs return 403 before auth. TLS 1.3. query_audit_log captures full query parameters per FERC market data access audit requirements; retained 3 years. SOC 2 Type II: quarterly access reviews, query_audit_log no-delete policy, ingestion record count reconciliation against ISO published totals. ClickHouse data encrypted at rest via disk encryption on GKE persistent volumes.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot us-central1. api-server: 3 replicas HPA max 10 on CPU 60% and custom metric query_latency_p95 >400ms. PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable 2. ingestion-worker CronJobs: CAISO 04:00 UTC, PJM 04:30 UTC, activeDeadlineSeconds 14400. batch-exporter: 1 replica. ClickHouse: 3-node StatefulSet 2TB SSD, ZooKeeper ensemble. PostgreSQL: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 4vCPU 16GB with automated GCS backups and PITR. GitHub Actions: go test -race, golangci-lint, Docker multi-stage build (golang:1.22-alpine -> gcr.io/distroless/static), push Artifact Registry, GKE rolling deploy with Istio canary 10% traffic 10 minutes before full rollout. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo triggered by Prometheus alert error_rate >0.01 for 5 minutes. golang-migrate PostgreSQL migrations in GKE Job pre-upgrade hook.
target_output.observability: Prometheus: API latency p50/p95/p99 by market and endpoint, ingestion job duration and records_loaded rate, rate limit rejection count by account, ClickHouse part count and insert queue depth. Grafana dashboards for all metrics. PagerDuty: ingestion job failure (P1), query p95 >500ms sustained 5 min (P2), ingestion not done by 07:00 UTC (P1), PostgreSQL connection pool >80% (P2). Structured JSON logs with correlation IDs shipped to GCP Cloud Logging.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2200/month: GKE Autopilot api-server 3-10 pods ~$800, ClickHouse 3-node StatefulSet ~$900, Cloud SQL ~$250, Redis ~$100, Artifact Registry + egress ~$150. ClickHouse: 100M records ~50GB compressed at 0.5 bytes/row. 90-day range on 15M rows returns ~150ms with partition pruning; 3x headroom to 500ms p95 target. Scale trigger: add ClickHouse shard when compressed storage >1.5TB or p95 >350ms.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 months): CAISO and PJM ingestion, canonical charge taxonomy (energy/capacity/ancillary_services/transmission/uplift), charge-code query API with HMAC auth and scope enforcement, date-range summary endpoint, imbalance breakdown, Redis cache, FERC query audit log, GKE Autopilot deployment, Grafana monitoring. Phase 2 (months 5-8): MISO, NYISO, ERCOT ingestion adapters, variance analysis endpoint, CSV/Parquet bulk export to GCS, preliminary settlement feeds. Phase 3 (months 9-12): SOC 2 Type II audit package, FTP-push delivery to retailer billing systems, real-time CAISO RTD 5-minute settlement stream.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with explicit versioning. Table-driven tests for normalizer charge-code mappings with recorded OASIS fixture responses. ClickHouse integration tests via testcontainer. No ClickHouse queries outside clickhouse_client package - scope injection guard enforced. Cache keys always include account_id - mandatory security requirement. golang-migrate for all schema changes. ADR log in /docs/adr.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CAISO OASIS API schema changes without notice - mitigate with parse failure alerting and recorded response fixture test suite. Risk: ClickHouse StatefulSet node failure causing replication lag - mitigate with ZooKeeper-replicated table RF2, promote replica before next ingestion. Risk: FERC audit requests for query logs older than 3-year retention - mitigate with cold-tier GCS archive beyond 3 years. Tradeoff: ClickHouse self-managed vs. ClickHouse Cloud - self-managed saves ~$800/month at this scale. Tradeoff: Redis 30-min query cache vs. real-time - cache invalidation triggered by ingestion job completion per market+date range to reduce stale data window.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ClickHouse over BigQuery for columnar query performance at lower cost. Go for API concurrency and OASIS parallel fetch. Redis cache key scoped to account_id - mandatory. Preliminary settlements deferred to phase 2. Cache invalidation triggered by ingestion job completion event.

metadata.notes: First-pass architecture for multi-ISO wholesale electricity settlement data API. Strong domain specificity with ClickHouse partition strategy, CAISO/PJM OASIS integration, HMAC signing, and FERC audit requirements.

project.goal: Replace WordPress RTO outreach site with performant Astro static site
project.target_users: General public, policymakers, journalists

security.headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options via CloudFront response headers policy

data.content: Contentful CMS for articles, press releases, briefings
data.metrics: JSON files in content repo for reliability and renewable data

reasoner_reviews.product: Content model in Contentful needs careful design for the reliability metrics section - annual report summaries with PDFs and interactive chart data are different content types and should not be conflated.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro island architecture is correct - only interactive Recharts metric components need React hydration; all editorial content pages should be zero-JS HTML. Round 1 left the JSON data file schema for reliability metrics undefined, which is the main engineering risk since the data pipeline from the RTO operations database is a content team dependency.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP header configuration for Contentful image CDN and Plausible script domains needs explicit allow-listing. SRI hashes for the Plausible analytics script should be pinned and updated on each Plausible version upgrade.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse 95+ requires deferring Plausible script load and lazy-loading chart components that are not above the fold on first render.

backend.service_design: No server-side service. Contentful webhook triggers GitHub Actions rebuild. JSON data files (reliability_metrics_YYYY.json) updated via PR workflow from data team. Astro build pulls Contentful content and JSON data at build time via Contentful Delivery API.
backend.api_patterns: No runtime API. Contentful Delivery API called at Astro build time via @astrojs/contentful integration. Plausible Analytics loaded via async defer script tag with SRI hash pinned to current version.
backend.failure_handling: Contentful API failures at build time cause CI to fail and block deployment; last successful build remains live on CloudFront until new build succeeds.

data.entities: reliability_metrics_YYYY.json: {schema_version, year, report_title, pdf_s3_url, metrics: [{metric_name, value, unit, description, year_over_year_change_pct, data_category ENUM reliability/renewable/load}]}. Contentful content types: NewsArticle (title, slug, body_richtext, published_at, category), EducationalResource (title, slug, summary, download_url, audience_tag ENUM consumer/policymaker). GridMetricsPage (year, interactive_data_file_ref).
data.schema_notes: JSON data file schema versioned with schema_version field; Astro build validates against Zod schema before rendering charts to surface data format errors at build time rather than at runtime

devops.deployment_topology: S3 bucket with versioning, CloudFront OAC, Route53 CNAME, ACM certificate. CloudFront response headers policy: HSTS, CSP with enumerated domains, X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: npm run build (Astro), Lighthouse CI gate >= 95, aws s3 sync --delete, CloudFront invalidation /* on production push. Preview: Netlify deploys on PR branches for content team review.
devops.rollback_strategy: S3 versioning enabled; CloudFront rollback by re-deploying tagged build artifact from GitHub Actions cache; deployment history in GitHub Actions run history.

frontend.app_structure: Astro with React component islands for interactive charts (reliability metrics, renewable integration progress); static HTML for informational sections
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home, Reliability Metrics Dashboard, Renewable Integration Progress, Grid Modernization Projects, Public FAQ, Media Contact
frontend.ui_modules: ReliabilityMetricsChart (React island, D3), RenewableProgressBar, ProjectStatusCard, GridModernizationMap, PublicFAQAccordion
frontend.performance_notes: Chart islands lazy-hydrated on scroll; metrics data fetched from public JSON API with 15-minute browser cache; static sections render instantly
frontend.accessibility_notes: Chart components must provide data table fallback; all interactive charts keyboard-navigable with announced value changes via aria-live

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-001 by defining the reliability_metrics_YYYY.json schema explicitly with Zod validation in the Astro build pipeline, unblocking the data team's RTO operations database export pipeline. Resolving ISS-002 by enumerating Contentful CDN and Plausible script domains in the CloudFront CSP response header policy. The static_website class means no server-side runtime; all fixes are build-time or infrastructure configuration.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined reliability_metrics_YYYY.json schema: top-level fields schema_version, year, report_title, pdf_s3_url, and metrics array where each entry has metric_name, value, unit, description, year_over_year_change_pct (nullable), and data_category (ENUM reliability/renewable/load). Zod schema ReliabilityMetricsSchema defined in src/schemas/reliability-metrics.ts; Astro build-time data loader validates each JSON file against schema and throws build error on violation, blocking deployment of malformed data. Schema published to content repository README for data team pipeline specification.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Data team has concrete specification for RTO operations database export pipeline; Astro build fails fast on malformed data files before they reach production chart rendering

target_output.title: RTO Grid Reliability Public Outreach Site - v2 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-server static Astro site replacing the RTO's outdated WordPress outreach presence, delivering reliability metrics dashboards, renewable integration progress trackers, and educational resources. Served via S3 and CloudFront with Contentful for editorial CMS workflows and Plausible for cookieless analytics. Lighthouse 95+ performance enforced in CI. Defined reliability metrics JSON schema with Zod build-time validation enables the data team to build the export pipeline from the RTO operations database.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static output generated by Astro 4 at build time: editorial content fetched from Contentful Delivery API, reliability metric data loaded and Zod-validated from versioned JSON files in the content repository, and interactive chart components hydrated as React islands only on metric dashboard pages using client:visible hydration directive. No server-side runtime at any point. GitHub Actions builds on every main branch push and Contentful webhook trigger, syncs to S3, and issues CloudFront invalidation. CloudFront enforces HTTPS, HSTS, and a CSP header policy explicitly allowing cdn.contentful.com for images and plausible.io for analytics script.
target_output.technology_stack: Astro 4 with @astrojs/react for interactive islands, Recharts for reliability metric charts, Zod for build-time JSON data validation, @astrojs/contentful for CMS integration. Analytics: Plausible Analytics with async defer script load. Hosting: AWS S3 with versioning, CloudFront OAC. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI gate >= 95. No runtime server; no database.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Reliability Metrics Dashboard: interactive Recharts charts from Zod-validated reliability_metrics_YYYY.json files, year selector, downloadable PDF report links to S3. Renewable Integration Tracker: renewable capacity and generation share charts from renewables_progress_YYYY.json. News and Press Releases: Contentful NewsArticle content type rendered as static HTML pages with RSS feed. Educational Resources: Contentful EducationalResource pages tagged by audience_tag (consumer/policymaker) with downloadable PDF links. Policymaker Briefing Hub: curated collection filtered by audience_tag=policymaker.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 build process: pulls Contentful content via Delivery API at build time, loads and Zod-validates JSON data files, generates static HTML for all routes. React islands: ReliabilityMetricsChart and RenewableProgressChart with client:visible hydration to defer JS load until chart enters viewport. CloudFront distribution with OAC for S3 access and custom response headers policy. GitHub Actions workflow: npm ci, astro build, Lighthouse CI, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation. Contentful webhook triggers GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch on content publish events.
target_output.workflows: Content Update Workflow: RTO communications staff publishes a new NewsArticle in Contentful; Contentful sends webhook to GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch; build fetches all Contentful content and validates JSON data files with Zod schemas; Astro build generates static HTML; Lighthouse CI gate enforces performance >= 95; S3 sync with --delete flag; CloudFront wildcard invalidation; updated pages live within 5 minutes. Reliability Metrics Update Workflow: RTO data analyst exports reliability_metrics_2026.json from operations database using schema specification from content repository README; opens PR against content repository; CI validates JSON against ReliabilityMetricsSchema Zod definition and fails PR if any metric entry violates schema; after merge, GitHub Actions production build deploys updated charts.
target_output.data_model: reliability_metrics_YYYY.json: {schema_version: '1.0', year: int, report_title: str, pdf_s3_url: str, metrics: [{metric_name: str, value: number, unit: str, description: str, year_over_year_change_pct: number|null, data_category: 'reliability'|'renewable'|'load'}]} validated by ReliabilityMetricsSchema Zod. renewables_progress_YYYY.json: {schema_version: '1.0', year: int, entries: [{quarter: str, renewable_pct: number, wind_mw: number, solar_mw: number, hydro_mw: number}]}. Contentful content types: NewsArticle (title, slug, body_richtext, published_at, category, hero_image), EducationalResource (title, slug, summary_richtext, download_url, audience_tag ENUM consumer/policymaker), GridMetricsPage (year, json_data_file_s3_key, report_pdf_url).
target_output.api_design: No runtime API. Build-time integrations: Contentful Delivery API REST called by Astro build step. Plausible Analytics async defer script injected in Astro base layout with SRI hash pinned to current Plausible version. Astro-generated RSS feed at /rss.xml for news section syndication. Contentful webhook POST to GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch endpoint triggers rebuild. No API Gateway or Lambda functions.
target_output.security_and_compliance: CloudFront response headers policy: Strict-Transport-Security max-age=31536000 includeSubDomains; Content-Security-Policy default-src 'self', img-src 'self' images.ctfassets.net cdn.contentful.com, script-src 'self' plausible.io 'sha256-{plausible_sri_hash}', style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline', frame-ancestors 'none'; X-Frame-Options DENY; X-Content-Type-Options nosniff; Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin. SRI hash on Plausible script pinned to current version and updated via Dependabot PR on Plausible version bump. No cookies set by application; Plausible is cookieless by design. S3 bucket blocks all direct public access; CloudFront OAC only.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow: npm ci, npx astro build, lhci autorun with performance threshold 95, aws s3 sync dist/ to S3 bucket with --delete flag, aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths '/*'. Triggers: push to main branch, Contentful webhook via workflow_dispatch. S3: static website hosting disabled (CloudFront OAC only), versioning enabled for rollback. CloudFront: OAC, price class 100 (US/Europe), custom error pages for 404. DNS: Route53 CNAME to CloudFront distribution. Rollback: re-run previous successful GitHub Actions workflow using cached build artifact and re-sync to S3.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics dashboard: page views by section (reliability-metrics, news, educational-resources), top referrers, geographic breakdown, unique visitor counts. CloudFront access logs to S3 for CDN cache hit rate, bandwidth, and error rate by path prefix. Lighthouse CI score tracked in GitHub Actions run history; score regressions fail build and block deployment. No APM or error tracking required for zero-JS static pages.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Approximately $25/month: S3 storage under 1GB, CloudFront bandwidth for approximately 10K monthly visitors, Route53 hosted zone, Contentful free tier (3 users, 25K API calls/month), Plausible Analytics $9/month up to 10K pageviews, GitHub Actions free tier. Spanish language version in Phase 2 doubles S3 storage; CloudFront scales automatically with traffic growth.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (5 weeks): Astro build pipeline with Contentful integration and Zod data file validation, reliability metrics charts from JSON files, news section, GitHub Actions CI with Lighthouse gate, S3 CloudFront deployment, production launch. Phase 2 (3 weeks): educational resources section, policymaker briefing hub, RSS feed, renewable integration tracker, Plausible analytics with goal events for PDF downloads. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Spanish language version via Astro i18n routing, interactive grid map component, live reliability widget via Contentful scheduled entries.
target_output.development_guidelines: All JSON data files must pass Zod schema validation in CI before merge; enforced via npm run validate:data in GitHub Actions. React islands must use client:visible hydration directive to avoid loading chart JS before component enters viewport. No inline scripts except Plausible snippet with SRI hash. Lighthouse CI gate blocks merge if score drops below 95. Contentful content type schemas defined in TypeScript using Zod for build-time type safety.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Contentful API rate limits during high-frequency rebuild cycles if webhook triggers exceed 10 builds per hour. Mitigation: debounce webhook in GitHub Actions with minimum 5-minute interval between builds. Risk: reliability metrics JSON schema version conflicts if data team and content team push incompatible changes simultaneously. Mitigation: schema versioning with backwards-compatible additive changes only; breaking changes require major schema_version bump. Tradeoff: Astro build-time Contentful fetch means new content requires a build before going live; acceptable for editorial content update cadence.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Contentful over Sanity for editorial CMS due to existing RTO staff familiarity. Chose Plausible over GA4 to eliminate GDPR cookie consent banner requirement. Confirmed S3+CloudFront over Netlify for production to align with existing RTO AWS account governance.

project.goal: Investor landing page for 800 MW pumped hydro project
project.target_users: Institutional investors, project finance advisors

security.form_protection: reCAPTCHA v3 server-side token verification plus honeypot field
security.headers: CSP, HSTS via Vercel headers config

data.content: YAML config files for milestones and specs
data.form: Salesforce CRM API for lead creation

reasoner_reviews.product: The investor inquiry form is the primary conversion goal. Round 1 left the Salesforce Lead field mapping underspecified - the CRM team needs the mapping before campaign launch to configure lead routing and campaign tracking.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js static generation with YAML milestone config is correct. Round 1 did not specify the milestone YAML schema or specify server-side reCAPTCHA verification in the API route. Client-side-only reCAPTCHA is trivially bypassable.
reasoner_reviews.security: reCAPTCHA v3 and honeypot are correct for bot prevention. The Next.js API route MUST verify the reCAPTCHA token server-side via Google siteverify API before forwarding to Salesforce. Client-side reCAPTCHA alone does not prevent bot form submissions.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse 95+ requires the reCAPTCHA v3 script to load with async defer and only when the inquiry form section enters viewport via IntersectionObserver to avoid penalizing initial page load score.

backend.service_design: Next.js API route POST /api/inquiry: validates reCAPTCHA token via Google reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify endpoint server-side; validates form fields with Zod InquirySchema; forwards to Salesforce Lead API with field mapping; rate limiting via Vercel Edge middleware token bucket.
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/inquiry {name, email, organization, investment_interest_text, recaptcha_token, hp_field (honeypot must be empty)}. Rate limiting: 5 POSTs per IP per 10 minutes via Vercel Edge middleware. Exit behavior: return 200 success for all outcomes (bot feedback prevention); log Salesforce API failures to Vercel log drain.
backend.failure_handling: Salesforce API failure: retry once with 1-second delay; on second failure return 200 to user and log full payload to Vercel log drain for manual CRM entry. reCAPTCHA score below 0.5: log score and return 200 silently to avoid bot feedback.

data.entities: milestones.yaml: [{id, label, date ISO8601, status ENUM completed/in_progress/upcoming, description}] validated by MilestoneSchema Zod. project_specs.yaml: {capacity_mw, storage_hours, reservoir_capacity_mwh, upper_reservoir_volume_af, head_elevation_ft, turbine_units, expected_annual_generation_gwh, permitting_status, target_cod, project_name, location} validated by ProjectSpecsSchema Zod. team_profiles.yaml: [{name, title, bio, photo_filename}]. Salesforce Lead fields: FirstName, LastName, Email, Company, Description, LeadSource, Campaign_Tag__c.
data.schema_notes: Zod schemas ProjectSpecsSchema and MilestoneSchema defined in src/schemas/; Next.js getStaticProps validates YAML data at build time; CI also validates schemas

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel project linked to GitHub; automatic preview deployments on PR branches; production deployment on main branch push. Vercel KV for Edge middleware rate limit token bucket state. Custom domain with Vercel-managed SSL.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: npm ci, next build, Lighthouse CI gate >= 95, axe-core WCAG 2.1 AA check on built pages. Vercel deployment triggered automatically on GitHub push via Vercel Git integration.
devops.rollback_strategy: Vercel instant rollback to any previous deployment via Vercel dashboard; deployment history retained 30 days

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export with TypeScript; single-page investor-focused layout with anchor navigation; construction timeline as interactive SVG
frontend.pages_and_flows: Project Overview (800 MW capacity), Construction Timeline, Financial Highlights, Investor Contact/NDA Request Form
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Investor NDA request form data must be transmitted over HTTPS only; no sensitive financial projections rendered client-side without authentication gate
frontend.frontend_risks: Page may be indexed by search engines before investor round closes; implement robots noindex meta tag until public launch and verify Vercel headers

previous_plan.title: Pumped Hydro Investment Landing Page Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 static landing page for 800 MW pumped hydro project investor outreach with Salesforce inquiry form and Plausible analytics.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Next.js static generation with YAML milestone config, Vercel Edge deployment, reCAPTCHA client-side on inquiry form.

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-002 by adding server-side reCAPTCHA v3 token verification in the POST /api/inquiry route via Google siteverify API before any Salesforce forwarding. reCAPTCHA v3 script loaded with IntersectionObserver only when inquiry form enters viewport to protect Lighthouse score. Also specifying Salesforce Lead field mapping to unblock the CRM team's configuration. The landing_page class means no persistent backend; the API route is a thin serverless function.

fix_report.action_taken: POST /api/inquiry now calls https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api/siteverify with RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY Vercel environment variable and submitted token; requests with score below 0.5 silently return 200 without Salesforce forward (bot feedback prevention); RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY stored in Vercel encrypted environment variables never exposed to client. reCAPTCHA v3 script loaded with async defer via IntersectionObserver triggered when inquiry section enters viewport, protecting Lighthouse performance score.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Bot form submissions blocked server-side; Lighthouse score maintained >= 95 with deferred reCAPTCHA load; no exploitable bot feedback channel via error responses

target_output.title: Pumped Hydro Investment Landing Page - v2 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static landing page presenting the 800 MW Mountain Creek Pumped Hydro Storage project to institutional energy infrastructure investors. Features a construction timeline visualization driven by YAML milestone config, technical specifications, team profiles, and an investor inquiry form with server-side reCAPTCHA v3 verification and Salesforce Lead API integration. Deployed to Vercel Edge; Plausible cookieless analytics tracks form conversion and PDF download events.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 statically generated site deployed to Vercel Edge. All page content generated at build time from Zod-validated YAML config files (milestones.yaml, project_specs.yaml) and static assets. Single serverless API route POST /api/inquiry handles form submissions: validates reCAPTCHA token server-side via Google siteverify API, applies IP-based rate limiting via Vercel Edge middleware token bucket backed by Vercel KV, validates form body with Zod InquirySchema, then maps and forwards validated lead data to Salesforce Lead API. No persistent backend service; no database; no server-side sessions. Vercel handles HTTPS, CDN distribution, and automatic preview deployments on PR branches.
target_output.technology_stack: Next.js 14 with TypeScript (static generation via getStaticProps). Tailwind CSS for responsive styling. Framer Motion for timeline animation. Plausible Analytics self-proxied via Next.js rewrites. reCAPTCHA v3 with async defer and IntersectionObserver load. Zod for form and YAML schema validation. js-yaml for YAML parsing at build time. Vercel Edge runtime for API route and middleware. Vercel KV for rate limit token bucket state. GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI and axe-core for CI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: project name, 800 MW and 8-hour storage highlights, primary CTA scrolling to inquiry form. Project Specifications: capacity_mw, storage_hours, annual_generation_gwh, reservoir details from project_specs.yaml. Construction Timeline: animated horizontal timeline from milestones.yaml with status indicators and date labels. Team Profiles: static cards from team_profiles.yaml. Investment Thesis: narrative section with downloadable project summary PDF tracked as Plausible goal event PDF_Download. Investor Inquiry Form: name, email, organization, interest description, honeypot field, server-side reCAPTCHA v3, Salesforce Lead API.
target_output.system_components: Next.js static pages: index page with all sections as scroll-linked components. Serverless API route pages/api/inquiry.ts: reCAPTCHA siteverify, Zod InquirySchema validation, honeypot check, Salesforce Lead API POST with field mapping. Vercel Edge middleware middleware.ts: rate limit check using Vercel KV token bucket (5 POSTs per IP per 10 minutes). YAML config loader lib/config.ts: reads and validates milestones.yaml and project_specs.yaml against Zod schemas in getStaticProps. Plausible Analytics: self-proxied via Next.js rewrites to /p/js/script.js to prevent ad-blocker interference. reCAPTCHA v3: IntersectionObserver loads script only when inquiry section enters viewport.
target_output.workflows: Investor Inquiry Submission: investor fills form and submits; Next.js client validates required fields; reCAPTCHA v3 executes and appends token to POST body; POST /api/inquiry sent; Vercel Edge middleware checks rate limit token bucket in Vercel KV; API route calls Google siteverify with RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY; if score >= 0.5, validates body with Zod InquirySchema, maps to Salesforce Lead fields (FirstName+LastName from name, Email, Company from organization, Description from investment_interest_text, LeadSource='Mountain_Creek_Landing_2026', Campaign_Tag__c='series_a_2026'), POSTs to Salesforce Lead API with Bearer token; returns {success: true} regardless of Salesforce outcome to prevent information leakage. Milestone Update: project team updates milestones.yaml via GitHub PR; Vercel creates preview deployment with updated timeline; PM reviews at preview URL; PR merged to main; production deployment live within 2 minutes.
target_output.data_model: milestones.yaml: [{id: str, label: str, date: str ISO8601, status: 'completed'|'in_progress'|'upcoming', description: str}] validated by MilestoneSchema Zod. project_specs.yaml: {capacity_mw: number, storage_hours: number, reservoir_capacity_mwh: number, upper_reservoir_volume_af: number, head_elevation_ft: number, turbine_units: number, expected_annual_generation_gwh: number, permitting_status: str, target_cod: str, project_name: str, location: str} validated by ProjectSpecsSchema Zod. team_profiles.yaml: [{name, title, bio, photo_filename}]. No database. Salesforce Lead fields: FirstName, LastName, Email, Company, Description, LeadSource, Campaign_Tag__c.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/inquiry request body: {name: string, email: string, organization: string, investment_interest_text: string, recaptcha_token: string, hp_field: string (honeypot, must be empty)}. Responses: 200 {success: true} always returned regardless of outcome; 429 returned by Vercel Edge middleware for rate limit exceeded before reaching route. Internal logic: honeypot non-empty returns 200 without forward; reCAPTCHA score < 0.5 logs score and returns 200; Salesforce API error logs payload to Vercel log drain and returns 200. Plausible goal events: PDF_Download and Inquiry_Submitted tracked via plausible custom events.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Server-side reCAPTCHA v3 verification via Google siteverify API; RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY in Vercel encrypted environment variables. Honeypot field hp_field validated server-side; non-empty returns 200 silently. Rate limiting via Vercel Edge middleware token bucket in Vercel KV: 5 POST /api/inquiry per IP per 10 minutes. Salesforce access token stored in SALESFORCE_ACCESS_TOKEN Vercel environment variable; never exposed to client bundle. HTTPS enforced by Vercel; HSTS and CSP headers in next.config.js headers config: script-src 'self' www.google.com/recaptcha plausible.io 'nonce-{build_nonce}', img-src 'self' data:, frame-src www.google.com. No cookies; Plausible is cookieless.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project linked to GitHub; automatic preview on PR push; production deploy on main push. GitHub Actions: npm ci, next build, Lighthouse CI performance >= 95, axe-core WCAG 2.1 AA check. Custom domain with Vercel-managed SSL. Vercel KV on Hobby tier sufficient for landing page rate limit traffic. Rollback: Vercel dashboard instant rollback to any previous deployment retained 30 days.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics goals: Inquiry_Submitted (POST /api/inquiry success callback), PDF_Download (onclick on project summary link), CTA_Click (hero button). Plausible dashboard: page views, inquiry conversion rate, referrer sources (LinkedIn, direct, investor newsletters). Vercel deployment logs: API route outcomes logged via console.error with structured JSON including Salesforce API response code. No APM needed; Vercel Edge function invocation metrics available in Vercel dashboard.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Approximately $20/month: Vercel Pro $20 for custom domain, team members, Vercel KV for rate limiting. Plausible Analytics $9/month up to 10K pageviews. Salesforce Lead API within existing CRM subscription. GitHub Actions free tier for CI. Scales automatically via Vercel Edge CDN during investor campaign traffic spikes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (5 weeks): design and copy finalization (weeks 1-2), Next.js component build with YAML config and Zod validation (weeks 2-4), server-side reCAPTCHA and Salesforce Lead API integration, Lighthouse and axe-core CI, Vercel production launch for Series A campaign. Phase 2 (4 weeks after Series A close): password-protected investor data room via Next.js middleware auth, multilingual support (Japanese, Korean) via next-i18next, construction progress photo gallery with S3 source.
target_output.development_guidelines: reCAPTCHA secret key and Salesforce access token must never appear in client-side code or console logs; enforced via pre-commit secret scan hook. YAML config changes require Zod schema validation to pass in CI before merge. Lighthouse CI gate blocks merge if performance score drops below 95. axe-core accessibility checks block merge on WCAG 2.1 AA violations. All user-visible strings in en.json locale file for future i18n compatibility.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: reCAPTCHA v3 score thresholds may block legitimate investors submitting from corporate proxy networks with atypically low behavioral scores. Mitigation: log borderline scores (0.4-0.5) without blocking during first week; adjust threshold based on observed investor traffic patterns. Risk: Salesforce API rate limits at 100 calls/minute on standard edition may cause inquiry drops during campaign launch spike. Mitigation: silent 200 response with Vercel log drain backup allows manual CRM entry for any missed leads. Tradeoff: returning 200 on all form outcomes prevents bot feedback but requires active monitoring of Vercel logs for genuine Salesforce integration failures.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Vercel KV over Redis for rate limiting due to Vercel-native integration simplicity for landing page traffic. Selected reCAPTCHA v3 over hCaptcha for Salesforce form tracking compatibility. Salesforce Lead API chosen over Web-to-Lead form to enable custom field mapping and campaign source tagging for Series A attribution.

profile.domain: ISO/RTO Control Room Operations

project_goal.value: Real-time grid operations dashboard for ISO/RTO control room operators providing sub-second visualization of system frequency, ACE, generation dispatch, and inter-area flows with operator alerting and situation logging
project_goal.rationale: Replaces aging EMS workstation displays with modern web-based situational awareness dashboard

target_users.value: ISO/RTO control room operators, shift supervisors, and reliability coordinators monitoring the bulk electric system 24/7
target_users.rationale: Safety-critical operational users requiring sub-second data currency and high-availability access

project_class.rationale: Browser-based dashboard on control room workstations with real-time streaming data

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Real-time EMS integration, streaming telemetry, operator auth, and historical analytics

complexity_level.rationale: 1-second telemetry resolution, thousands of data points, 24/7 high-availability requirement

risk_level.rationale: Control room operational tool; availability failure impacts grid reliability operations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Real-time grid operational data is NERC CIP-011 protected operational data

external_exposure.rationale: Accessible only from control room network segment; no internet-facing endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC: operator (read all metrics, write situation_log), supervisor (all operator rights plus alert acknowledgment override), read_only (display screens); Keycloak SSO with MFA
access_model.rationale: Role segregation matches control room operational hierarchy

feature_scope.value: System frequency trend with ACE bar chart, generation dispatch by fuel type, inter-area tie-line flow tables, operator alert management, situation log with event annotation, historical playback for post-event analysis
feature_scope.rationale: Full situational awareness feature set from operations requirements

mvp_scope.value: Real-time frequency and ACE display with threshold alerts, generation dispatch summary, operator alert acknowledge workflow, basic situation log entry
mvp_scope.rationale: Core frequency and ACE monitoring needed first; tie-line flows and historical playback in Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Keycloak MFA for all users, TLS 1.3 on all internal connections, no data egress to internet, CIP-011 operational data marking, audit log of all operator actions
security_baseline.rationale: NERC CIP-011 requires operational data protection; MFA required for safety-critical systems

frontend_stack.rationale: D3.js canvas renderer for real-time frequency charts with smooth transitions; React for component lifecycle

backend_stack.rationale: Go goroutine concurrency model handles high-throughput 1-second telemetry fan-out efficiently

data_platform.value: TimescaleDB for 1-second telemetry time-series; Redis 7 for current-state cache and pub-sub; no cloud database
data_platform.rationale: TimescaleDB hypertables for efficient 1-second interval queries; Redis pub-sub for real-time WebSocket fan-out

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes in control room data center - self-hosted air-gapped
hosting_target.rationale: Control room system cannot depend on cloud connectivity; bare-metal required for latency and availability

privacy_retention_policy.value: Operational telemetry retained 2 years for NERC reliability standard compliance; situation log entries retained 7 years; no personal data collected
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: NERC data retention requirements for reliability events

future_scope.value: Tie-line flow and interchange schedule display, historical event playback, ML-based frequency anomaly scoring, multi-region dashboard federation
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features after core frequency/ACE dashboard is operational

constraints.value: Telemetry display latency under 2 seconds end-to-end from EMS scan to dashboard update; 99.9% uptime; no cloud dependency; EMS ICCP or OPC-UA adapter required
constraints.rationale: Control room SLA and connectivity constraints drive bare-metal on-premise architecture

observability_baseline.value: Grafana plus Prometheus for infrastructure metrics; custom metrics: telemetry_ingest_lag_ms, websocket_connected_clients, alert_acknowledgment_latency_ms; Alertmanager for on-premise alerting
observability_baseline.rationale: On-premise observability stack required due to air-gap constraint

execution_preference.value: Safety-critical deployment process: staging in backup control room before production; change freeze during high-risk grid conditions
execution_preference.rationale: Control room tooling requires conservative deployment aligned with grid operations schedule

compliance_context.value: NERC BAL-001 (real and reactive power balancing monitoring), NERC CIP-011 (operational data protection), NERC FAC reliability standards for situational awareness
compliance_context.rationale: ISO/RTO operations subject to NERC reliability standards and CIP data protection requirements

project.goal: Real-time grid ops dashboard for control room frequency and ACE monitoring
project.target_users: ISO/RTO operators, supervisors, reliability coordinators

frontend.charting: D3.js canvas for frequency trends; Recharts for dispatch summary

backend.api: REST for situation log and config; WebSocket for telemetry stream

security.audit: Every operator action logged to audit_events table

data.pubsub: Redis pub-sub channels per metric group for WebSocket fan-out

reasoner_reviews.product: Frequency and ACE are the highest-priority metrics for NERC BAL-001 compliance monitoring. The situation log is a legal requirement for NERC event documentation - it must be append-only with immutable timestamps. Round 1 missed this immutability requirement by including a PUT edit endpoint.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The 2-second end-to-end latency budget from EMS scan to dashboard must be broken down across the integration path: EMS ICCP scan 200-400ms, Go ingest under 50ms, Redis pub-sub under 10ms, WebSocket batch interval 500ms, D3 canvas render under 100ms. Round 1 had no latency budget analysis. WebSocket fan-out to 20+ concurrent operator workstations also needs specification.
reasoner_reviews.security: CIP-011 operational data protection requires that data access events are audit-logged. Round 1 audit log covered authentication events but missed data access events - every operator viewing situation log entries must be recorded in audit_events.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 99.9% uptime on bare-metal Kubernetes requires PodDisruptionBudgets and minimum replica counts. EMS adapter must have a circuit breaker to prevent EMS scan failures from cascading to dashboard availability.

backend.service_design: Go services: telemetry_ingest (EMS ICCP adapter polling at 1-second rate, normalizes to canonical schema, writes to TimescaleDB and Redis current-state hash, publishes to Redis pub-sub channels), websocket_hub (manages operator WebSocket connections, subscribes to Redis channels, fans out batched metric updates every 500ms via goroutine-per-client), api_service (REST for situation log, alert management, operator auth via Keycloak JWT middleware).
backend.api_patterns: WebSocket /ws/telemetry: server pushes BatchedMetricUpdate every 500ms. REST: GET /metrics/frequency?from=&to=, POST /situation-log/entries (no PUT or DELETE), PATCH /alerts/{id}/acknowledge, GET /audit-events (supervisor role only).
backend.scaling_notes: WebSocket hub: goroutine per connected client; Redis pub-sub subscription shared across all hub goroutines; message fan-out via Go channel per client. EMS adapter circuit breaker: 3 consecutive ICCP scan failures trigger stale_data alert and serve Redis last-known-good values.
backend.failure_handling: EMS circuit breaker: 3 failures trigger stale_data alert; Redis cache serves last good values during breaker open; half-open after 30 seconds. WebSocket client reconnect: exponential backoff max 5 seconds; client shows Reconnecting banner with last-update timestamp during gap.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with operator and read_only route trees; Keycloak OIDC via keycloak-js adapter; WebSocket connection manager with auto-reconnect and connection status indicator
frontend.pages_and_flows: Main Dashboard: D3.js canvas frequency chart 60-second rolling window, ACE bar with BAL-001 threshold markers, generation dispatch Recharts stacked bar by fuel type. Alert Panel: active alerts sorted by severity with acknowledge button (operator role). Situation Log: append-only entry form with category selector and NERC-reportable flag, log timeline filterable by shift.
frontend.performance_notes: D3.js frequency chart uses canvas renderer for 60-second 1 Hz window to avoid SVG DOM thrashing with 60 data points per render. WebSocket message batching: 500ms batch interval groups all changed data points reducing React re-renders.

security.auth_design: Keycloak OIDC: JWT tokens validated by Go middleware on every request; WebSocket upgrade validates JWT before connection accepted; token refresh via keycloak-js adapter before expiry
security.authorization_model: RBAC in Go middleware: operator role required for POST /situation-log/entries and PATCH /alerts/{id}/acknowledge; supervisor role required for GET /audit-events; read_only has WebSocket telemetry access only
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events table append-only with no UPDATE/DELETE grants to app service account: event_type (auth_login, alert_acknowledged, situation_log_created, data_exported), operator_id, target_resource_id, ip_address, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Situation log entries immutable after creation via PostgreSQL trigger.

data.entities: frequency_measurements (measurement_id BIGSERIAL, value_hz NUMERIC(6,4), quality_flag ENUM good/suspect/bad, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ) TimescaleDB hypertable 1-hour chunks. ace_signals (signal_id, area_id, ace_mw NUMERIC, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ) TimescaleDB hypertable. generation_units (unit_id, plant_name, fuel_type ENUM coal/gas/nuclear/wind/solar/hydro/storage, capacity_mw, current_dispatch_mw, last_updated). operator_alerts (id UUID, alert_type, severity, triggered_at, acknowledged_at, acknowledged_by). situation_log_entries (id UUID PK, entry_text, operator_id, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW, shift_id, event_category ENUM routine/abnormal/emergency, is_nerc_reportable, supersedes_entry_id FK) with immutability trigger. audit_events (id UUID PK, event_type, operator_id, target_resource_id, ip_address, occurred_at).
data.storage_design: TimescaleDB hypertables with 1-hour chunk interval and compression after 7 days. Redis hash frequency:current and ace:{area_id}:current for current-state serving. Redis pub-sub channels metrics.frequency, metrics.ace, metrics.generation for WebSocket fan-out.

devops.deployment_topology: Bare-metal k3s Kubernetes: api_service Deployment 3 replicas PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable:2, websocket_hub Deployment 3 replicas PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable:2, telemetry_ingest Deployment 2 replicas. TimescaleDB StatefulSet primary plus streaming replica. Redis Sentinel 3-node. Nginx Ingress with TLS termination.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: go vet, golangci-lint, go test -race, Docker build, Helm chart lint, deploy to staging (backup control room) with manual approval gate, production deploy with change freeze calendar integration check.
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback to previous release within 5 minutes; TimescaleDB streaming replication provides RPO under 5 seconds; forward-compatible Flyway migrations only.
