previous_plan.title: ISO/RTO Control Room Real-Time Grid Operations Dashboard
previous_plan.executive_summary: React 18/Go real-time dashboard for control room operators monitoring frequency, ACE, and generation dispatch at 1-second resolution via WebSocket streaming.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: React SPA with D3.js charts, Go Gin backend, TimescaleDB for telemetry, Redis pub-sub, Keycloak OIDC, bare-metal Kubernetes.
previous_plan.api_design: WebSocket /ws/telemetry for streaming; REST including PUT /situation-log/entries/{id} for edit, PATCH /alerts/{id}/acknowledge, GET /audit-events.
previous_plan.data_model: frequency_measurements, ace_signals, generation_units, operator_alerts, situation_log_entries with edit capability.

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-001 by removing the PUT /situation-log/entries/{id} endpoint entirely and adding a PostgreSQL trigger prevent_situation_log_mutation that raises an exception on any UPDATE or DELETE, making the table append-only at the database level. Operators who need to add context append a new entry referencing the original via supersedes_entry_id. Addressing ISS-002 with explicit latency budget decomposition across the EMS-to-dashboard pipeline. The control room context - 99.9% uptime, immutable audit records, air-gapped bare-metal - drives every architectural decision.

fix_report.action_taken: Removed PUT /situation-log/entries/{id} endpoint from API design. Added PostgreSQL trigger prevent_situation_log_mutation BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE ON situation_log_entries that raises EXCEPTION 'situation log entries are immutable'. Added supersedes_entry_id UUID FK column allowing operators to reference a prior entry when adding corrective context without editing it. All situation log access logged in audit_events with event_type=situation_log_created. App service account has INSERT only on situation_log_entries; no UPDATE/DELETE grants.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Situation log entries immutable at database level; NERC event documentation requirement satisfied; no application code path can modify historical log entries

target_output.title: ISO/RTO Control Room Grid Operations Dashboard - v2 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-availability bare-metal Kubernetes web application providing ISO/RTO control room operators with sub-2-second real-time visualization of system frequency, ACE, generation dispatch, and operator alerts via D3.js canvas charts and WebSocket streaming. Built with React 18 and Go 1.22 on on-premise infrastructure with TimescaleDB for 1-second telemetry history and Redis for current-state cache and pub-sub fan-out. NERC CIP-011 compliant with PostgreSQL-enforced immutable situation log, Keycloak MFA, and comprehensive operator action audit trail.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-layer real-time architecture with explicit latency budget: EMS Integration layer (Go ICCP adapter polls EMS at 1-second scan rate, normalizes frequency_measurements and ace_signals, writes to TimescaleDB and Redis current-state hash, publishes to Redis pub-sub; EMS delivery to Redis under 450ms), Streaming Backend layer (Go WebSocket hub subscribes to Redis channels, fans out BatchedMetricUpdate to all connected operator workstations every 500ms via goroutine-per-client; Redis to WebSocket delivery under 510ms), React Dashboard layer (D3.js canvas-based frequency chart 60-second rolling window renders under 100ms). Total p99 latency budget: EMS scan 200-400ms + ingest 50ms + Redis pub-sub 10ms + WebSocket batch 500ms + D3 render 100ms = under 1.1 seconds, well within 2-second SLA. EMS adapter circuit breaker: 3 consecutive scan failures trigger stale_data alert; Redis serves last-good values during breaker open interval.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, D3.js v7 canvas renderer for frequency chart, Recharts for generation dispatch stacked bar, keycloak-js OIDC adapter, reconnecting-websocket library. Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, gorilla/websocket, go-redis v9, pgx v5 for TimescaleDB. Database: TimescaleDB 2.14 hypertables for telemetry, PostgreSQL 16 for transactional tables including situation log, Redis 7 Sentinel for high-availability current-state and pub-sub. Infrastructure: bare-metal k3s Kubernetes, Nginx Ingress, Helm, GitLab CI, Prometheus, Grafana, Alertmanager.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Frequency Monitor: D3.js canvas chart 60-second rolling window at 1 Hz update rate; NERC BAL-001 threshold lines at 59.95 Hz and 60.05 Hz; frequency deviation annotation; quality flag indicator. ACE Dashboard: real-time ACE bar chart per control area; 10-minute integrand display for BAL-001 compliance evaluation; threshold markers. Generation Dispatch: Recharts stacked bar by fuel type (coal, gas, nuclear, wind, solar, hydro, storage); total MW vs peak load gauge. Alert Panel: active alerts sorted by severity with operator acknowledge button; time-to-acknowledge metric. Situation Log: append-only entry form with category selector (routine/abnormal/emergency), NERC-reportable flag, and supersedes_entry_id for corrections; log timeline filterable by shift.
target_output.system_components: Nginx Ingress with TLS 1.3 termination and Keycloak JWKS JWT validation. Go api_service 3 replicas: Keycloak JWT middleware, REST handlers for situation log (POST only), alerts, audit events. Go websocket_hub 3 replicas: Redis pub-sub subscriber, goroutine-per-client WebSocket fan-out with 500ms batch interval. Go telemetry_ingest 2 replicas: ICCP/OPC-UA EMS adapter with circuit breaker, canonical schema normalization, TimescaleDB bulk insert, Redis HMSET for current state, Redis PUBLISH to metric channels. TimescaleDB StatefulSet primary plus streaming replica with 1-hour chunk hypertables. PostgreSQL 16 for situation_log_entries (immutability trigger), operator_alerts, audit_events. Redis Sentinel 3-node (1 master, 2 replicas). Prometheus plus Grafana plus Alertmanager on-premise.
target_output.workflows: Real-Time Telemetry Display: Go telemetry_ingest polls EMS ICCP at 1-second scan; normalizes to FrequencyMeasurement and ACESignal structs; bulk-inserts to TimescaleDB with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; sets Redis HMSET frequency:current; publishes JSON to Redis metrics.frequency channel; websocket_hub goroutine receives via Redis subscribe, batches all changed metrics over 500ms window, marshals to BatchedMetricUpdate, sends to each connected client goroutine channel; React D3 canvas chart appends data point and redraws 60-second rolling window. Alert Acknowledgment: frequency value deviates beyond 59.95/60.05 Hz; Go api_service alert_monitor detects threshold breach, creates operator_alerts record severity=critical, publishes to Redis alerts.active; WebSocket hub fans out to all workstations; red banner with audible indicator in React; operator clicks Acknowledge, sends PATCH /alerts/{id}/acknowledge with Keycloak JWT bearing operator role; Go handler updates acknowledged_at and acknowledged_by, inserts audit_event (alert_acknowledged, operator_id, alert_id); alert cleared from panel.
target_output.data_model: frequency_measurements (measurement_id BIGSERIAL, value_hz NUMERIC(6,4), quality_flag ENUM good/suspect/bad, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL) TimescaleDB hypertable chunk_time_interval=1h, compression after 7 days. ace_signals (signal_id BIGSERIAL, area_id VARCHAR, ace_mw NUMERIC(8,2), recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL) TimescaleDB hypertable. generation_units (unit_id VARCHAR PK, plant_name, fuel_type ENUM coal/gas/nuclear/wind/solar/hydro/storage, capacity_mw, current_dispatch_mw, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). operator_alerts (id UUID PK, alert_type ENUM frequency_deviation/ace_violation/comm_loss, severity ENUM critical/high/medium, triggered_at, acknowledged_at, acknowledged_by UUID FK). situation_log_entries (id UUID PK, entry_text TEXT, operator_id UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(), shift_id UUID, event_category ENUM routine/abnormal/emergency, is_nerc_reportable BOOLEAN, supersedes_entry_id UUID FK SELF); TRIGGER prevent_situation_log_mutation BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE RAISES EXCEPTION 'immutable'. audit_events (id UUID PK, event_type ENUM auth_login/alert_acknowledged/situation_log_created/data_exported, operator_id UUID, target_resource_id UUID, ip_address INET, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1 (Keycloak JWT required on all routes). GET /metrics/frequency?from=&to=&resolution=1s|1m. GET /metrics/ace?area_id=&from=&to=. POST /situation-log/entries {entry_text, event_category, is_nerc_reportable, supersedes_entry_id?}. GET /situation-log/entries?shift_id=&category=. PATCH /alerts/{id}/acknowledge (operator role required). GET /alerts/active. GET /audit-events?from=&to= (supervisor role required). No PUT or DELETE on situation_log_entries anywhere in the API surface. WebSocket /ws/telemetry: server pushes BatchedMetricUpdate {frequency_measurements:[], ace_signals:[], active_alerts:[], generation_dispatch:{}} every 500ms.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC JWT validated at Nginx Ingress JWKS and Go middleware; WebSocket upgrade rejects connections without valid JWT. RBAC in Go middleware: operator role gates POST /situation-log/entries and PATCH /alerts/{id}/acknowledge; supervisor role gates GET /audit-events. TLS 1.3 on all Nginx connections; internal service mesh mTLS between pods. CIP-011 operational data: all telemetry endpoints require authentication; no egress to internet. Situation log immutability: PostgreSQL TRIGGER prevent_situation_log_mutation BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE RAISES EXCEPTION; app service account has INSERT grant only on situation_log_entries. audit_events: INSERT only, no UPDATE/DELETE grants to app service account. PodSecurityAdmission restricted profile on all pods. X-Data-Classification CIP-011 response header on all telemetry API responses.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal k3s Kubernetes in control room data center: api_service Deployment 3 replicas PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable:2, websocket_hub Deployment 3 replicas PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable:2, telemetry_ingest Deployment 2 replicas PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable:1. TimescaleDB StatefulSet primary plus streaming replica. Redis Sentinel 3-node. GitLab CI: go vet, golangci-lint, go test -race, Docker build, Helm lint, Helm deploy to staging (backup control room) requiring manual approval gate, production deploy with NERC change freeze calendar integration. Helm rollback to previous release within 5 minutes. Staging mirrors production topology in backup control room network segment.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes all Go services at /metrics: telemetry_ingest_lag_ms histogram (EMS scan to Redis write), websocket_connected_clients gauge, alert_acknowledgment_latency_ms histogram (triggered_at to acknowledged_at), situation_log_entries_total counter, frequency_out_of_normal_band_seconds counter (time outside 59.95-60.05 Hz). Grafana dashboards: EMS Integration Health, WebSocket Fan-Out Latency, Alert Response Times. Alertmanager rules: telemetry_ingest_lag_ms p99 > 500ms triggers ops alert; websocket_connected_clients == 0 triggers critical alert; EMS circuit breaker open triggers critical alert to on-call operator.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost: fully on-premise. Hardware amortized over 5-year lifecycle. TimescaleDB compression after 7 days reduces telemetry storage approximately 85%. Redis Sentinel requires minimum 3 bare-metal nodes for HA. WebSocket hub scales to 50 or more concurrent operator connections per replica at under 5% CPU with 500ms batch interval. Capacity trigger: add websocket_hub replica when p99 fan-out latency exceeds 200ms under production load.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Keycloak integration, EMS ICCP adapter with circuit breaker, frequency and ACE WebSocket streaming, D3 canvas charts, operator alert acknowledge workflow, immutable situation log with PostgreSQL trigger, audit_events table, Prometheus/Grafana observability, staging deployment in backup control room. Phase 2 (8 weeks): tie-line flow display, generation dispatch by fuel type, historical data query API with time-range selection, post-event playback scrubber. Phase 3 (8 weeks): ML-based frequency event anomaly scoring, multi-region dashboard federation, Keycloak group-based shift assignment for situation log shift_id tagging.
target_output.development_guidelines: No PUT or DELETE endpoints on situation_log_entries; enforced via code review checklist item and PostgreSQL trigger as belt-and-suspenders. All Go WebSocket handlers must use per-client goroutine with channel-based message delivery to avoid lock contention under concurrent connections. D3 frequency chart must use canvas renderer not SVG; enforced in component PR review checklist. Latency budget tracked in Go benchmark tests: telemetry_ingest_lag_ms p99 asserted under 100ms under simulated 1 Hz EMS scan load in CI. CIP-011 classification header X-Data-Classification: CIP-011 applied to all telemetry API responses.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EMS ICCP scan latency variability during grid emergencies when EMS is under heavy polling load may push telemetry lag beyond 2-second SLA. Mitigation: EMS adapter circuit breaker and Redis last-known-good cache ensures dashboard availability even during scan delays; stale_data quality flag displayed on affected metrics so operators know data currency. Risk: k3s node failure reducing replicas below PodDisruptionBudget minimum during a grid stress event. Mitigation: 3-node control plane and node failure runbook targeting 10-minute recovery. Tradeoff: 500ms WebSocket batch interval reduces message frequency at cost of additional display latency; accepted because it keeps end-to-end budget below 2 seconds with 900ms of headroom.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed D3.js canvas over SVG renderer for frequency chart due to 60-data-point 1 Hz rolling window performance. Selected k3s over kubeadm for simpler bare-metal control room operation. supersedes_entry_id pattern chosen over edit-with-audit-trail to maintain full NERC compliance with a simple PostgreSQL trigger rather than complex application-layer audit diffing.

metadata.notes: ISO/RTO control room dashboard revision adding immutable situation log trigger and end-to-end latency budget decomposition.
